this is in Mathlib
Definition 7.7\(k\)-positive map

A linear map \(E : M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\to M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) is \(k\)-positive if \(E\otimes \mathbb {1}_k\) is positive on \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\otimes M_{k}(\mathbb {C})\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 7.8Two-positive map

A linear map is two-positive if it is \(2\)-positive.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 7.9Completely positive maps are \(k\)-positive

Every completely positive map is \(k\)-positive for all \(k\ge 1\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 7.10\((k\! +\! 1)\)-positive implies \(k\)-positive

Positivity under amplification is monotone in \(k\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 7.11Completely positive implies two-positive

Every completely positive map is two-positive.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 7.12Two-positive implies positive

Every two-positive map is positive.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 7.13Unital linear map

A linear map \(E\) is unital if \(E(\mathbb {1})=\mathbb {1}\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 7.14Kadison–Schwarz for unital two-positive maps

If \(E\) is unital and two-positive, then

\[ E(X^\dagger X) \ge E(X)^\dagger E(X) \]

for all \(X\in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 7.15Kraus-form Kadison–Schwarz as a special case

The Kraus-map Kadison–Schwarz inequality is recovered from the two-positive formulation.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 7.16Range inclusion implies right factorization

If \(\operatorname {ran}(A)\subseteq \operatorname {ran}(B)\), then there exists \(C\) such that \(A=BC\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 7.17Vectorwise range criterion implies factorization

If \(Av\in \operatorname {ran}(B)\) for every vector \(v\), then there exists \(C\) with \(A=BC\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 9.66Corner preservation

For an orthogonal projection \(P\) and linear map \(E\), the corner \(PM_{D}(\mathbb {C})P\) is preserved if \(E(PXP)=PE(PXP)P\) for all \(X\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 9.67Corner subspace

The corner subspace associated with \(P\) is \(\{ PXP : X\in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\} \).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 9.68Restricted corner map

The restriction of \(E\) to an invariant corner \(PM_{D}(\mathbb {C})P\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 9.69Irreducible restriction on a corner

Irreducibility of the corner-restricted map.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.70Peripheral unitary eigenvector

For an irreducible unital Schwarz map with faithful adjoint fixed point, each peripheral eigenvalue admits a unitary eigenvector.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.71Powers on a peripheral-unitary orbit

If \(E(U)=\mu U\) with \(|\mu |=1\) and \(U\) unitary, then \(E(U^n)=\mu ^n U^n\) for all \(n\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.72Normalized peripheral unitary

Peripheral unitary eigenvectors can be normalized compatibly with the faithful fixed-point state.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.73Cyclic projections from a peripheral unitary

A primitive \(m\)-th peripheral root yields \(m\) orthogonal projections summing to \(\mathbb {1}\) and cyclically permuted by the channel.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.74Cyclic decomposition for irreducible Schwarz maps

Under irreducibility and faithful fixed point hypotheses, there is a full cyclic projection decomposition realizing the peripheral period.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.75Power maps preserve each cyclic sector

Appropriate powers of the channel preserve each sector corner.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.76Irreducibility of restricted sector dynamics

Each sector restriction is irreducible.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.77Primitivity of restricted sector dynamics

Each sector restriction is primitive.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.78Corner invariance at the period

The channel raised to the period preserves every cyclic corner.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.79Peripheral product closure

The product of two peripheral eigenvalues is peripheral.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.80Peripheral inverse closure

The inverse of a peripheral eigenvalue is peripheral.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.81Peripheral cyclic group with period divisibility

The peripheral eigenvalues form a finite cyclic group whose order divides the dimension.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.49Semigroup power identity

For \(t\ge 0\) and \(n\in \mathbb {N}\), one has \(T_{nt} = (T_t)^n\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.50Eigenvector propagation under powers

If \(E(v)=\mu v\), then \(E^n(v)=\mu ^n v\) for all \(n\in \mathbb {N}\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 15.51Density matrices are nonzero

Every density matrix is nonzero.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.52Compression invariance is stable under powers

If a corner compression is invariant under \(E\), it remains invariant under every power \(E^n\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.53Generator eigenvectors along the exponential semigroup

If \(L(X)=\mu X\), then \(e^{tL}(X)=e^{t\mu }X\) for all \(t\in \mathbb {R}\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.54Spectral mapping for semigroup generators

If \(\mu \) is in the spectrum of \(L\), then \(e^{t\mu }\) is in the spectrum of \(e^{tL}\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.55Root-of-unity rigidity for phases

If \(e^{it\theta }\) is a root of unity for every \(t{\gt}0\), then \(\theta =0\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.56Peripheral generator eigenvalues are purely imaginary

Peripheral spectral points of the generator have vanishing real part.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.57Peripheral cardinality bound

The number of peripheral eigenvalues is bounded by \(\dim (M_{D}(\mathbb {C}))\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.58Bounded order under peripheral power-closure

If all powers of a peripheral eigenvalue remain peripheral, then some positive power not exceeding \(\dim (M_{D}(\mathbb {C}))\) equals \(1\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.59Peripheral powers stay peripheral under irreducibility

For an irreducible channel with faithful fixed point, every power of a peripheral eigenvalue is again peripheral.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.60Continuous-linear-map power evaluation

Evaluating powers of a linear map agrees with powers in its continuous linear realization.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.61Spectral-radius criterion from eigenvalue bounds

If all eigenvalues satisfy \(|\lambda |{\lt}1\), then the spectral radius is \({\lt}1\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.64Reduced-time eigenvector has nonzero trace

Under the reduced-time hypotheses \(T_{t_0} = (T_u)^{(\dim M_{D}(\mathbb {C}))!}\), if \(\mu \) is a peripheral eigenvalue of \(T_u\), then \(T_u\) admits a \(\mu \)-eigenvector with nonzero trace.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.65Peripheral collapse at the reduced time step

Under the same hypotheses, every peripheral eigenvalue of \(T_u\) equals \(1\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.66Primitivity at the reduced time step

Under the same hypotheses, \(T_u\) is primitive.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.67Fixed points of the primitive reduced time step are trace-scalars

If \(T_u\) is primitive and \(\sigma \) is the common fixed density, then every fixed point of any positive-time slice \(T_s\) has the form \(\tau = \operatorname{tr}(\tau )\, \sigma \).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.68Existence of a primitive reduced time step

If \(T_{t_0}\) is irreducible for some \(t_0{\gt}0\), then there exists a reduced time step \(u \in (0,t_0]\) such that \(T_u\) is primitive.

LaTeX Lean
Corollary 11.48Compression preserves positive-length MPV coefficients

Under the hypotheses of Theorem 11.47, the compressed tensor \(C\) additionally satisfies \(V^{(N)}(C)_\sigma = V^{(N)}(A)_\sigma \) for all positive chain lengths \(N {\gt} 0\).

LaTeX Lean
Corollary 16.19TI case

Let \(A\) and \(B\) be injective MPS tensors of bond dimension \(D\). Suppose the two translation-invariant chains \((A, A, \ldots , A)\) and \((B, B, \ldots , B)\) of length \(n \ge 3\) generate the same chain state. Then there exists an invertible \(Z \in \mathrm{GL}_D(\mathbb {C})\), unique up to a scalar, and a scalar \(\lambda \in \mathbb {C}^{\times }\) with \(\lambda ^n = 1\), such that

\[ B^i = \lambda \, Z^{-1}\, A^i\, Z \]

for every physical index \(i\).

LaTeX Lean
Corollary 16.20Non-TI realisation of a TI state

Let \(\mathbf{A} = (A_0, \ldots , A_{n-1})\) be an injective non-TI chain of length \(n \ge 3\). Suppose the state generated by \(\mathbf{A}\) is translation-invariant, i.e. it equals the state generated by the cyclically shifted chain \((A_1, \ldots , A_{n-1}, A_0)\). Then \(\mathbf{A}\) admits a translation-invariant description with the same bond dimension: there exist an injective tensor \(B\) such that the TI chain \((B, \ldots , B)\) generates the same state as \(\mathbf{A}\). Explicitly, \(B^i = A_0^i\, Z_0^{-1}\) (right multiplication, not conjugation), where \(Z_0\) is defined below.

LaTeX
Corollary 16.21Normal MPS Fundamental Theorem

Let \(\{ A_k\} _{k=0}^{n-1}\) and \(\{ B_k\} _{k=0}^{n-1}\) be two non-TI periodic MPS on \(n\) sites. Suppose that blocking any \(L\) consecutive sites in either chain yields an injective tensor, and that \(n \ge 3L\). If both chains generate the same state, then there exist invertible matrices \(Z_0, \ldots , Z_{n-1} \in \mathrm{GL}_D(\mathbb {C})\), unique up to a common scalar, such that for every site \(k\) and physical index \(i\),

\[ B_k^i = Z_k^{-1}\, A_k^i\, Z_{k+1}, \]

with \(Z_n = Z_0\). That is, gauge equivalence holds for the original (unblocked) tensors. The reduction to the injective case proceeds by grouping the chain into consecutive length-\(L\) blocks and then applying the injective-chain theorem to the blocked local tensors. The remaining passage from blocked gauges back to the original site tensors is the reason this corollary remains marked .

LaTeX Lean
Corollary 15.13Dyson series summability

The Dyson–Phillips series \(\sum _n \widetilde{T}^{(n)}_t\) converges in operator norm for every \(t \ge 0\).

LaTeX Lean
Corollary 17.10Periodic symmetry yields \(\mathbb {Z}\)-gauge equivalence

Let \(A\) be in irreducible form II and let \(u\) be a matrix such that \(B := A_u\) is also in irreducible form II and \(\mathcal{V}(A) = \mathcal{V}(B)\). Then there exists \(m {\gt} 0\) such that \(A\) and \(B\) are \(\mathbb {Z}_m\)-gauge equivalent.

LaTeX Lean
Corollary 17.1Physical symmetry implies virtual gauge

Let \(A\) be an injective MPS tensor and let \(U \in \mathrm{GL}_d(\mathbb {C})\) be a unitary acting on the physical index. Fix \(n \ge 3\) and suppose the \(n\)-site TI state satisfies

\[ U^{\otimes n}\, |\psi _n(A)\rangle = |\psi _n(A)\rangle . \]

Then there exist \(V \in \mathrm{GL}_D(\mathbb {C})\) and a scalar \(\lambda \in \mathbb {C}^{\times }\) with \(\lambda ^n = 1\) such that

\[ \sum _{j=0}^{d-1} U_{ij}\, A^j = \lambda \, V^{-1}\, A^i\, V \]

for every physical index \(i\).

LaTeX
Corollary 15.17Tsum form of the Dyson series

\(\sum _{n=0}^{\infty } \widetilde{T}^{(n)}_t = e^{tL'}\) (tsum form).

LaTeX Lean
Corollary 17.222-cocycle identity for the factor system

If \(D {\gt} 0\), the factor system \(\omega \) from Theorem 17.21 satisfies the 2-cocycle identity:

\[ \omega (g, h)\, \omega (gh, k) = \omega (g, hk)\, \omega (h, k) \qquad \text{for all } g, h, k \in G. \]
LaTeX Lean
Definition 5.72Adjoint fixed points

The adjoint fixed-point set \(\mathrm{Fix}(E^*) = \{ X \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C}) : E^*(X) = X\} \), where \(E^*(X) = \sum _i K_i^\dagger X K_i\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 20.6AKLT tensor

Set \(d = 3\) (spin-1) and \(D = 2\). The AKLT tensor is the family \(\{ A^0, A^1, A^2\} \subset M_{2}(\mathbb {C})\) defined via the Pauli matrices by

\[ A^0 = \frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}\, \sigma _z, \qquad A^1 = \frac{\sqrt{2}}{\sqrt{3}}\, |0\rangle \! \langle 1|, \qquad A^2 = -\frac{\sqrt{2}}{\sqrt{3}}\, |1\rangle \! \langle 0|, \]

where \(\sigma _z = |0\rangle \! \langle 0| - |1\rangle \! \langle 1|\), so equivalently \(A^1 = \frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}\, \sigma _+\) and \(A^2 = -\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}\, \sigma _-\) for \(\sigma _+ = \sqrt{2}\, |0\rangle \! \langle 1|\) and \(\sigma _- = \sqrt{2}\, |1\rangle \! \langle 0|\). Equivalently, the matrices are the Clebsch–Gordan coefficients coupling spin-\(1\) and spin-\(\tfrac 12\) to spin-\(\tfrac 12\).

LaTeX
Definition 11.18Algebra span

For an MPS tensor \(A\), let \(\operatorname{alg}(A)\) be the unital \(\mathbb {C}\)-subalgebra of \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) generated by the matrices \(\{ A^i\} \):

\[ \operatorname{alg}(A) = \mathbb {C}\langle A^i : i = 0,\ldots ,d{-}1\rangle \subseteq M_{D}(\mathbb {C}). \]
LaTeX Lean
Definition 16.44Apply gauge

The PEPS tensor obtained by applying edge-gauge matrices to \(A\), with vertex tensors \(A^X_v\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 2.27Block-diagonal tensor from blocks

Given \(r\) blocks with bond dimensions \((D_k)_{k=1}^r\), per-block tensors \(\{ A_k^i\} \), and scaling factors \((\mu _k)_{k=1}^r\), the associated block-diagonal tensor is the tensor of total bond dimension \(D = \sum _{k=1}^r D_k\) defined by

\[ A^i \; =\; \bigoplus _{k=1}^{r} \mu _k \, A_k^i. \]
LaTeX Lean
Definition 2.29Blocked tensor

Given a tensor \(A\) and a blocking length \(L\), the \(L\)-blocked tensor is the tensor with physical index set \(\{ 0,\ldots ,d{-}1\} ^L\) (hence physical dimension \(d^L\)) and the same bond dimension \(D\), whose matrices are indexed by words \((i_1, \ldots , i_L) \in \{ 0,\ldots ,d{-}1\} ^L\):

\[ (A^{[L]})^{(i_1, \ldots , i_L)} \; =\; A^{i_1} A^{i_2} \cdots A^{i_L}. \]
LaTeX Lean
Definition 13.1Basis of Normal Tensors (BNT)

A basis of normal tensors (BNT) for a total tensor \(A_{\mathrm{tot}}\) consists of \(g\) block tensors \((A_1, \ldots , A_g)\) with bond dimensions \((D_1, \ldots , D_g)\) such that:

  1. each \(A_j\) is normal (Definition 2.23),

  2. at each system size \(N\), the MPV of \(A_{\mathrm{tot}}\) lies in the span of the block MPVs \(\{ |V^{(N)}(A_j)\rangle \} _{j=1}^g\),

  3. for sufficiently large \(N\), the block MPV vectors \(\{ |V^{(N)}(A_j)\rangle \} _{j=1}^g\) are linearly independent.

This is ( .

LaTeX Lean
Definition 2.25Block-injective canonical form

Here, a block-injective canonical form for a tensor consists of:

  1. a number of blocks \(r \ge 1\),

  2. bond dimensions \(D_1, \ldots , D_r\),

  3. injective tensors \(\{ A_k^i\} _{i=0}^{d-1}\) with \(A_k^i \in M_{D_k}(\mathbb {C})\) for each block \(k \in \{ 1,\ldots ,r\} \),

  4. scaling factors \(\mu _1, \ldots , \mu _r \in \mathbb {C}\).

The associated tensor is the block-diagonal tensor

\[ A^i \; =\; \bigoplus _{k=1}^{r} \mu _k \, A_k^i. \]

This is the block-injective version of the canonical-form data; see  and  .

LaTeX Lean
Definition 5.16Cesàro mean

The Cesàro mean of a linear map \(E\) at order \(N\), applied to a starting matrix \(\rho _0\), is

\[ \sigma _N = \frac{1}{N} \sum _{n=0}^{N-1} E^n(\rho _0). \]
LaTeX Lean
Definition 13.2Canonical form with BNT separation

A family is in canonical form with BNT separation if it satisfies Definition 12.9 and, in addition:

  1. the moduli are strictly decreasing: \(|\mu _1| {\gt} |\mu _2| {\gt} \cdots {\gt} |\mu _r|\) (strengthened from the non-increasing ordering of Definition 12.9),

  2. distinct blocks of the same bond dimension are not gauge-phase equivalent.

The strictly decreasing condition is justified by the BNT grouping step, which merges gauge-phase-equivalent blocks with equal moduli.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 18.30Periodic chain ground space

For \(N {\gt} 0\) and \(L \le N\), define the periodic chain ground space

\[ \mathcal{G}_{N,L}(A) := \bigl\{ \psi \in (\{ 0,\ldots ,d{-}1\} ^N \to \mathbb {C}) : \text{for every cyclic window } [i,i{+}L{-}1], \psi |_{[i,i+L-1]} \in G_L(A)\bigr\} , \]

where the condition \(\psi |_{[i,i+L-1]} \in G_L(A)\) means that for every choice of physical indices outside the window, the restriction of \(\psi \) to that window lies in \(G_L(A)\). When \(N = 0\) or \(L {\gt} N\), set \(\mathcal{G}_{N,L}(A) := \top \) by convention.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 5.6Quantum channel

A linear map \(E : M_{D}(\mathbb {C}) \to M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) is a quantum channel if it is both completely positive and trace-preserving (CPTP), following  .

LaTeX Lean
Definition 5.65Channel determinant

For a linear map \(T : M_{D}(\mathbb {C}) \to M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\), the channel determinant \(\det T\) is the determinant of the matrix of \(T\) with respect to the standard matrix-unit basis of \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\). This is the quantity studied in ( .

LaTeX Lean
Definition 5.36Channel period

The period of a channel \(E\) is the number of peripheral eigenvalues (counted without multiplicity): \(p(E) := |\mathrm{peripheral}(E)|\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 5.43Choi matrix

The Choi matrix of a linear map \(T : M_{D}(\mathbb {C}) \to M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) is

\[ \tau = (T \otimes \operatorname{id})(\lvert \Omega \rangle \! \langle \Omega \rvert ) \; \in \; M_{D \times D}(\mathbb {C}). \]

Concretely, \(\tau _{(i_1,i_2),(j_1,j_2)} = \frac{1}{D}\, (T(E_{i_2 j_2}))_{i_1 j_1}\) where \(E_{i_2 j_2}\) is the matrix unit. This is the Choi–Jamiol\- kowski convention of ( .

LaTeX Lean
Definition 20.10Cluster-state tensor

Set \(d = D = 2\). The cluster-state tensor is the family \(\{ A^0, A^1\} \subset M_{2}(\mathbb {C})\) defined by

\[ A^0 = |+\rangle \! \langle 0| = \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \begin{pmatrix} 1 & 0 \\ 1 & 0 \end{pmatrix}, \qquad A^1 = |-\rangle \! \langle 1| = \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \begin{pmatrix} 0 & 1 \\ 0 & -1 \end{pmatrix}, \]

where \(|\pm \rangle = \tfrac {1}{\sqrt{2}}(|0\rangle \pm |1\rangle )\).

LaTeX
Definition 17.24Cohomologous cocycles

Two cocycles \(\omega _1,\omega _2\) are cohomologous (written \(\omega _1 \sim \omega _2\)) if there exists \(\varphi \colon G\to \mathbb {C}^{\times }\) with

\[ \omega _1(g,h) = \varphi (g) \varphi (h) \varphi (gh)^{-1} \omega _2(g,h) \qquad \text{for all } g,h\in G. \]
LaTeX Lean
Definition 14.19Common-period blocked family

Given a finite family of tensors \((B_i)\) with designated periods \((p_i)\), block each \(B_i\) to the shared period \(\operatorname {lcm}(p_i)\), so every block lives in the same physical space.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 15.27Commutator form of Lindblad equation

The commutator form of the Lindblad equation writes the generator as

\[ L(\rho ) = i[\rho , H] + \tfrac {1}{2}\sum _{j} \bigl([L_j,\, \rho L_j^\dagger ] + [L_j\rho ,\, L_j^\dagger ]\bigr), \]

where \([A,B] = AB - BA\). This is ( .

LaTeX Lean
Definition 17.40Local physical intertwining

The local physical intertwining relation is \(\sum _j u_{ij}\, A^j = V\, A^i\, V^\dagger \). Locally, the physical action of \(u\) can therefore be pushed to the virtual legs:

\[ \begin{array}{ccccccc}& & i & & & & i \\ & & \vert & & & & \vert \\ u & & \textcolor{red}{\bullet } & & & & \bullet \\ & & \vert & & = & & \\ \bullet & \text{---} & \bullet & & \textcolor{red}{\bullet } & \text{---} & \textcolor{red}{\bullet } \\ & & & & V & & V^\dagger \end{array} \]
LaTeX Lean
Definition 17.41Transfer-map covariance

Transfer-map covariance is the identity that the transfer map commutes with virtual conjugation: \(\mathcal{E}(V X V^\dagger ) = V\, \mathcal{E}(X)\, V^\dagger \). In doubled-layer notation, the virtual operators slide through the local transfer cell:

\[ \begin{array}{ccccccccc} V & & & & & & & & V \\ \textcolor{red}{\bullet } & \text{---} & \bullet & & = & & \bullet & \text{---} & \textcolor{red}{\bullet } \\ & & \vert & & & & \vert & & \\ \textcolor{red}{\bullet } & \text{---} & \bullet & & = & & \bullet & \text{---} & \textcolor{red}{\bullet } \\ V^\dagger & & & & & & & & V^\dagger \end{array} \]
LaTeX Lean
Definition 17.42Commutation in the doubled transfer picture

In the doubled transfer picture, one asks that the doubled transfer matrix commutes with \(V \otimes \bar V\), expressed as \(V\, \mathcal{E}_1(X)\, V^\dagger = \mathcal{E}_1(V X V^\dagger )\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 19.3Connected correlator

The connected two-point function is

\[ C(X,Y;n) := \langle X_0Y_n\rangle - \langle X_0\rangle \langle Y_0\rangle . \]
LaTeX Lean
Definition 15.2Continuous dynamical semigroup

A dynamical semigroup \(T\) is norm-continuous if the map \(t \mapsto T_t\) is continuous in the operator norm topology. In finite dimension this coincides with strong continuity.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 19.7Correlation length

The correlation length associated with \(\lambda _2\) is

\[ \xi := -\frac{1}{\log |\lambda _2|}. \]
LaTeX Lean
Definition 5.4Completely positive map

A linear map \(E : M_{D}(\mathbb {C}) \to M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) is completely positive (CP) if it admits a Kraus representation: there exist operators \(\{ K_i\} _{i=0}^{r-1}\) with \(K_i \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) such that

\[ E(X) = \sum _{i=0}^{r-1} K_i X K_i^\dagger \quad \text{for all } X \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C}). \]

We use the Kraus characterisation because it matches the later Kadison–Schwarz arguments. By Choi’s theorem, this is equivalent to \(E \otimes \operatorname{id}_n\) being positive for all \(n\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 10.2Cumulative span

The cumulative span at level \(n\) is

\[ T_n(A) = S_0(A) + S_1(A) + \cdots + S_n(A). \]
LaTeX Lean
Definition 10.19Cumulative vector span

Given an MPS tensor \(A\) and a vector \(\varphi \in \mathbb {C}^D\), the cumulative vector span is

\[ K_n(A, \varphi ) = \operatorname{span}_{\mathbb {C}}\{ A^w \varphi : |w| \le n\} \subseteq \mathbb {C}^D. \]

This is ( .

LaTeX Lean
Definition 14.30Cyclic sector decomposition

A family of compressed sector tensors \((C_k)_{k=0}^{m-1}\) on corner bond spaces forms a cyclic sector decomposition of the blocked tensor \(A^{(m)}\) if there exist orthogonal projections \(P_0,\dots ,P_{m-1}\) on the ambient bond space such that \(\sum _k P_k = \mathbb {1}\), the projections satisfy the cyclic-shift relation \(E^\dagger (P_{k+1}) = P_k\), each \(P_k\) commutes with every blocked letter \(P_k A^{(m)}_i = A^{(m)}_i P_k\), and \(V^{(N)}(C_k)_\sigma = \operatorname{tr}(P_k (A^{(m)})^\sigma )\) for every word \(\sigma \).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 16.2Decomposition map

Let \(A\) be an injective MPS tensor. The decomposition map \(\Psi _A : M_{D}(\mathbb {C}) \to \mathbb {C}^d\) is a fixed choice of right inverse of \(\Phi _A\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 5.7Density matrices

The set of density matrices in \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) is

\[ \mathcal{D}_D = \{ \rho \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C}) : \rho \ge 0, \operatorname{tr}(\rho ) = 1\} . \]
LaTeX Lean
Definition 15.1Dynamical semigroup

A family of linear maps \(T : \mathbb {R}\to (M_{D}(\mathbb {C}) \to _{\ell } M_{D}(\mathbb {C}))\) is a dynamical semigroup if

  1. (semigroup law) \(T_{t+s} = T_t \circ T_s\) for all \(t,s \ge 0\); and

  2. (initial condition) \(T_0 = \operatorname{id}\).

This is ( .

LaTeX Lean
Definition 16.42Edge gauge at a vertex

For an edge \(e = (u,w)\) with \(u {\lt} w\) and gauge \(X_e \in \mathrm{GL}(D_e)\), the gauge matrix applied at vertex \(v\) is \(X_e\) if \(v = u\) and \((X_e^{-1})^\top \) if \(v = w\). Diagrammatically, the ordered edge carries opposite endpoint actions:

\[ \begin{array}{c} \text{edge } e = (u,w) \text{ oriented by } u {\lt} w \\ u \; \text{---} \; X_e \; \text{---} \; (X_e^{-1})^\top \; \text{---} \; w \end{array} \]
LaTeX Lean
Definition 2.2Word evaluation

Given a word \(w = (i_1, \ldots , i_L) \in \{ 0,\ldots ,d{-}1\} ^L\), the word evaluation is the matrix product

\[ A^w \; :=\; A^{i_1} A^{i_2} \cdots A^{i_L} \; \in \; M_{D}(\mathbb {C}), \]

with the convention that the empty word evaluates to the identity: \(A^\varnothing = \mathbb {1}_D\). In tensor-network notation,

\[ \begin{array}{ccccccc} i_1 & & & & & & i_L \\ \vert & & \vert & & \cdots & & \vert \\ \bullet & \text{---} & \bullet & \text{---} & \cdots & \text{---} & \bullet \\ \multicolumn{7}{c}{L} \end{array} \]

in which each black node denotes the same local tensor \(A\), the virtual legs remain open, and the physical legs record the word \((i_1,\ldots ,i_L)\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 15.3Exponential semigroup

Given a generator \(L \in \mathrm{End}_{\mathbb {C}}(M_{D}(\mathbb {C}))\), the exponential semigroup is

\[ T_t := e^{tL} = \sum _{k=0}^{\infty } \frac{(tL)^k}{k!}. \]
LaTeX
Lean
  • expSemigroup
  • expSemigroupCLM
Definition 10.8Fitting decomposition

A Fitting decomposition of a linear endomorphism \(f : V \to V\) on a finite-dimensional vector space over an algebraically closed field consists of:

  1. \(f\) is nilpotent on the generalized \(0\)-eigenspace,

  2. \(f\) is invertible on each generalized \(\mu \)-eigenspace for \(\mu \neq 0\),

  3. the generalized eigenspaces span \(V\),

  4. the generalized eigenspaces are linearly independent.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 5.20Fixed-point projection

Given a linear map \(E : M_{D}(\mathbb {C}) \to M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) and a fixed point \(\rho \) with \(E(\rho ) = \rho \) and \(\operatorname{tr}(\rho ) \neq 0\), the fixed-point projection is the rank-one map

\[ P(X) = \frac{\operatorname{tr}(X)}{\operatorname{tr}(\rho )} \rho . \]

This projects onto the span of \(\rho \) along the kernel of the trace functional. When the fixed point is unique up to scaling, this span is the full fixed-point space.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 9.10Frobenius norm squared

For a (possibly rectangular) matrix \(X \in M_{m \times n}(\mathbb {C})\), the Frobenius norm squared is

\[ \| X\| _F^2 := \sum _{i=0}^{m-1}\sum _{j=0}^{n-1} |X_{ij}|^2. \]
LaTeX Lean
Definition 7.27Full multiplicative domain

The multiplicative domain of \(E\) is

\[ \mathcal{A}(E) := \mathcal{A}_R(E) \cap \mathcal{A}_L(E). \]
LaTeX Lean
Definition 2.10Gauge equivalence

Two tensors \(A\) and \(B\) of the same bond dimension \(D\) are gauge equivalent if there exists an invertible matrix \(X \in \mathrm{GL}_D(\mathbb {C})\) such that

\[ B^i \; =\; X \, A^i \, X^{-1} \qquad \text{for all } i \in \{ 0,\ldots ,d{-}1\} . \]

In tensor-network notation,

\[ \begin{array}{ccccc}& & i & & \\ & & \vert & & \\ \textcolor{red}{\bullet } & \text{---} & \bullet & \text{---} & \textcolor{red}{\bullet } \\ X & & & & X^{-1} \end{array} \]

the black node denotes the tensor named in the surrounding formula, the upper leg is the physical index \(i\), and the two red side nodes denote the gauge matrices acting on the virtual legs.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 2.16Gauge-phase equivalence

Two tensors \(A\) and \(B\) are gauge-phase equivalent if there exist \(X \in \mathrm{GL}_D(\mathbb {C})\) and \(\zeta \in \mathbb {C}\setminus \{ 0\} \) such that

\[ B^i \; =\; \zeta \, X \, A^i \, X^{-1} \qquad \text{for all } i. \]
LaTeX Lean
Definition 16.45Gauge equivalence for PEPS

Two PEPS tensors \(A, B\) are gauge-equivalent if they have the same bond dimensions and \(B\) is obtained from \(A\) by applying invertible gauge matrices on each edge.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 16.43Gauge vertex action

The gauged tensor at vertex \(v\) is \(A^X_v\), where \(A^X_v(\eta , \sigma ) = \sum _{\eta '} (\prod _{ie} M_{ie}(\eta (ie), \eta '(ie))) A_v(\eta ', \sigma )\). In tensor-network notation, one inserts the appropriate gauge matrix on each incident virtual leg of the local tensor:

\[ \begin{array}{c} \text{one gauge matrix } M_{ve} \text{ is inserted on each incident virtual leg of } A_v \\ A_v \mapsto A_v^X \end{array} \]
LaTeX Lean
Definition 15.18Generator decomposition

A generator decomposition consists of a completely positive map \(\phi : M_{d}(\mathbb {C}) \to M_{d}(\mathbb {C})\) and a matrix \(\kappa \in M_{d}(\mathbb {C})\), defining the linear map

\[ L(\rho ) = \phi (\rho ) - \kappa \rho - \rho \kappa ^\dagger . \]

This is ( .

LaTeX Lean
Definition 20.1GHZ tensor

Set \(d = D = 2\). The GHZ tensor is the family \(\{ A^0, A^1\} \subset M_{2}(\mathbb {C})\) defined by

\[ A^0 = |0\rangle \! \langle 0| = \operatorname{diag}(1,0), \qquad A^1 = |1\rangle \! \langle 1| = \operatorname{diag}(0,1). \]

For a system of \(N\) sites the resulting MPV is the GHZ state

\[ |\mathrm{GHZ}_N\rangle = |0\rangle ^{\otimes N} + |1\rangle ^{\otimes N}. \]
LaTeX Lean
Definition 18.1Ground-space map

The map \(\Gamma _L\) above is a \(\mathbb {C}\)-linear map

\[ \Gamma _L : M_{D}(\mathbb {C}) \to (\{ 0,\ldots ,d{-}1\} ^L \to \mathbb {C}). \]

In tensor-network notation,

\[ \begin{array}{ccccccc} \sigma _1 & & & & & & \sigma _L \\ \vert & & \vert & & \cdots & & \vert \\ \bullet & \text{---} & \bullet & \text{---} & \cdots & \text{---} & \bullet \\ \vert & & & & & & \vert \\ \multicolumn{7}{c}{\textcolor{red}{\bullet }} \\ \multicolumn{7}{c}{X} \end{array} \]

the upper chain denotes the word tensor \(A^\sigma \) with open physical indices \(\sigma _1,\ldots ,\sigma _L\), and the lower red node denotes the boundary matrix \(X\) inserted on the closing virtual bond before taking the trace. The chain length \(L\) is fixed by the surrounding formula, not by an extra label inside the diagram.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 18.2Local ground space

The local ground space is the image

\[ G_L(A) := \mathrm{im}(\Gamma _L) \subseteq (\{ 0,\ldots ,d{-}1\} ^L \to \mathbb {C}). \]
LaTeX Lean
Definition 17.29Second cohomology quotient

The second cohomology set is the quotient

\[ H^2(G,\mathbb {C}^{\times }) := Z^2(G,\mathbb {C}^{\times })/{\sim }, \]

where \(\sim \) is the coboundary-equivalence relation.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 15.76Block-upper-triangular Lindblad form

Condition (4): there exists a nontrivial projector \(P\) and a Lindblad form \((H, \{ L_j\} )\) for \(L\) such that \((1-P)L_j P = 0\) and \((1-P)\kappa P = 0\) for all \(j\), where \(\kappa = iH + \frac{1}{2}\sum _j L_j^\dagger L_j\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 18.42Commuting parent Hamiltonian structure

A subspace \(K\) (given by its idempotent endomorphism \(P_K\)) has a commuting parent Hamiltonian structure if there exist idempotent endomorphisms \(P_{AX}\) and \(P_{XB}\) that commute and satisfy \(P_{AX} \circ P_{XB} = P_K\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 11.12Invariant projection predicate

An MPS tensor \(A\) has an invariant projection if there exists an orthogonal projection \(P\) with \(P \neq 0\), \(P \neq \mathbb {1}\), and \((\mathbb {1}- P) A^i P = 0\) for all \(i\). A tensor is irreducible if it has no nontrivial invariant projection (Definition 11.15).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 15.75Invariant compression

Condition (3): there exists a nontrivial orthogonal projector \(P\) such that \(e^{tL}(P M_{D}(\mathbb {C}) P) \subseteq P M_{D}(\mathbb {C}) P\) for all \(t \ge 0\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 15.84Trivial Lindblad commutant

The commutant of the Lindblad family \(\{ L_j\} '\) equals \(\mathbb {C}\cdot \mathbb {1}\), i.e. the Lindblad operators act irreducibly on \(M_d(\mathbb {C})\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 15.73Rank-deficient fixed density

Condition (1): there exists a density matrix \(\rho _0\) with nontrivial kernel such that \(e^{tL}(\rho _0) = \rho _0\) for all \(t \ge 0\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 15.74Rank-deficient kernel element

Condition (2): there exists a density matrix \(\rho _0\) with nontrivial kernel satisfying \(L(\rho _0) = 0\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 8.25Wolf spectral properties

A completely positive map \(E\) on \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) has the spectral properties of ( if there exist a positive real number \(r\), a positive definite right eigenvector \(\rho \), and a positive definite left eigenvector \(\sigma \) for the adjoint map such that \(E(\rho )=r\rho \), \(E^\dagger (\sigma )=r\sigma \), every positive semidefinite right eigenvector for eigenvalue \(r\) is a scalar multiple of \(\rho \), and the spectral radius of \(E\) is equal to \(r\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 17.39String order

String order exists for \(u\) with respect to a boundary state \(\Lambda \) if there exist boundary matrices \(X, Y\) and a positive constant \(c {\gt} 0\) such that \(c \le \| R_L(u; X, Y)\| \) for all \(L\), where \(R_L(u; X, Y)\) is the boundary-refined string-order parameter. Informally, this means the scalar string-order parameter \(R_L(u)\) does not decay to zero.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 15.36Traceless Kraus operators

A Lindblad form has traceless Kraus operators if \(\operatorname{tr}(L_j) = 0\) for every \(j\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 18.28Unique ground state

A submodule \(S\) has a unique ground state if \(\dim S = 1\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 3.24Horizontal canonical form for an MPO

An MPO tensor is in horizontal canonical form if its doubled-index MPS tensor generates the same MPV family as a block-diagonal tensor \(\bigoplus _k \mu _k A_k\) whose blocks are injective, left-canonical, and have nonzero weights \(\mu _k\).

LaTeX
Definition 18.17Left ground membership

A state \(\psi \) on \(L+1\) sites satisfies the left ground condition if \(\mathrm{restrictLast} \psi j \in G_L(A)\) for every \(j\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 18.18Right ground membership

A state \(\psi \) on \(L+1\) sites satisfies the right ground condition if \(\mathrm{restrictFirst} \psi i \in G_L(A)\) for every \(i\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 2.21Injectivity

A tensor \(A\) is injective if its matrices span the full matrix algebra: \(\operatorname{span}_{\mathbb {C}}\{ A^i : i = 0,\ldots ,d{-}1\} = M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 11.19Invariant submodule

A subspace \(W \le \mathbb {C}^D\) is \(A\)-invariant if \(A^i W \subseteq W\) for every \(i\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 11.20Irreducible action

The Kraus operators of \(A\) act irreducibly on \(\mathbb {C}^D\) if every \(A\)-invariant subspace is trivial, i.e. equal to \(0\) or to \(\mathbb {C}^D\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 5.12Irreducible map

A linear map \(E : M_{D}(\mathbb {C}) \to M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) is irreducible if whenever \(P\) is an orthogonal projection satisfying \(E(P M_{D}(\mathbb {C}) P) \subseteq P M_{D}(\mathbb {C}) P\), then \(P = 0\) or \(P = \mathbb {1}\). This is ( . The definition applies to any linear map; complete positivity is not required.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 14.16Irreducible form

A tensor \(A\) is in irreducible form if it is block-diagonal

\[ A^i = \bigoplus _{j \in J} \mu _j\, A_j^i, \]

where each block \(A_j\) is an \(m_j\)-periodic tensor, each weight \(\mu _j \neq 0\), and the blocks are pairwise non-repeated (no two distinct \(j, j'\) have \(A_j\) gauge-equivalent to \(A_{j'}\)). This is the standard form introduced in ( . It extends the normal canonical form (Definition 11.31), which requires the stronger condition that every block is \(1\)-periodic.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 11.15Irreducible tensor

An MPS tensor \(A\) is irreducible if it has no nontrivial invariant projection.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 12.9Canonical form conditions

A collection of scaling factors \((\mu _k)_{k=1}^r\) and block tensors \((A_k)_{k=1}^r\) satisfies the canonical form conditions if:

  1. each \(A_k\) is injective,

  2. each \(A_k\) satisfies the TP normalization \(\sum _i (A_k^i)^\dagger A_k^i = \mathbb {1}\),

  3. the moduli are non-increasing: \(|\mu _1| \ge |\mu _2| \ge \cdots \ge |\mu _r|\),

  4. all \(\mu _k \neq 0\),

  5. the self-overlap converges: \(O_{A_k A_k}(N) \to 1\) for each \(k\).

Only this one-sided normalization is assumed; no unital condition is assumed here. Condition (5) is a primitivity hypothesis: for a primitive channel ( ( ), \(T^n(\rho )\to \rho _\infty \) for every initial state, which forces the self-overlap to converge to \(1\). See also ( for the completely-positive characterisation and Chapter 9 for the spectral-gap proof.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 15.19Conditional complete positivity

A linear map \(L : M_{d}(\mathbb {C}) \to M_{d}(\mathbb {C})\) is conditionally completely positive (CCP) if it admits a generator decomposition, i.e. there exist a CP map \(\phi \) and a matrix \(\kappa \) such that \(L(\rho ) = \phi (\rho ) - \kappa \rho - \rho \kappa ^\dagger \). This is condition 1 of ( .

LaTeX Lean
Definition 19.23Correlations independent of distance

For a positive definite right fixed point \(\rho ^R {\gt} 0\), an MPS tensor has correlations independent of distance (CID) when for all local observables \(X\) and \(Y\) and all separations \(n,m \ge 1\),

\[ C(X,Y;n) = C(X,Y;m). \]
LaTeX Lean
Definition 17.23Coboundary

A scalar 2-cocycle \(\omega \) is a coboundary if there exists \(\varphi \colon G \to \mathbb {C}^{\times }\) such that

\[ \omega (g,h) = \varphi (g) \varphi (h) \varphi (gh)^{-1} \qquad \text{for all } g,h\in G. \]
LaTeX Lean
Definition 17.28Multiplicative 2-cocycle condition

A scalar 2-cochain \(\omega \colon G\times G\to \mathbb {C}^{\times }\) is a 2-cocycle if it satisfies

\[ \omega (g,h) \omega (gh,k) = \omega (g,hk) \omega (h,k) \qquad \text{for all } g,h,k\in G. \]
LaTeX Lean
Definition 18.37Commuting parent Hamiltonian

The parent Hamiltonian with block length \(L\) on \(N\) sites has commuting local terms if \(h_i h_j = h_j h_i\) for all \(i,j \in \{ 0,\ldots ,N{-}1\} \). See Ref.  ( .

LaTeX Lean
Definition 9.34Conditional expectation

Given a \(*\)-subalgebra \(S \subseteq M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\), a linear map \(P : M_{D}(\mathbb {C}) \to M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) is a conditional expectation onto \(S\) if it is idempotent, unital, has range contained in \(S\), and satisfies \(P(X)=X\) for all \(X \in S\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 18.41Decorrelation

Given an idempotent endomorphism \(P_K\) and sets of observables \(\mathcal{O}_A\), \(\mathcal{O}_B\), regions \(A\) and \(B\) are decorrelated w.r.t. \(K\) when \(P_K \circ O_A \circ (1 - P_K) \circ O_B \circ P_K = 0\) for all \(O_A \in \mathcal{O}_A\), \(O_B \in \mathcal{O}_B\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 18.10Frustration-free condition

A state \(\psi \) is frustration-free if each local term annihilates it:

\[ \forall \, i \in \{ 0,\ldots ,N{-}1\} ,\quad h_i \psi = 0. \]
LaTeX Lean
Definition 15.42GKSL generator

A linear map \(L : M_{d}(\mathbb {C}) \to M_{d}(\mathbb {C})\) is a GKSL generator if \(e^{tL}\) is a quantum channel (CPTP) for all \(t \ge 0\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 15.83Hermitian-closed Lindblad span

The span of the Lindblad operators is closed under Hermitian conjugation, i.e. it forms a \(*\)-subspace of \(M_d(\mathbb {C})\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 19.15Local orthogonality

A single BNT block is locally orthogonal when its self-transfer map is idempotent. For a single tensor this coincides with the RFP condition (Definition 19.13); in the full multi-block BNT setting, local orthogonality additionally requires that mixed transfer operators vanish. See ( .

LaTeX Lean
Definition 18.38Nearest-neighbor commuting parent Hamiltonian

A nearest-neighbor commuting parent Hamiltonian (NNCPH) is a commuting parent Hamiltonian with block length \(L = 2\). See Ref.  ( .

LaTeX Lean
Definition 11.31Normal canonical form conditions

A collection of scaling factors \((\mu _k)_{k=1}^r\) and block tensors \((A_k)_{k=1}^r\) satisfies the normal canonical form conditions if:

  1. each \(A_k\) is irreducible,

  2. each \(A_k\) satisfies the TP normalization \(\sum _i (A_k^i)^\dagger A_k^i = \mathbb {1}\),

  3. each transfer map \(\mathcal{E}_{A_k}\) is primitive (equivalently, has peripheral spectrum \(\{ 1\} \)),

  4. the moduli are non-increasing: \(|\mu _1| \ge |\mu _2| \ge \cdots \ge |\mu _r|\),

  5. all \(\mu _k \neq 0\),

  6. all bond dimensions are positive.

Here “normal” is used in the spectral sense of : irreducible blocks whose TP-normalized transfer maps are primitive. As noted in the remark after Definition 2.23, this is equivalent to the eventual block-injectivity formulation of Definition 2.23; see ( .

LaTeX Lean
Definition 3.15Purification RFP

An MPO tensor \(M\) has a purification RFP if it admits an LPDO witness whose purifying MPS tensor is a pure-state renormalization fixed point. This is ( .

LaTeX Lean
Definition 19.13Renormalization fixed point

An MPS tensor \(A\) is a renormalization fixed point (RFP) when its transfer map is idempotent, \(\mathcal{E}_A \circ \mathcal{E}_A = \mathcal{E}_A\). See ( .

LaTeX Lean
Definition 3.17MPDO renormalization fixed point

An MPO tensor \(M\) is an MPDO renormalization fixed point if its transfer map is idempotent:

\[ \mathcal{E}_M \circ \mathcal{E}_M = \mathcal{E}_M. \]
LaTeX Lean
Definition 17.51Same SPT phase

Injective tensors \(A\) and \(B\), both symmetric under an on-site representation \(U\colon G\to \mathrm{GL}_d(\mathbb {C})\), are in the same SPT phase if there exist virtual cocycles \(\omega _A,\omega _B\) with corresponding projective representations that intertwine the respective twisted tensors and satisfy \(\omega _A\sim \omega _B\) (cohomologous).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 15.20Trace-annihilating map

A linear map \(L : M_{d}(\mathbb {C}) \to M_{d}(\mathbb {C})\) is trace-annihilating if \(\operatorname{tr}(L(\rho )) = 0\) for all \(\rho \in M_{d}(\mathbb {C})\). This is the infinitesimal version of trace preservation.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 15.21Trace constraint

A generator decomposition \((\phi , \kappa )\) satisfies the trace constraint if \(\phi ^*(\mathbb {1}) = \kappa + \kappa ^\dagger \), where \(\phi ^*\) is the Hilbert–Schmidt adjoint. This is the condition in ( .

LaTeX Lean
Definition 19.24Zero correlation length

An MPS tensor has zero correlation length when it is both locally orthogonal and correlations are independent of distance. See ( .

LaTeX Lean
Definition 16.32Canonical form

A family of block tensors \(\{ A_k\} _{k=0}^{r-1}\) with scaling weights \(\mu _0, \ldots , \mu _{r-1} \in \mathbb {C}^{\times }\) is in canonical form if:

  1. each \(A_k\) is injective,

  2. each \(A_k\) is left-canonical: \(\sum _i (A_k^i)^{\dagger }\, A_k^i = \mathbb {1}_{D_k}\),

  3. the weights are strictly ordered by norm: \(|\mu _0| {\gt} |\mu _1| {\gt} \cdots {\gt} |\mu _{r-1}| {\gt} 0\),

  4. the self-overlaps are normalised: \(O_{A_k A_k}(N) \to 1\) as \(N \to \infty \).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 15.47Kossakowski form

A Kossakowski form consists of \(H = H^\dagger \), a finite family of matrices \(\{ F_k\} _{k=0}^{n-1}\), and a positive semidefinite matrix \(C \in M_{n}(\mathbb {C})\), defining

\[ L(\rho ) = i[\rho , H] + \tfrac {1}{2}\sum _{k,l} C_{lk}\bigl( [F_k, \rho F_l^\dagger ] + [F_k\rho , F_l^\dagger ] \bigr). \]

In Wolf’s formula one takes \(\{ F_k\} \) to be a basis of traceless matrices; here we keep only the algebraic data needed for the conversion to Lindblad form.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 15.85Kossakowski rank

The minimal number of Lindblad operators across all GKSL representations of \(L\), which equals the rank of the Kossakowski matrix in the orthonormal-basis representation.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 7.2Adjoint Kraus map

The adjoint Kraus map is \(E^*(X) = \sum _{i=0}^{d-1} K_i^\dagger X K_i\). When the \(\{ K_i\} \) are the matrices of an MPS tensor \(A\), this is the transfer map of the conjugate-transposed family \(i \mapsto (A^i)^\dagger \).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 5.74Kraus commutant

The Kraus commutant is \(\{ K_i, K_i^\dagger \} ' = \{ X \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C}) : [X, K_i] = [X, K_i^\dagger ] = 0 \; \forall i\} \). It is a \(*\)-subalgebra of \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 5.69Kraus fixed points

The fixed-point set of a Kraus map \(E(X) = \sum _i K_i X K_i^\dagger \) is \(\mathrm{Fix}(E) = \{ X \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C}) : E(X) = X\} \).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 7.1Kraus map

Given operators \(\{ K_i\} _{i=0}^{d-1}\) with \(K_i \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\), the Kraus map is \(E(X) = \sum _{i=0}^{d-1} K_i X K_i^\dagger \). A linear map is completely positive (Definition 5.4) if and only if it can be written in this form.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 2.22\(L\)-block injectivity

A tensor \(A\) is \(L\)-block injective if \(\operatorname{span}_{\mathbb {C}}\{ A^{i_1} \cdots A^{i_L} : (i_1,\ldots ,i_L) \in \{ 0,\ldots ,d{-}1\} ^L\} = M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\). An injective tensor is \(1\)-block injective.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 14.18LCM period for a finite block family

Given a finite family of blocking periods \((p_i)\) indexed by a finite set, their least common multiple serves as the shared blocking period.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 11.74Left sector tensor

For a projection \(P\), the left sector tensor is obtained by left multiplication of each Kraus operator by \(P\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 15.23Lindblad form

A Lindblad form consists of a Hermitian matrix \(H = H^\dagger \) (the Hamiltonian) and a family of matrices \(\{ L_j\} _{j=0}^{r-1}\) (the Lindblad operators), defining the linear map

\[ L(\rho ) = i[\rho , H] + \sum _{j} \Bigl( L_j \rho L_j^\dagger - \tfrac {1}{2}\{ L_j^\dagger L_j, \rho \} _+ \Bigr), \]

where \([A,B] = AB - BA\) and \(\{ A,B\} _+ = AB + BA\). This is ( .

LaTeX Lean
Definition 15.82Lindblad span

The \(\mathbb {C}\)-linear span of the Lindblad operators \(\{ L_j\} \).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 17.38Local symmetry

An MPS tensor \(A\) has local symmetry under a unitary \(u\) with respect to a boundary state \(\Lambda \) if there exist a unitary matrix \(V\) and a phase \(\mu \) with \(|\mu | = 1\) such that \(V^\dagger \Lambda V = \Lambda \) and

\[ \sum _j u_{ij}\, A^j = \mu \, V\, A^i\, V^\dagger \quad \text{for all } i. \]

Informally, this means \(\| R_L(u)\| = \| R_L(\mathbb {1})\| \) for all \(L\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 18.8Translated local term

For each site index \(i \in \{ 0,\ldots ,N{-}1\} \), the translated local term \(h_i\) embeds the parent interaction into the full \(N\)-site space at position \(i\) (with periodic boundary conditions). Thus \(h_i\) acts on the cyclic interval \([i,i+L-1]\) and as the identity outside that interval.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 16.48Local tensor evaluation

The local tensor evaluated at vertex \(v\) with virtual-index weighting \(f\), computing \(\sum _\eta (\prod _{ie} f(ie)(\eta (ie))) \cdot A_v(\eta , \sigma )\). This is multilinear in the components of \(f\), not linear in the full tuple.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 3.12LPDO

An MPO tensor is an LPDO if there exist an ancilla dimension \(d_K\), an inner bond dimension \(D'\), an identification of bond indices \(e : \{ 0, \ldots , D - 1\} \simeq \{ 0, \ldots , D' - 1\} \times \{ 0, \ldots , D' - 1\} \) (equivalently, \(D\) and \((D')^2\) have the same cardinality), and matrices \(A^{(i,k)} \in M_{D'}(\mathbb {C})\) such that

\[ M^{ij} = \Bigl(\sum _{k=0}^{d_K-1} A^{(i,k)} \otimes \overline{A^{(j,k)}}\Bigr)_{e,e} \qquad \text{for all } i,j, \]

where \(\otimes \) denotes the Kronecker product, \(\overline{(\cdot )}\) denotes entrywise complex conjugation, and \((\cdot )_{e,e}\) denotes reindexing along \(e\) from \(\{ 0, \ldots , D' - 1\} \times \{ 0, \ldots , D' - 1\} \) back to \(\{ 0, \ldots , D - 1\} \). This is the local purification form of ( .

LaTeX Lean
Definition 9.12Euclidean-space embedding

The map \(\mathrm{vec} : M_{m \times n}(\mathbb {C}) \to \mathbb {C}^{mn}\) that flattens matrix entries is an isometric embedding with respect to the Frobenius norm: \(\| \mathrm{vec}(X)\| ^2 = \| X\| _F^2\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 5.41Maximally entangled state

The maximally entangled state on \(\mathbb {C}^d \otimes \mathbb {C}^d\) is the rank-one projector

\[ \lvert \Omega \rangle \! \langle \Omega \rvert , \quad \lvert \Omega \rangle = \frac{1}{\sqrt{d}} \sum _{j=0}^{d-1} \lvert j,j\rangle . \]

Concretely, \((\lvert \Omega \rangle \! \langle \Omega \rvert )_{(i_1,i_2),(j_1,j_2)} = \frac{1}{d}\, \delta _{i_1 j_1}\delta _{i_2 j_2}\) and \(\operatorname {tr}(\lvert \Omega \rangle \! \langle \Omega \rvert ) = 1\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 9.1Mixed transfer operator

The mixed transfer operator (or cross transfer operator) for two MPS tensors \(A\) and \(B\) of the same bond dimension \(D\) is the linear map \(F_{AB} : M_{D}(\mathbb {C}) \to M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) defined by

\[ F_{AB}(X) = \sum _{i=0}^{d-1} A^i X (B^i)^\dagger . \]
LaTeX Lean
Definition 9.2Rectangular mixed transfer operator

For MPS tensors \(A\) of bond dimension \(D_1\) and \(B\) of bond dimension \(D_2\) (possibly \(D_1 \neq D_2\)), the rectangular mixed transfer operator is \(F_{AB}^{\mathrm{rect}} : M_{D_1 \times D_2}(\mathbb {C}) \to M_{D_1 \times D_2}(\mathbb {C})\) defined by

\[ F_{AB}^{\mathrm{rect}}(X) = \sum _{i=0}^{d-1} A^i X (B^i)^\dagger . \]
LaTeX Lean
Definition 3.11MPDO

An MPO tensor is an MPDO if

\[ \rho ^{(N)}(M) \ge 0 \qquad \text{for every } N \in \mathbb {N}. \]

This is the global positivity condition of ( .

LaTeX Lean
Definition 3.3Word evaluation

For words \(u = (i_1,\ldots ,i_N)\) and \(v = (j_1,\ldots ,j_N)\) in \(\{ 0,\ldots ,d{-}1\} ^N\), the word evaluation is

\[ M^{u,v} := M^{i_1 j_1} M^{i_2 j_2} \cdots M^{i_N j_N} \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C}). \]

The empty pair of words evaluates to \(\mathbb {1}_D\), and word pairs of different lengths evaluate to \(0\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 3.4MPO operator family

The operator generated by \(M\) on \(N\) sites is the matrix \(\rho ^{(N)}(M)\) indexed by configurations \(\sigma ,\tau \in \{ 0,\ldots ,d{-}1\} ^N\) and given by

\[ \rho ^{(N)}(M)_{\sigma ,\tau } = \operatorname{tr}(M^{\sigma ,\tau }). \]
LaTeX Lean
Definition 3.5Hermitian MPO tensor

An MPO tensor is Hermitian if

\[ M^{ij} = (M^{ji})^\dagger \qquad \text{for all } i,j \in \{ 0,\ldots ,d{-}1\} . \]
LaTeX Lean
Definition 3.9Zero correlation length for MPO tensors

An MPO tensor \(M\) has zero correlation length when its transfer map is idempotent:

\[ \mathcal{E}_M \circ \mathcal{E}_M = \mathcal{E}_M. \]

This is the mixed-state analogue of the renormalization fixed-point condition for MPS tensors; see ( .

LaTeX Lean
Definition 3.1MPO tensor

An MPO tensor is a family of matrices

\[ \{ M^{ij}\} _{i,j=0}^{d-1}, \qquad M^{ij} \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C}), \]

indexed by a ket index \(i\) and a bra index \(j\). Diagrammatically,

\[ \begin{array}{c} i\\[-0.3ex] \vert \\[-0.4ex] \text{---}\, \bullet \, \text{---}\\[-0.4ex] \vert \\[-0.3ex] j \end{array} \]

The black node denotes the tensor \(M\), the horizontal legs are virtual, the upper leg is the ket index \(i\), and the lower leg is the bra index \(j\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 3.2Doubled-index MPS view

By combining the pair \((i,j)\) into a single physical index in \(\{ 0,\ldots ,d^2-1\} \), one obtains an MPS tensor with physical dimension \(d^2\) and bond dimension \(D\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 3.6MPO transfer map

The transfer map associated to \(M\) is the linear map \(\mathcal{E}_M : M_{D}(\mathbb {C}) \to M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) defined by

\[ \mathcal{E}_M(X) = \sum _{i,j=0}^{d-1} M^{ij} X (M^{ij})^\dagger . \]
LaTeX Lean
Definition 2.1MPS tensor

A (translation-invariant, PBC) MPS tensor with physical dimension \(d\) and bond dimension \(D\) is a collection of matrices

\[ \{ A^i\} _{i=0}^{d-1}, \qquad A^i \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C}), \]

indexed by a physical index \(i \in \{ 0, \ldots , d{-}1\} \). Such a tensor defines an MPV family. Diagrammatically,

\[ \begin{array}{c} i\\[-0.3ex] \vert \\[-0.4ex] \text{---}\, \bullet \, \text{---} \end{array} \]

The black node denotes the tensor \(A\), the horizontal legs are virtual, and the upper leg is the physical index \(i\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 2.4Matrix product vector

The matrix product vector (MPV) of a tensor \(A\) at system size \(N\) is the vector

\[ |V^{(N)}(A)\rangle = \sum _{i_1, \ldots , i_N = 0}^{d-1} \operatorname{tr}\! (A^{i_1} A^{i_2} \cdots A^{i_N}) |i_1, \ldots , i_N\rangle \in (\mathbb {C}^d)^{\otimes N}. \]

Equivalently, for a configuration \(\sigma = (i_1, \ldots , i_N) \in \{ 0,\ldots ,d{-}1\} ^N\), we write

\[ V^{(N)}(A)_\sigma := \operatorname{tr}\! (A^{i_1} A^{i_2} \cdots A^{i_N}). \]

The coefficient function \(\sigma \mapsto V^{(N)}(A)_\sigma \) gives the components; the displayed ket is the corresponding vector in \((\mathbb {C}^d)^{\otimes N}\). For a general word \(w\), we also write

\[ c_w(A) := \operatorname{tr}(A^w), \]

The MPV family generated by \(A\) is the collection \(\mathcal{V}(A) = \bigl\{ |V^{(N)}(A)\rangle \bigr\} _{N \ge 1}\). The coefficient \(V^{(N)}(A)_\sigma \) is the periodic contraction

\[ \begin{array}{ccccccc} i_1 & & & & & & i_N \\ \vert & & \vert & & \cdots & & \vert \\ \bullet & \text{---} & \bullet & \text{---} & \cdots & \text{---} & \bullet \\ \vert & & & & & & \vert \\ \multicolumn{7}{c}{\text{---}\, \cdots \, \text{---}} \end{array} \]

of \(N\) copies of the local tensor \(A\), with the outer virtual legs closed by the trace.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 2.34MPV inner product

The MPV inner product between two tensors \(A\) and \(B\) at system size \(N\) is

\[ \langle V^{(N)}(A) | V^{(N)}(B) \rangle \; :=\; \sum _{\sigma \in \{ 0,\ldots ,d{-}1\} ^N} \overline{V^{(N)}(A)_\sigma } \; V^{(N)}(B)_\sigma , \]

i.e. the Euclidean inner product on \(\mathbb {C}^{d^N}\) (conjugate-linear in the first argument).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 2.35MPV overlap

The MPV overlap between two tensors \(A\) (of bond dimension \(D_1\)) and \(B\) (of bond dimension \(D_2\)) at system size \(N\) is

\[ O_{AB}(N) \; :=\; \sum _{\sigma \in \{ 0,\ldots ,d{-}1\} ^N} V^{(N)}(A)_\sigma \; \overline{V^{(N)}(B)_\sigma }. \]

This is the bilinear overlap (without complex conjugation) used later. By Lemma 2.36, it is the complex conjugate of the Hilbert-space inner product from Definition 2.34.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 2.33MPV as Hilbert-space vector

We regard \(|V^{(N)}(A)\rangle \) as an element of \(\mathbb {C}^{d^N}\) indexed by configurations \(\sigma \in \{ 0,\ldots ,d{-}1\} ^N\), with \(\sigma \)-component \(V^{(N)}(A)_\sigma \) as in Definition 2.4. This allows us to use the standard Euclidean inner product on \(\mathbb {C}^{d^N}\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 18.26MPV submodule

The subspace spanned by the MPV state \(V^{(N)}(A)(\sigma ) = \operatorname{tr}(A^{\sigma _0}\cdots A^{\sigma _{N-1}})\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 6.12Mutual information

The quantum mutual information of a bipartite state \(\rho _{AB}\) is

\[ I(A{:}B) = S(\rho _A) + S(\rho _B) - S(\rho _{AB}). \]
LaTeX Lean
Definition 4.12Periodic non-TI chain data

Fix a chain length \(n\). A non-translation-invariant periodic MPS chain consists of sitewise tensors

\[ A_k = \{ A_k^i\} _{i=0}^{d-1}, \qquad k = 0, \dots , n-1, \]

where each \(A_k^i \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\). For a basis configuration \(\sigma = (\sigma _0,\dots ,\sigma _{n-1})\), its coefficient is

\[ c_{A,\sigma } := \operatorname{tr}\! \bigl(A_0^{\sigma _0} A_1^{\sigma _1} \cdots A_{n-1}^{\sigma _{n-1}}\bigr). \]

Two such chains generate the same chain state if these coefficients agree for every \(\sigma \). The chain is injective if each local tensor \(A_k\) is injective in the usual single-site sense. Finally, two chains are cyclically gauge equivalent if there are invertible bond matrices \(Z_0,\dots ,Z_{n-1}\) such that

\[ B_k^i = Z_k A_k^i Z_{k+1}^{-1} \]

for every site \(k\) and physical index \(i\), with indices understood modulo \(n\) so that \(Z_n = Z_0\). Locally, this gauge relation is represented by

\[ \begin{array}{ccccc}& & i & & \\ & & \vert & & \\ \textcolor{red}{\bullet } & \text{---} & \bullet & \text{---} & \textcolor{red}{\bullet } \\ Z_k & & & & Z_{k+1}^{-1} \end{array} \]

where the black node denotes the site tensor named in the surrounding formula and the two red side nodes denote the bond gauges on the neighboring virtual legs.

LaTeX
Lean
  • MPSChainTensor
  • MPSChainTensor.coeff
  • MPSChainTensor.SameState
  • MPSChainTensor.IsInjective
  • MPSChainTensor.GaugeEquiv
Definition 11.68Norm-class enumeration in decreasing order

From any finite weight family, construct norm classes ordered by strictly decreasing norm values.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 11.67Norm-class partition

The grouping data consists of: norm classes, one representative value per class, class multiplicities, an enumeration of members in each class, and a regrouping identity recovering the full finite sum.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 2.23Normal tensor

A tensor \(A\) is normal if there exists a finite \(L_0\) such that \(A\) is \(L_0\)-block injective.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 16.33Normal canonical form

A family is in normal canonical form if it satisfies the canonical-form conditions with each block additionally irreducible (no nontrivial invariant projection) and primitive (the transfer map has spectral gap).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 13.3Normal canonical form with BNT separation

A family is in normal canonical form with BNT separation if it satisfies Definition 11.31 and, in addition:

  1. the moduli are strictly decreasing (strengthened from non-increasing),

  2. distinct blocks of the same bond dimension are not gauge-phase equivalent.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 17.12On-site symmetry

A tensor \(A\) is on-site symmetric under a group representation \(U : G \to \mathrm{GL}_d(\mathbb {C})\) if \(\mathcal{V}(A) = \mathcal{V}(\widetilde{A}_g)\) for every \(g \in G\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 17.32Permutation symmetry data

An on-site symmetry datum for a group \(G\) on a physical space of dimension \(d\) consists of a matrix-valued map \(u : G \to M_{d}(\mathbb {C})\) and a physical-index action \(\sigma : G \to \mathrm{End}(\{ 0,\ldots ,d{-}1\} )\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 19.1One-point expectation

Fix an MPS tensor and a right fixed point of its transfer map. Write the tensor as \(A\) and the fixed point as \(\rho ^R\). Under the hypotheses of Theorem 8.9, that fixed point exists and is unique up to scaling; we normalize it so that \(\operatorname{tr}(\rho ^R)=1\). For a local observable \(X \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) define

\[ \langle X_0 \rangle := \operatorname{tr}(X\rho ^R). \]
LaTeX Lean
Definition 7.26Right and left multiplicative domains

For a Kraus map \(E\), define

\[ \mathcal{A}_R(E) := \{ X \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C}) : E(XY) = E(X) E(Y) \text{ for all } Y \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\} \]

and

\[ \mathcal{A}_L(E) := \{ X \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C}) : E(YX) = E(Y) E(X) \text{ for all } Y \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\} . \]

We follow the convention that \(\mathcal{A}_R(E)\) controls right multiplication and \(\mathcal{A}_L(E)\) controls left multiplication. These are the two one-sided multiplicative domains from ( .

LaTeX
Lean
  • KadisonSchwarz.rightMultiplicativeDomain
  • KadisonSchwarz.leftMultiplicativeDomain
Definition 14.9Sector data and sector decomposition

A sector datum over \(g\) basis blocks specifies: multiplicities \(r_j {\gt} 0\), sector weights \(\mu _{j,q} \neq 0\) for \(q \in \{ 1,\dots ,r_j\} \), and the sector coefficients

\[ c_{S,j}^{(N)} := \sum _{q=1}^{r_j} \mu _{j,q}^N \]

for a sector datum \(S\). A sector decomposition consists of the basis blocks \((A_j)_{j=1}^g\) together with their sector data.

LaTeX
Lean
  • MPSTensor.SectorWeightData
  • MPSTensor.SectorDecomposition
Definition 18.9Parent Hamiltonian

The chain Hamiltonian is

\[ H_N(A,L) := \sum _{i=0}^{N-1} h_i. \]
LaTeX Lean
Definition 18.6Parent interaction

The parent interaction at range \(L\) is the orthogonal projector

\[ h_L(A) := \Pi _{G_L(A)^\perp }, \]

on the local \(L\)-site space.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 5.40Partial traces

Let \(X \in M_{d \cdot d'}(\mathbb {C})\) be a bipartite matrix indexed by \((\mathrm{Fin}\, d \times \mathrm{Fin}\, d')^2\). The left partial trace \(\operatorname {tr}_A(X)\) and right partial trace \(\operatorname {tr}_B(X)\) are the \(d' \times d'\) and \(d \times d\) matrices defined by

\[ (\operatorname {tr}_A(X))_{ij} = \sum _{k} X_{(k,i),(k,j)}, \qquad (\operatorname {tr}_B(X))_{ij} = \sum _{k} X_{(i,k),(j,k)}. \]
LaTeX
Lean
  • Matrix.traceLeft
  • Matrix.traceRight
Definition 12.5Per-block linear extension

Given injective tensors \(A_k, B_k\) of bond dimension \(D_k\) with the same MPV for each block \(k\), the per-block linear extension \(T_k\) is the unique linear map \(M_{D_k}(\mathbb {C})\to M_{D_k}(\mathbb {C})\) satisfying \(T_k(A_k^i)=B_k^i\) for all \(i\). It exists (and is unique) because \(\{ A_k^i\} \) spans \(M_{D_k}(\mathbb {C})\) by injectivity.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 16.23Per-block linear extension

Let \(A_k, B_k\) be MPS tensors of bond dimension \(D_k\) with \(A_k\) injective and \(\mathcal{V}(A_k) = \mathcal{V}(B_k)\) for each block \(k \in \{ 0, \ldots , r{-}1\} \). The per-block linear extension \(T_k : M_{D_k}(\mathbb {C}) \to M_{D_k}(\mathbb {C})\) is the unique \(\mathbb {C}\)-linear map satisfying \(T_k(A_k^i) = B_k^i\) for every physical index \(i\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 2.7Periodic-chain tensor alias

For a fixed period \(m\), a periodic-chain tensor is a site-dependent tensor family indexed by \(\{ 0,\ldots ,m-1\} \), with local physical dimension \(d\) and bond dimension \(D\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 2.8Periodic same-state and gauge-equivalence relations

For periodic-chain tensors \(A,B\) at fixed period \(m\):

  • equality of the induced periodic-chain states;

  • cyclic gauge equivalence.

LaTeX
Lean
  • MPSTensor.PeriodicMPSTensor.SameState
  • MPSTensor.PeriodicMPSTensor.GaugeEquiv
Definition 14.15Periodic tensor

An irreducible MPS tensor \(A\) of bond dimension \(D\) is \(m\)-periodic (for some \(m \ge 1\)) if the peripheral spectrum of its transfer map \(\mathcal{E}_A\) is exactly the \(m\)-th roots of unity \(\{ e^{2\pi i k/m} : 0 \le k {\lt} m\} \). A \(1\)-periodic tensor is exactly an irreducible tensor with primitive transfer map (Definition 5.37).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 5.23Peripheral eigenvalues

The peripheral eigenvalues of a linear map \(E\) on a finite-dimensional space are the eigenvalues \(\lambda \) on the unit circle: \(|\lambda | = 1\). For a channel, the spectral radius is \(1\) ( , so these coincide with the eigenvalues of maximal modulus.

Note: The condition \(|\lambda | = 1\) is appropriate for channels, since the spectral radius of a channel is \(1\). For a general linear map with spectral radius \(r \neq 1\) the condition would generalise to \(|\lambda | = r\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 5.22Peripheral spectrum

The peripheral spectrum of a bounded linear operator \(T\) is the set of spectral values \(\mu \) with \(|\mu |\) equal to the spectral radius of \(T\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 17.33Permutation-twisted tensor

Given on-site symmetry data \((u, \sigma )\), the permutation-twisted tensor at group element \(g\) is

\[ (A^{\sigma _g})^i := A^{\sigma (g)(i)}. \]

In tensor-network notation the local tensor is unchanged and only the physical leg is relabelled:

\[ \begin{array}{ccccc} i & & & & \sigma (g)(i) \\ \vert & & & & \vert \\ \bullet & & \xrightarrow {\ \sigma _g\ } & & \bullet \end{array} \]
LaTeX Lean
Definition 16.5Physical realisation map

Let \(A\) be an injective tensor. For \(X \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\), define the \(d \times d\) matrix \(O_A(X) \in M_{d}(\mathbb {C})\) by

\[ O_A(X)_{ij} := (\Psi _A(A^i X))_j, \]

so that row \(i\) of \(O_A(X)\) contains the decomposition coefficients of \(A^i X\) in the spanning set \(\{ A^j\} \). Diagrammatically, the inserted virtual matrix is re-expressed as a physical operation on the same local tensor:

\[ \begin{array}{ccccccccc} \multicolumn{3}{c}{X} & & & \multicolumn{3}{c}{O_A(X)} \\ & & & & & & \vert & & \\ \bullet & \text{---} & \textcolor{red}{\bullet } & & = & & \bullet & & \\ & & & & & & \vert & & \\ & & & & & & \textcolor{red}{\bullet } & & \end{array} \]

The map \(X \mapsto O_A(X)\) is \(\mathbb {C}\)-linear.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 12.6Product algebra automorphism

The product algebra automorphism is the blockwise map \(T:\prod _kM_{D_k}(\mathbb {C})\to \prod _kM_{D_k}(\mathbb {C})\) defined by \(T(M)_k := T_k(M_k)\). Theorem 12.7 shows that each \(T_k\) is a \(\mathbb {C}\)-algebra homomorphism and that this map is a \(\mathbb {C}\)-algebra automorphism.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 16.26Product algebra automorphism

The product algebra automorphism is the \(\mathbb {C}\)-algebra equivalence

\[ T : \prod _{k=0}^{r-1} M_{D_k}(\mathbb {C}) \xrightarrow { \sim } \prod _{k=0}^{r-1} M_{D_k}(\mathbb {C}) \]

defined by \(T(M)_k = T_k(M_k)\), where each \(T_k\) is the per-block linear extension. It is an algebra automorphism since each \(T_k\) is a multiplicative, unital, bijective linear map. Diagrammatically, this is the blockwise map on the product algebra obtained by applying the corresponding per-block extension to each summand.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 5.1Positive map

A linear map \(E : M_{D}(\mathbb {C}) \to M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) is positive if \(E(X) \ge 0\) whenever \(X \ge 0\), where \(X \ge 0\) means that \(X\) is positive semidefinite.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 5.37Primitive map

A linear map is primitive if its only peripheral eigenvalue is \(\lambda = 1\), i.e. \(\mathrm{peripheral}(E) = \{ 1\} \). For channels this corresponds to ( . This definition applies to any linear endomorphism, not only to channels.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 10.43Primitive MPS tensor

Assume \(D {\gt} 0\). A tensor \(A\) together with a matrix \(\rho \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) is primitive if

\[ \sum _i (A^i)^\dagger A^i = \mathbb {1}, \qquad \rho \ge 0, \qquad \rho \neq 0, \qquad \mathcal{E}_A(\rho ) = \rho , \]

and, if \(P\) is the fixed-point projection associated to \(\rho \), then \(\rho _{\mathrm{spec}}(\mathcal{E}_A - P) {\lt} 1\). When the choice of \(\rho \) is irrelevant, we simply say that \(A\) is a primitive MPS tensor. This is weaker than the paper’s notion, which requires \(\rho {\gt} 0\); see Remark 10.49. The stronger positive-definite hypothesis is imposed separately in Theorems 10.50 and 10.61.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 17.19Projective representation

Given a scalar 2-cochain \(\omega \), a projective representation of \(G\) on \(\mathbb {C}^D\) is a map \(\rho : G \to \mathrm{GL}_D(\mathbb {C})\) satisfying

\[ \rho (g)\, \rho (h) = \omega (g,h)\, \rho (gh) \qquad \text{for all } g, h \in G. \]
LaTeX Lean
Definition 17.30Projective equivalence

Two projective representations are projectively equivalent when their factor systems are cohomologous.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 2.14Proportional MPV

Two tensors \(A\) and \(B\) (of possibly different bond dimensions) generate proportional MPV families if for every \(N\) there exists \(c_N \in \mathbb {C}\) such that \(|V^{(N)}(A)\rangle = c_N \, |V^{(N)}(B)\rangle \).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 3.14Purifying MPS tensor

Given a purifying family \(A^{(i,k)} \in M_{D'}(\mathbb {C})\) for \(i \in \{ 0,\ldots ,d{-}1\} \) and \(k \in \{ 0,\ldots ,d_K{-}1\} \), the purifying MPS tensor is the MPS tensor with physical dimension \(d \cdot d_K\) and bond dimension \(D'\) obtained by bundling the pair \((i,k)\) into a single index.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 18.16Right restriction

Fix the first physical index \(i\):

\[ (\mathrm{restrictFirst} \psi i)(\sigma ) := \psi (i,\sigma _1,\ldots ,\sigma _L). \]
LaTeX Lean
Definition 18.15Left restriction

Fix the last physical index \(j\):

\[ (\mathrm{restrictLast} \psi j)(\sigma ) := \psi (\sigma _1,\ldots ,\sigma _L,j). \]
LaTeX Lean
Definition 17.2Physical-index rotation

Given a matrix \(u \in M_d(\mathbb {C})\) and an MPS tensor \(A\), the physical-index rotation is the tensor \((A_u)^i := \sum _j u_{ij}\, A^j\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 2.11Same MPV

Two tensors \(A\) and \(B\) (of the same bond dimension) generate the same MPV family if \(|V^{(N)}(A)\rangle = |V^{(N)}(B)\rangle \) for every system size \(N\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 2.12Same MPV — different bond dimensions

For tensors \(A\) and \(B\) of possibly different bond dimensions \(D_1\) and \(D_2\), we write \(\mathcal{V}(A) = \mathcal{V}(B)\) if \(|V^{(N)}(A)\rangle = |V^{(N)}(B)\rangle \) for every \(N\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 16.16Finite-length MPV agreement

Two tensors \(A\) and \(B\) of the same bond dimension satisfy finite-length MPV agreement from \(N_0\) onward if they generate the same MPV family for all system sizes \(N \ge N_0\):

\[ V^{(N)}(A)_\sigma = V^{(N)}(B)_\sigma \qquad \text{for all } N \ge N_0, \sigma \in [d]^N. \]
LaTeX Lean
Definition 17.18Scalar 2-cochain

A scalar 2-cochain on a group \(G\) is a function \(\omega : G \times G \to \mathbb {C}^{\times }\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 9.35Scalar conditional expectation

For \(\sigma \ge 0\) with \(\operatorname{tr}(\sigma )\neq 0\), define

\[ E_\sigma (X) := \frac{\operatorname{tr}(\sigma X)}{\operatorname{tr}(\sigma )}\, \mathbb {1}. \]
LaTeX Lean
Definition 6.11SSA equality

A tripartite density matrix \(\rho _{ABC}\) satisfies SSA equality if

\[ S(\rho _{ABC}) + S(\rho _B) = S(\rho _{AB}) + S(\rho _{BC}). \]
LaTeX Lean
Definition 9.49Stationary state

For an irreducible channel \(E\) on \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) (with \(D \ge 1\)), the stationary state is the unique density-matrix fixed point \(\rho _\infty \) satisfying \(E(\rho _\infty ) = \rho _\infty \), \(\rho _\infty {\gt} 0\), and \(\operatorname{tr}(\rho _\infty ) = 1\). Uniqueness follows from the quantum Perron–Frobenius theorem (Theorem 8.9).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 9.50Stationary support

The stationary support of an irreducible channel \(E\) is the support projection of the stationary state (Definition 9.49).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 5.56Stinespring isometry

Given Kraus operators \(\{ K_j\} _{j=0}^{r-1}\) with \(K_j \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\), the Stinespring isometry \(V : \mathbb {C}^D \to \mathbb {C}^D \otimes \mathbb {C}^r\) is the \((D \cdot r) \times D\) matrix

\[ V_{(i,j),k} = (K_j)_{ik}. \]

This implements \(V = \sum _j K_j \otimes \lvert j\rangle \).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 17.37String order parameter

The string order parameter for twist \(u\) and stationary state \(\Lambda \) at length \(L\) is

\[ R_L(u) := \operatorname{tr}\! (\Lambda \cdot \mathcal{E}_u^L(\mathbb {1})). \]

In tensor-network notation this is the length-\(L\) doubled chain with a copy of \(u\) inserted on each physical rung:

\[ \begin{array}{ccccccccc} \multicolumn{9}{c}{\operatorname {tr}} \\ \bullet & \text{---} & \begin{array}{c}\bullet \\ \vert \\ \textcolor{red}{\bullet }\\ \vert \\ \bullet \end{array}& \text{---} & \begin{array}{c}\bullet \\ \vert \\ \textcolor{red}{\bullet }\\ \vert \\ \bullet \end{array}& \text{---} & \cdots & \text{---} & \begin{array}{c}\bullet \\ \vert \\ \textcolor{red}{\bullet }\\ \vert \\ \bullet \end{array}\\ \Lambda & & & & & & & & \\ \multicolumn{9}{c}{u} \\ \multicolumn{9}{c}{L} \end{array} \]

The boundary matrix \(\Lambda \) is contracted into the virtual boundary, the red nodes labelled \(u\) sit on the physical rungs, and the right boundary is traced.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 11.11Support projection

For a positive semi-definite matrix \(\rho \ge 0\), the support projection \(P\) is the orthogonal projection onto the range of \(\rho \). It is constructed via the spectral decomposition: if \(\rho = U \operatorname{diag}(\lambda _1,\ldots ,\lambda _D) U^\dagger \), then \(P = U \operatorname{diag}(\mathbf{1}_{\lambda _1{\gt}0},\ldots ,\mathbf{1}_{\lambda _D{\gt}0}) U^\dagger \), where \(\mathbf{1}_{\lambda _j{\gt}0}\) is \(1\) if \(\lambda _j {\gt} 0\) and \(0\) otherwise.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 11.1Block-diagonal tensor from blocks

Recall the block-diagonal tensor of Definition 2.27. Given \(r\) blocks with bond dimensions \((D_k)_{k=1}^r\), per-block MPS tensors \(\{ A_k^i\} \), and scaling factors \((\mu _k)_{k=1}^r\), we write \(\bigoplus _{k=1}^r \mu _k A_k\) for the tensor of total bond dimension \(D = \sum _k D_k\) whose Kraus operators are \(A^i = \bigoplus _{k=1}^r \mu _k A_k^i\), obtained by placing the scaled block Kraus operators on the diagonal.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 5.42Tensor product of a map with identity

Given a linear map \(T : M_{D}(\mathbb {C}) \to M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\), the tensor extension \(T \otimes \operatorname{id}\) acts on bipartite matrices \(X \in M_{D \times D}(\mathbb {C})\) by applying \(T\) to each “slice”:

\[ (T \otimes \operatorname{id})(X)_{(i_1,i_2),(j_1,j_2)} = (T(X^{(i_2,j_2)}))_{i_1 j_1}, \]

where \(X^{(i_2,j_2)}_{ab} = X_{(a,i_2),(b,j_2)}\) is the bipartite slice.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 3.20Diagonal pure-state embedding as an MPO

An MPS tensor \(A = \{ A^i\} _{i=0}^{d-1}\) determines an MPO tensor by placing \(A^i\) on the diagonal in the bra and ket indices:

\[ M^{ij} = \delta _{ij} A^i. \]

Equivalently, \(M^{ii} = A^i\) and \(M^{ij} = 0\) for \(i \ne j\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 7.4Trace-preserving Kraus map

A Kraus map is trace-preserving if \(\sum _{i=0}^{d-1} K_i^\dagger K_i = \mathbb {1}\). Equivalently, the adjoint Kraus map is unital. This is the standard MPS normalization condition. In the later gauge language it is the left-canonical condition, so Kadison–Schwarz arguments are often applied to the adjoint map.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 5.63Trace-pairing coefficients

For a linear map \(T : M_{D}(\mathbb {C}) \to M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) and a trace-self-dual basis \(\{ \sigma _i\} \), define coefficients

\[ t_{ij} := \operatorname {tr}(\sigma _i\, T(\sigma _j)). \]
LaTeX Lean
Definition 4.3Trace pairing map

For a tensor \(A\), attach to the chosen family \(\{ A^i\} \) the auxiliary linear map \(\Phi _A : M_{D}(\mathbb {C}) \to \mathbb {C}^d\) defined by

\[ \Phi _A(M)_i = \operatorname{tr}(M \cdot A^i). \]
LaTeX Lean
Definition 5.62Trace-self-dual basis

A basis \(\{ \sigma _i\} _i\) of \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) is trace-self-dual when its coordinate functionals are given by trace pairing:

\[ X = \sum _i \operatorname {tr}(\sigma _i X)\, \sigma _i. \]
LaTeX Lean
Definition 5.3Trace-preserving map

A linear map \(E : M_{D}(\mathbb {C}) \to M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) is trace-preserving if \(\operatorname{tr}(E(X)) = \operatorname{tr}(X)\) for all \(X\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 6.6Partial trace over \(A\)

For a tripartite matrix \(\rho _{ABC}\) on \(\mathbb {C}^{d_A} \otimes \mathbb {C}^{d_B} \otimes \mathbb {C}^{d_C}\), the partial trace over \(A\) is

\[ (\operatorname{tr}_A \rho _{ABC})_{(b_1, c_1)(b_2, c_2)} = \sum _{a=0}^{d_A - 1} (\rho _{ABC})_{(a, b_1, c_1)(a, b_2, c_2)}. \]
LaTeX Lean
Definition 6.8Partial trace over \(AC\)

The partial trace over \(A\) and \(C\):

\[ (\operatorname{tr}_{AC} \rho _{ABC})_{b_1 b_2} = \sum _{a=0}^{d_A - 1} \sum _{c=0}^{d_C - 1} (\rho _{ABC})_{(a, b_1, c)(a, b_2, c)}. \]
LaTeX Lean
Definition 6.7Partial trace over \(C\)

The partial trace over \(C\):

\[ (\operatorname{tr}_C \rho _{ABC})_{(a_1, b_1)(a_2, b_2)} = \sum _{c=0}^{d_C - 1} (\rho _{ABC})_{(a_1, b_1, c)(a_2, b_2, c)}. \]
LaTeX Lean
Definition 2.5Transfer map

The transfer map associated to a tensor \(A\) is the linear map \(\mathcal{E}_A : M_{D}(\mathbb {C}) \to M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) defined by

\[ \mathcal{E}_A(X) \; =\; \sum _{i=0}^{d-1} A^i \, X \, (A^i)^\dagger . \]

Diagrammatically, the transfer map is the double-layer contraction

\[ \begin{array}{ccc} X & \text{---} & \mathcal{E}_{A}(X) \\ & \bullet & \\ & \vert & \\ & \bullet & \\ & i & \end{array} \]

in which the upper node denotes \(A\), the lower node denotes \(A^\dagger \), and the physical index is summed over between them.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 17.11Twisted tensor

Given a group representation \(U : G \to \mathrm{GL}_d(\mathbb {C})\) on the physical index, the \(g\)-twisted tensor is

\[ \widetilde{A}_g^i := \sum _{j=0}^{d-1} U(g)_{ij}\, A^j \qquad \text{for each } i \in \{ 0,\ldots ,d{-}1\} . \]
LaTeX Lean
Definition 17.36Twisted transfer map

Given an MPS tensor \(A\) and a physical-index matrix \(u\), the twisted transfer map is

\[ \mathcal{E}_u(X) := \sum _{n,n'} \langle n'|u|n\rangle A_n\, X\, A_{n'}^\dagger . \]

Diagrammatically one inserts the physical operator \(u\) on the doubled local transfer cell:

\[ \begin{array}{ccc} X & \text{---} & \mathcal{E}_{u}(X) \\ & \bullet & \\ & \vert & \\ u & \textcolor{red}{\bullet } & \\ & \vert & \\ & \bullet & \\ & n,\; n’ & \end{array} \]

The upper and lower black nodes denote \(A_n\) and \(A_{n'}^\dagger \), and the red node labelled \(u\) couples the physical legs.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 19.2Two-point expectation at distance \(n\)

For observables \(X,Y \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) and \(n\ge 0\),

\[ \langle X_0 Y_n \rangle := \operatorname{tr}\! \left(Y\, \mathcal{E}_A^n(X\rho ^R)\right). \]

Equivalently: insert \(X\) at site \(0\), propagate by \(\mathcal{E}_A^n\), then insert \(Y\) and take the trace.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 7.3Unital Kraus map

A Kraus map is unital if \(\sum _{i=0}^{d-1} K_i K_i^\dagger = \mathbb {1}\). Equivalently, \(E(\mathbb {1}) = \mathbb {1}\). In the later gauge language this is the right-canonical condition.

LaTeX Lean
Definition 5.66Unitary channel

For a unitary matrix \(U \in \mathcal{U}(D)\), the unitary channel is \(T(\rho ) = U\rho U^\dagger \).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 10.30Fixed-length vector span

The fixed-length vector span at length \(n\) is

\[ H_n(A, \varphi ) = \operatorname{span}_{\mathbb {C}}\{ A^w \varphi : |w| = n\} \subseteq \mathbb {C}^D. \]

This is \(S_n(A)|\varphi \rangle \) in the notation of  .

LaTeX Lean
Definition 3.25Vertical canonical form for an MPO

An MPO tensor is in vertical canonical form if its diagonal MPS tensor \(\{ M^{ii}\} _{i=0}^{d-1}\) generates the same MPV family as a block-diagonal tensor obtained from a basis of normal tensors by repeating each block according to a multiplicity and weighting each copy by a positive scalar. This is the flattened form of the decomposition \(\bigoplus _\alpha \mu _\alpha \otimes M_\alpha \) from ( .

LaTeX
Definition 6.1Von Neumann entropy

For a Hermitian matrix \(\rho \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) with eigenvalues \(\lambda _0, \ldots , \lambda _{D-1}\), the von Neumann entropy is

\[ S(\rho ) = -\sum _{i=0}^{D-1} \lambda _i \log \lambda _i, \]

where \(0 \log 0 := 0\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 10.23Wielandt analysis

A Wielandt analysis for a normal MPS tensor \(A\) consists of: a word \(w_0\) of length \(\le D^2\), a nonzero eigenvalue \(\mu \), and a nonzero eigenvector \(\varphi \) of the matrix \(A^{w_0}\), such that \(A^{w_0} \varphi = \mu \varphi \).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 10.1Word span

The word span at length \(n\) is the subspace

\[ S_n(A) = \operatorname{span}_{\mathbb {C}}\{ A^w : |w| = n\} \subseteq M_{D}(\mathbb {C}). \]

This is ( .

LaTeX Lean
Definition 14.24Diagonal periodic \(Z\)-gauge matrix

For matched weight families \((\mu _i)_i\) and \((\nu _i)_i\) on a finite index type, define the diagonal matrix

\[ Z := \operatorname{diag}\! \bigl(z(\mu _i,\nu _i)\bigr)_i. \]
LaTeX Lean
Definition 14.21Entrywise periodic \(Z\)-gauge ratio

Given complex weights \(\mu ,\nu \), define the entrywise \(Z\)-gauge ratio by

\[ z(\mu ,\nu ) := \mu /\nu . \]
LaTeX Lean
Definition 14.27\(Z\)-gauge equivalence

Let \(A\) be an \(m\)-periodic irreducible tensor of bond dimension \(D\). A \(Z\)-gauge transformation of \(A\) is a diagonal unitary matrix \(Z \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) satisfying:

  1. \(Z\) commutes with every Kraus operator: \([A^i, Z] = 0\) for all physical indices \(i\), and

  2. \(Z^m = \mathbb {1}_D\) (all diagonal entries are \(m\)-th roots of unity).

Replacing \(A\) by \(ZA\) (i.e. \(A^i \mapsto Z A^i\)) leaves the MPV family invariant for lengths \(N\) that are multiples of \(m\), because the factor \(\operatorname{tr}(Z^N) = \operatorname{tr}(\mathbb {1}) = D\) for \(N \equiv 0 \pmod{m}\). Two tensors \(A, B\) are \(Z\)-gauge equivalent if there exists an invertible \(Y\) and a \(Z\)-gauge transformation \(Z\) for \(A\) such that \(Z A^i = Y B^i Y^{-1}\) for all \(i\).

LaTeX Lean
Definition 11.38Zero MPS tensor

The zero MPS tensor of physical dimension \(d\) and bond dimension \(D\), denoted \(\mathbf{0}_{d,D}\), is the tensor whose Kraus operators are all zero.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 12.15Block separation core

Let \(((\mu _k), (A_k))\) satisfy the canonical form conditions, and let \((B_k)\) be injective tensors satisfying the same TP normalization \(\sum _i (B_k^i)^\dagger B_k^i = \mathbb {1}\). If \(\sum _k \mu _k^N (V^{(N)}(A_k)_\sigma - V^{(N)}(B_k)_\sigma ) = 0\) for all \(N,\sigma \), then each pair \((A_k, B_k)\) generates the same MPV family.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 16.9Blocking preserves injectivity

Let \(A_0, \ldots , A_{m-1}\) be injective MPS tensors with compatible bond dimensions. Then the blocked tensor \((A_0 \otimes \cdots \otimes A_{m-1})^{(i_0,\ldots ,i_{m-1})} := A_0^{i_0} A_1^{i_1} \cdots A_{m-1}^{i_{m-1}}\) is again injective.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 10.34Blocking transfer

If the blocked tensor \(A^{[L]}\) satisfies \(S_n(A^{[L]}) = M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\), then \(S_{nL}(A) = M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 3.26Blockwise equality from agreement on the MPV family

Let \(\bigoplus _k \mu _k A_k\) be a block-diagonal tensor whose blocks are injective, left-canonical, weighted by nonzero scalars, and jointly block-injective in the sense that for some blocking length \(L\) the tuple of trace pairings against length-\(L\) block words is faithful across all blocks simultaneously (the block-injective canonical form, biCF). If two physical operators induce the same first-site action on every MPV generated by this tensor, then their first-site insertions agree on each block \(A_k\).

LaTeX Lean
