Lemma 18.60Each BNT MPV lies in the Hamiltonian kernel

Let \(A = \bigoplus _{k=1}^g \mu _k A_k\) be in canonical form with BNT separation (Definition 13.3), where \(\{ A_1,\ldots ,A_g\} \) is a basis of normal tensors (Definition 13.1). For \(N \ge 2(L_0 + 1)\) (where \(L_0\) is the maximum injectivity length of the blocks) and each \(k = 1,\ldots ,g\),

\[ V^{(N)}(A_k) \in \ker (H_N(A,L_0{+}1)). \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 16.10Centre of \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\)

If \(Z \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) commutes with every element of \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\), then \(Z = \lambda \mathbb {1}_D\) for some \(\lambda \in \mathbb {C}\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 4.13Cyclic gauge equivalence is an equivalence relation

On fixed-length periodic chains, cyclic gauge equivalence is reflexive, symmetric, and transitive.

LaTeX
Lean
  • MPSChainTensor.GaugeEquiv.refl
  • MPSChainTensor.GaugeEquiv.symm
  • MPSChainTensor.GaugeEquiv.trans
Lemma 17.26Coboundary iff cohomologous to the trivial cocycle

A cocycle \(\omega \) is a coboundary if and only if \(\omega \sim \mathbf{1}\), where \(\mathbf{1}(g,h)=1\) for all \(g,h\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 17.20Associativity forces the cocycle condition

If \(\rho \) is a projective representation with factor system \(\omega \) and \(D {\gt} 0\), then \(\omega \) satisfies the 2-cocycle identity:

\[ \omega (g, h)\, \omega (gh, k) = \omega (g, hk)\, \omega (h, k). \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 5.30Common exponent for a finite set of roots of unity

Let \(s \subseteq \mathbb {C}\) be a finite set. Assume that for every \(\mu \in s\) there exists an exponent \(p_\mu {\gt} 0\) with \(\mu ^{p_\mu } = 1\). Then there exists \(p {\gt} 0\) such that \(\mu ^p = 1\) for all \(\mu \in s\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 11.75Commuting letters imply commuting words

If a projection commutes with every Kraus operator, it commutes with every finite word product.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 18.25Iterated contiguous-window intersection

If an \(N\)-site state satisfies the \(L\)-site ground condition on every non-wrapping contiguous window (with \(L \ge 2\)), then it lies in \(G_N(A)\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 11.54Corner preservation from adjoint-fixed projection

Let \(A\) be a left-canonical MPS tensor and let \(P\) be an orthogonal projection fixed by \(\mathcal{E}_A^\dagger \). Then \(\mathcal{E}_A^\dagger \) preserves the corner algebra \(P \cdot M_D(\mathbb {C}) \cdot P\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 19.8Correlation length is positive

If \(|\lambda _2| {\lt} 1\) and \(\lambda _2 \ne 0\), then \(\xi {\gt} 0\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 10.4Dimension bound

\(\dim T_n(A) \le D^2\) for all \(n\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 10.3Monotonicity of cumulative span

\(T_n(A) \le T_{n+1}(A)\) for all \(n\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 6.3Eigenvalue sum

The eigenvalues of a density matrix sum to \(1\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 10.32Eigenvector padding

Suppose \(A^{i_0} \varphi = \mu \varphi \) with \(\mu \neq 0\). Then for all \(n\),

\[ K_n(A, \varphi ) = H_n(A, \varphi ). \]

In particular, if \(K_n(A,\varphi ) = \mathbb {C}^D\) then already \(H_n(A,\varphi ) = \mathbb {C}^D\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 2.3Multiplicativity of word evaluation

For any words \(w_1, w_2\), \(A^{w_1 \cdot w_2} = A^{w_1} \, A^{w_2}\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 2.30Blocked word evaluation

If \(w = (J_1, \ldots , J_m)\) is a word in the blocked alphabet, where each \(J_k\) is an \(L\)-tuple in \(\{ 0, \ldots , d{-}1\} ^L\), then concatenating the block words gives a word \(\widetilde{w}\) of length \(mL\) in the original alphabet and one has

\[ (A^{[L]})^w = A^{\widetilde{w}}. \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 2.18Gauge covariance of word evaluation

If \(B^i = X \, A^i \, X^{-1}\) for all \(i\), then for every word \(w\),

\[ B^w \; =\; X \, A^w \, X^{-1}. \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 2.15Scaling of word evaluation

Let \(\zeta A\) denote the tensor with matrices \(\{ \zeta \, A^i\} _{i=0}^{d-1}\). Then for any word \(w\),

\[ (\zeta A)^w \; =\; \zeta ^{|w|} \, A^w, \]

where \(|w|\) is the length of \(w\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 16.3Decomposition property

If \(A\) is injective, then for every \(X \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) there exist coefficients \(c \in \mathbb {C}^d\) such that

\[ X = \sum _{i=0}^{d-1} c_i\, A^i. \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 16.1Existence of a right inverse

If \(A\) is injective, then there exists a \(\mathbb {C}\)-linear map \(\Psi : M_{D}(\mathbb {C}) \to \mathbb {C}^d\) satisfying \(\Phi _A \circ \Psi = \mathrm{id}_{M_{D}(\mathbb {C})}\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 9.11Trace formula for Frobenius norm

\(\| X\| _F^2 = \operatorname {Re}\operatorname{tr}(X^\dagger X)\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 18.53Frustration-free identity

For any endomorphisms \(P\) and \(Q\),

\[ (1-P) + (1-Q) - (1-P)\circ (1-Q) = 1 - P\circ Q. \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 17.15Symmetric twists are gauge equivalent

If \(A\) is injective and on-site symmetric under \(U\), then for every \(g \in G\) the twisted tensor \(\widetilde{A}_g\) is gauge equivalent to \(A\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 16.39Gauge ratio commutes with tensor matrices

Diagrammatically, the hypothesis is that the two local gauges

\[ \begin{array}{ccccc}& & i & & \\ & & \vert & & \\ \textcolor{red}{\bullet } & \text{---} & \bullet & \text{---} & \textcolor{red}{\bullet } \\ X & & & & X^{-1} \end{array} \qquad \text{and}\qquad \begin{array}{ccccc}& & i & & \\ & & \vert & & \\ \textcolor{red}{\bullet } & \text{---} & \bullet & \text{---} & \textcolor{red}{\bullet } \\ Y & & & & Y^{-1} \end{array} \]

produce the same target tensor \(B^i\) from the same source tensor \(A^i\). If \(X, Y \in \mathrm{GL}_D(\mathbb {C})\) both conjugate an MPS tensor \(A\) to the same tensor \(B\) (i.e. \(B^i = X A^i X^{-1} = Y A^i Y^{-1}\) for all \(i\)), then \(Y^{-1} X\) commutes with every \(A^i\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 16.40Gauge ratio is scalar for injective tensors

Under the same hypotheses, if \(A\) is additionally injective, then \(Y^{-1} X = \lambda \mathbb {1}_D\) for some scalar \(\lambda \in \mathbb {C}\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 17.17Gauge uniqueness up to scalar

Let \(A\) be an injective tensor. If \(X, Y \in \mathrm{GL}_D(\mathbb {C})\) both satisfy \(B^i = X\, A^i\, X^{-1} = Y\, A^i\, Y^{-1}\) for all \(i\), then there exists a nonzero scalar \(u \in \mathbb {C}^{\times }\) such that \(Y = u \cdot X\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 18.3Dimension bound

For every \(L\),

\[ \dim G_L(A) \le D^2. \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 18.19Left restriction

If \(\psi \in G_{L+1}(A)\), i.e. \(\psi (\sigma ) = \operatorname{tr}(A^\sigma X)\) for some \(X\), then \(\mathrm{restrictLast} \psi j \in G_L(A)\) with witness \(A^j X\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 18.20Right restriction

If \(\psi \in G_{L+1}(A)\) with \(\psi (\sigma ) = \operatorname{tr}(A^\sigma X)\), then \(\mathrm{restrictFirst} \psi i \in G_L(A)\) with witness \(X A^i\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 18.5Nontriviality criterion

If \(d^L {\gt} D^2\), then \(G_L(A)\) is a proper subspace of the local Hilbert space:

\[ G_L(A) \neq (\{ 0,\ldots ,d{-}1\} ^L \to \mathbb {C}). \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 9.61Peripheral eigenvectors are invertible

Let \(E\) be an irreducible unital Kraus map on \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) with a positive-definite adjoint fixed point. If \(X \ne 0\) satisfies \(E(X) = \mu X\) with \(|\mu | = 1\), then \(X\) is invertible.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 11.76Left multiplication identity for sector words

If a projection commutes with all Kraus operators, then word evaluation for the left sector tensor equals left multiplication by the projection.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 11.77Sector support

The left sector tensor is supported on the chosen projection.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 4.10Promotion to algebra homomorphism

If \(T : M_{D}(\mathbb {C}) \to M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) is a multiplicative surjective \(\mathbb {C}\)-linear map, then \(T(\mathbb {1}) = \mathbb {1}\). Equivalently, the same underlying function defines a \(\mathbb {C}\)-algebra homomorphism.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 18.12Local term annihilates the MPV

For each site \(i\), \(h_i\, V^{(N)}(A) = 0\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 9.47Lower-triangular Kraus condition implies corner invariance

Let \(A\) be an MPS tensor and let \(P\) be an orthogonal projection. If

\[ (\mathbb {1}- P) A^i P = 0 \qquad \text{for all } i, \]

then

\[ P \, \mathcal{E}_A(P X P) \, P = \mathcal{E}_A(P X P) \]

for all \(X \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 18.58Martingale condition for MPS

Let \(A\) be injective and fix an interaction range \(L \ge 2\) as in Definition 18.6. Let \(h_L(A) := \Pi _{G_L(A)^\perp }\) be the \(L\)-site parent interaction, and for each chain length and site \(i\) let \(h_i\) denote its translate as in Definition 18.8. Then for every pair of overlapping terms \(h_i\) and \(h_j\) with \(0 {\lt} d_N(i,j) {\lt} L\), where \(d_N(i,j) := \min (|i-j|,\, N-|i-j|)\) is the cyclic distance on the ring (i.e. whose \(L\)-site supports share at least one site under periodic boundary conditions), the martingale condition of Theorem 18.57 holds with constants \(c_{ij}\) and \(\gamma {\gt} 0\) depending only on \(A\) (not on \(N\)). Note: The proof below fully establishes the martingale constants only for \(L = 2\); for \(L {\gt} 2\) the existence of suitable constants is asserted without complete proof (see the remark at the end of the proof). Only the \(L = 2\) case is used in this chapter (Theorem 18.59). (When \(L = 1\) the interaction terms have disjoint supports, so there are no overlapping pairs and the spectral gap follows directly without the martingale machinery.)

LaTeX
Lemma 3.7Identification with the doubled-index MPS transfer map

The transfer map of an MPO tensor agrees with the transfer map of its doubled-index MPS view.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 2.31Blocking preserves MPV coefficients

For each basis state \(\sigma = (\sigma _1, \ldots , \sigma _N)\) of the blocked system, where each \(\sigma _k\) is an \(L\)-tuple in \(\{ 0, \ldots , d{-}1\} ^L\), there exists a basis state \(\widetilde{\sigma } = (\widetilde{\sigma }_1, \ldots , \widetilde{\sigma }_{NL})\) of the original system such that

\[ V^{(N)}(A^{[L]})_\sigma \; =\; V^{(NL)}(A)_{\widetilde{\sigma }}. \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 18.27MPV lies in the ground space

\(V^{(N)}(A) \in G_N(A)\) for every \(N\), with boundary matrix \(X = I\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 18.11MPV window membership

For any window of \(L\) consecutive sites starting at position \(i\) in an \(N\)-site periodic chain (\(L \le N\)), the restriction of the MPV state to that window lies in \(G_L(A)\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 9.13Norm of embedded matrix

\(\| \mathrm{vec}(X)\| ^2 = \| X\| _F^2\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 18.4Ambient-space dimension

The coefficient-function model satisfies

\[ \dim \! (\{ 0,\ldots ,d{-}1\} ^L \to \mathbb {C})=d^L. \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 11.57Orbit iterate preserves orthogonal projection

Under the same cyclic shift and multiplicative-domain hypotheses as Lemma 11.56, suppose additionally that \(T\) maps every orthogonal projection supported on a single sector to an orthogonal projection. If \(Q\) is an orthogonal projection supported on \(P_k\), then \(T^l(Q)\) is an orthogonal projection for each \(0 \le l {\lt} m\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 11.56Orbit iterate supported on shifted sector

Let \((P_k)_{k=1}^m\) be orthogonal projections with cyclic shift \(T(P_{k+1}) = P_k\), and suppose \(T\) is multiplicative on each sector corner. If \(Q\) is supported on sector \(P_k\) (i.e. \(QP_k = Q = P_k Q\)), then for each \(0 \le l {\lt} m\) the iterate \(T^l(Q)\) is supported on the shifted sector \(P_{k-l}\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 11.55Orbit-sum fixed point

Let \(T\) be a linear endomorphism and \(Q\) a matrix satisfying \(T^m(Q) = Q\). Then the orbit sum \(\sum _{l=0}^{m-1} T^l(Q)\) is a fixed point of \(T\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 11.58Orbit sum of a full sector projection

Let \((P_k)_{k=1}^m\) be orthogonal projections with \(\sum _k P_k = \mathbb {1}\) and cyclic shift \(T(P_{k+1}) = P_k\). Then for every sector index \(k\),

\[ \sum _{l=0}^{m-1} T^l(P_k) \; =\; \mathbb {1}. \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 2.36Overlap equals conjugate inner product

\(O_{AB}(N) = \overline{\langle V^{(N)}(A) | V^{(N)}(B) \rangle }\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 2.37Overlap determined by positive-length MPV

If \(V^{(N)}(A)_\sigma = V^{(N)}(A')_\sigma \) and \(V^{(N)}(B)_\sigma = V^{(N)}(B')_\sigma \) for all \(N {\gt} 0\) and all \(\sigma \), then \(O_{AB}(N) = O_{A'B'}(N)\) for all positive \(N\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 11.53Pairwise orthogonality from projection sum

Let \((P_k)_{k=1}^m\) be orthogonal projections summing to the identity: \(\sum _k P_k = \mathbb {1}\). Then for \(i \neq j\) we have \(P_i P_j = 0\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 18.13Parent Hamiltonian annihilates the MPV

For every \(N\), the MPV state satisfies

\[ H_N(A,L)\, V^{(N)}(A) = 0. \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 18.14Frustration-freeness of the MPV

The MPV state is frustration-free for the parent model.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 18.7Parent interaction kills ground-space elements

For any \(v \in G_L(A)\), we have \(h_L(A)\, v = 0\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 16.25Per-block bijectivity

Each \(T_k\) is bijective.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 16.24Per-block multiplicativity

For each block \(k\), the per-block linear extension \(T_k\) is multiplicative: \(T_k(MN) = T_k(M)\, T_k(N)\) for all \(M, N \in M_{D_k}(\mathbb {C})\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 2.9Equivalence structures for periodic relations

Both periodic relations are equivalence relations (reflexive, symmetric, transitive).

LaTeX
Lean
  • MPSTensor.PeriodicMPSTensor.instEquivalenceSameState
  • MPSTensor.PeriodicMPSTensor.instEquivalenceGaugeEquiv
Lemma 5.33Peripheral closure via adjoint fixed point

Let \(E(X) = \sum _i K_i X K_i^\dagger \) be a Kraus map that is unital (\(\sum _i K_i K_i^\dagger = \mathbb {1}\)), and assume:

  1. the adjoint Kraus map \(E^*(X) = \sum _i K_i^\dagger X K_i\) has a positive definite fixed point \(\rho {\gt} 0\), and

  2. \(E\) is irreducible.

Then \(\mathrm{peripheral}(E)\) is closed under powers: if \(\mu \in \mathrm{peripheral}(E)\) then \(\mu ^n \in \mathrm{peripheral}(E)\) for every \(n \in \mathbb {N}\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 9.62Peripheral eigenvalues: product closure

Let \(E\) be an irreducible unital Kraus map on \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) with a positive-definite adjoint fixed point. If \(\mu , \nu \) are peripheral eigenvalues of \(E\), then so is \(\mu \nu \).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 5.31Powering collapses peripheral eigenvalues

Let \(E : M_{D}(\mathbb {C}) \to M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) be a linear map, and assume that \(E\) has a nonzero fixed point \(\rho \neq 0\). Let \(p {\gt} 0\). If every peripheral eigenvalue \(\mu \) of \(E\) satisfies \(\mu ^p = 1\), then \(\mathrm{peripheral}(E^p) = \{ 1\} \).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 17.35Composition of permutation twists

If \(\sigma (gh) = \sigma (g) \circ \sigma (h)\) for all \(g, h \in G\), then

\[ A^{\sigma _{gh}} = (A^{\sigma _g})^{\sigma _h}. \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 17.34Identity permutation twist

If \(\sigma (1) = \mathrm{id}\), then \((A^{\sigma _1})^i = A^i\) for all \(i\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 16.6Defining property of the physical realisation map

For every \(X \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) and every physical index \(i\),

\[ A^i\, X = \sum _{j=0}^{d-1} O_A(X)_{ij}\, A^j. \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 16.28Nondegeneracy of the product trace pairing

Let \(M = (M_0, \ldots , M_{r-1}) \in \prod _{k} M_{D_k}(\mathbb {C})\). If \(\sum _{k} \operatorname{tr}(M_k N_k) = 0\) for all \(N = (N_0, \ldots , N_{r-1})\), then \(M = 0\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 16.29Injectivity of the product Gram map

Let \(\{ A_k\} \) be a family of injective block tensors. The product Gram map \(M \mapsto (k, i \mapsto \operatorname{tr}(M_k \cdot A_k^i))\) is injective.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 7.20Positive-definite trace test

If \(A{\gt}0\), \(X\ge 0\), and \(\operatorname{tr}(AX)=0\), then \(X=0\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 18.56Quadratic form to norm bound

Let \(H\) be a positive semidefinite self-adjoint operator on a finite-dimensional complex Hilbert space, and suppose that

\[ \gamma \, \langle v, H v\rangle \le \langle H v, H v\rangle \qquad \text{for all } v, \]

i.e. \(H^2 \ge \gamma H\) as a quadratic form, for some \(\gamma {\gt} 0\). Then for every \(v\) in the orthogonal complement of \(\ker H\),

\[ \gamma \, \| v\| \le \| H v\| . \]

In particular, every positive eigenvalue of \(H\) is at least \(\gamma \). The positive-semidefiniteness hypothesis is essential: without it, \(H = -\mathrm{Id}\) with \(\gamma = 2\) satisfies the quadratic-form inequality vacuously but violates the norm bound.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 4.2Same MPV implies trace agreement

If \(A\) and \(B\) generate the same MPV family, then \(\operatorname{tr}(A^w) = \operatorname{tr}(B^w)\) for every word \(w\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 9.36Evaluation formula

\(E_\sigma (X)=\dfrac {\operatorname{tr}(\sigma X)}{\operatorname{tr}(\sigma )}\, \mathbb {1}\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 9.40Fixes scalar operators

For \(c\in \mathbb {C}\), \(E_\sigma (c\, \mathbb {1})=c\, \mathbb {1}\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 9.43Image lies in adjoint fixed points

For all \(X\), \(E_\sigma (X)\) is fixed by \(T^*\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 9.39Range is scalar

For every \(X\), there exists \(c\in \mathbb {C}\) with \(E_\sigma (X)=c\, \mathbb {1}\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 14.33Sector-block normality for periodic tensors

For a periodic tensor equipped with a cyclic sector decomposition, each nonzero blocked sector tensor is normal.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 14.35Sector-match propagation under translation

A single gauge-phase match between blocked sectors propagates to all cyclic translates of that sector pair.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 14.36Per-site proportionality from blocked sector match

If all aligned blocked sectors match up to gauge and phase, then the original tensors are related by repeated-block proportionality.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 8.13Similarity transform preserves irreducibility

Let \(E\) be an irreducible completely positive map on \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\), let \(C \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) be invertible (\(\det C \neq 0\)), and let \(c {\gt} 0\). Define the similarity-transformed map

\[ E'(X) \; =\; c \cdot C^{-1}\, E\! \bigl(C\, X\, C^\dagger \bigr)\, (C^\dagger )^{-1}. \]

Then \(E'\) is also irreducible.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 5.57Entry formula for the Stinespring isometry

For indices \(i,k \in \{ 0,\dots ,D-1\} \) and \(j \in \{ 0,\dots ,r-1\} \),

\[ V_{(i,j),k} = (K_j)_{ik}. \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 15.11Dyson-series summability from factorial majorant

Assume a factorial majorant of Dyson–Phillips iterates:

\[ \| D_n(t)\| \le M\, \frac{(t\, \| L'-L\| \, M)^n}{n!}. \]

Then the Dyson series is summable in operator norm.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 16.12Aligned gauges force proportionality – Lemma 2 of

Let \(A_1, A_2\) and \(B_1, B_2\) be injective MPS tensors with the same bond dimension \(D\). Suppose that for all \(X \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) (on the internal bond) and all \(Y \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) (on the external bond),

\begin{align} \operatorname{tr}(A_1^{i}\, X\, A_2^{j}) & = \operatorname{tr}(B_1^{i}\, X\, B_2^{j}) \qquad \text{for all } i, j, \label{eq:internal_bond}\\ \operatorname{tr}(A_2^{j}\, Y\, A_1^{i}) & = \operatorname{tr}(B_2^{j}\, Y\, B_1^{i}) \qquad \text{for all } i, j. \label{eq:external_bond} \end{align}

Diagrammatically, Eq. ?? is the equality obtained by inserting \(X\) on the bond between the two sites,

\[ \begin{array}{ccccc} i & & & & j \\ \vert & & & & \vert \\ \bullet & \text{---} & \textcolor{red}{\bullet } & \text{---} & \bullet \\ & & X & & \\ & & \operatorname {Tr} & & \end{array} \]

while Eq. ?? is the corresponding equality with the insertion \(Y\) on the outer trace bond,

\[ \begin{array}{ccccc} i & & & & j \\ \vert & & & & \vert \\ \bullet & \text{---} & \bullet & & \\ & & \textcolor{red}{\bullet } & & \\ & & Y & & \end{array} \]

so the two hypotheses distinguish the internal and external virtual loops of the two-site periodic chain.

Then there exists a nonzero scalar \(\lambda \in \mathbb {C}^{\times }\) such that

\[ A_1^{i} = \lambda \, B_1^{i} \qquad \text{and}\qquad A_2^{j} = \lambda ^{-1}\, B_2^{j} \]

for all physical indices \(i, j\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 9.6Trace expansion over matrix units

For any linear endomorphism \(T\) on \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\), the operator trace can be expanded as

\[ \operatorname{Tr}(T) = \sum _{p,q = 0}^{D-1} (T(E_{pq}))_{pq}, \]

where \(E_{pq}\) is the matrix with \(1\) in position \((p,q)\) and \(0\) elsewhere.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 6.9Partial trace preserves Hermiticity

If \(\rho _{ABC}\) is Hermitian, then \(\operatorname{tr}_A(\rho _{ABC})\), \(\operatorname{tr}_C(\rho _{ABC})\), and \(\operatorname{tr}_{AC}(\rho _{ABC})\) are all Hermitian. The same holds for bipartite partial traces \(\operatorname{tr}_A(\rho _{AB})\) and \(\operatorname{tr}_B(\rho _{AB})\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 7.19Trace pairing for Kraus map and adjoint map

For all \(X,Y \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\),

\[ \operatorname{tr}\! \bigl(X\, E(Y)\bigr) = \operatorname{tr}\! \bigl(E^*(X)\, Y\bigr). \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 17.13Functoriality of twisting

The twisted tensor satisfies the composition law

\[ \widetilde{A}_{gh} = \widetilde{(\widetilde{A}_h)}_g, \]

i.e. twisting by \(gh\) is the same as first twisting by \(h\) and then by \(g\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 17.14Identity twist

Twisting by the identity group element is trivial: \(\widetilde{A}_1 = A\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 17.44Unitary Kraus mixing

If \(u\) is unitary, then replacing Kraus operators \(\{ A_i\} \) by their unitary mixtures \(\{ \sum _j u_{ij} A_j\} \) preserves the channel: \(\sum _i (\sum _j u_{ij} A_j)\, Y\, (\sum _j u_{ij} A_j)^\dagger = \sum _i A_i\, Y\, A_i^\dagger \).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 10.21Vector span from matrix span

If \(T_N(A) = M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) and \(\varphi \neq 0\), then \(K_N(A, \varphi ) = \mathbb {C}^D\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 16.11Virtual bond gauge – 3-site case

Let \(A = (A_0, A_1, A_2)\) and \(B = (B_0, B_1, B_2)\) be two 3-site injective periodic chains with common bond dimension \(D \ge 1\) and \(\mathcal{V}(A) = \mathcal{V}(B)\) (same MPV family). Then at the middle bond there exists an invertible matrix \(Z \in \mathrm{GL}_D(\mathbb {C})\) such that the virtual-insertion trace coefficients agree after conjugation:

\[ \operatorname{tr}(A_0^{i_0} X\, A_1^{i_1} A_2^{i_2}) = \operatorname{tr}(B_0^{i_0} Z^{-1} X Z\, B_1^{i_1} B_2^{i_2}) \]

for all \(X \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) and all physical indices \(i_0, i_1, i_2\).

In the paper  , Lemma 1 is stated for arbitrary chain length \(n \ge 3\) and any bond position, with uniqueness of \(Z\) up to scalar. The chain-level generalization to arbitrary \(n \ge 3\) and any bond position is future work.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 10.37Chunking: word span embeds into blocked word span

\(S_{nL}(A) \subseteq S_n(A^{[L]})\) for all \(n, L\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 10.31Word span products

\(S_m(A) \cdot S_n(A) \subseteq S_{m+n}(A)\): the product of word spans at lengths \(m\) and \(n\) is contained in the word span at length \(m + n\).

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 9.9Word trace as entrywise inner product

For a word \(\sigma \in \{ 0,\ldots ,d{-}1\} ^N\),

\[ \operatorname{tr}\bigl(A^\sigma (B^\sigma )^\dagger \bigr) = \sum _{j,k = 0}^{D-1} (A^\sigma )_{jk}\, \overline{(B^\sigma )_{jk}}. \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 10.7Word span characterises block injectivity

\(S_L(A) = M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) if and only if \(A\) is \(L\)-block injective.

LaTeX Lean
Lemma 14.26Diagonal \(Z\)-gauge transports diagonal weights

Under \(\nu _i\neq 0\) for all \(i\),

\[ Z\, \operatorname{diag}(\nu )=\operatorname{diag}(\mu ). \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 14.25Diagonal \(Z\)-gauge has finite order under matched powers

If \(\mu _i^m=\nu _i^m\) and \(\nu _i\neq 0\) for all \(i\), then

\[ Z^m=\mathbb {1}. \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 14.23Ratio rescales denominator back to numerator

If \(\nu \neq 0\), then

\[ z(\mu ,\nu )\, \nu =\mu . \]
LaTeX Lean
Lemma 14.22Matched powers imply root-of-unity ratio

If \(\mu ^m=\nu ^m\) and \(\nu \neq 0\), then

\[ z(\mu ,\nu )^m = 1. \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 11.78Adjoint-fixed projections commute with Kraus operators

If a projection is fixed by the adjoint transfer map of a trace-preserving tensor, then it commutes with each Kraus operator.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 8.16Adjoint transfer map is nonzero

If some Kraus operator \(A^i \neq 0\), then the adjoint transfer map \(\mathcal{E}_A^\dagger (X) = \sum _i (A^i)^\dagger X A^i\) is nonzero.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 5.75Thm. 6.13: Adjoint fixed points = Kraus commutant

Under the hypotheses of Theorem 5.73, the adjoint fixed-point \(*\)-subalgebra equals the Kraus commutant:

\[ \mathrm{Fix}(E^*) = \{ K_i, K_i^\dagger \} '. \]

This is ( .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.55Fixed-point algebra admits Wedderburn block decomposition

The adjoint-fixed-point \(*\)-subalgebra admits a concrete Wedderburn block decomposition inside the ambient matrix algebra (Wolf Eq. 1.39).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 5.73Thm. 6.12 in the Heisenberg picture

If the Kraus map is trace-preserving and has a positive definite fixed point \(\rho {\gt} 0\) with \(E(\rho ) = \rho \), then \(\mathrm{Fix}(E^*)\) is a \(*\)-subalgebra of \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.71Thm. 7.2: Adjoint kernel equals commutant (faithful state)

Let \(L\) be a GKSL generator in Lindblad form with Hamiltonian \(H\) and Lindblad operators \(\{ L_j\} \). Define the adjoint generator \(L^*\) by \(\operatorname {tr}(\rho \, L^*(A)) = \operatorname {tr}(L(\rho )\, A)\). If \(L\) has a faithful stationary state \(\rho _0 {\gt} 0\) with \(L(\rho _0) = 0\), then for any \(A\):

\[ L^*(A) = 0 \; \implies \; [A,H] = [A,L_j] = [A,L_j^\dagger ] = 0\ \forall j. \]

That is, \(\ker (L^*) \subseteq \{ H, L_j, L_j^\dagger \} '\). This is ( .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.72Thm. 7.2: Adjoint kernel equals commutant (full equivalence)

Under the same hypotheses as Theorem 15.71,

\[ \ker (L^*) = \{ H, L_j, L_j^\dagger \} ', \]

i.e. the adjoint kernel equals the commutant of \(\{ H, L_j, L_j^\dagger \} \). This is ( .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.42Adjoint map absorbs scalar projection

If \(T\) is trace-preserving, then \(T^*\circ E_\sigma = E_\sigma \).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 20.8AKLT tensor is normal

The transfer map of the AKLT tensor has a unique eigenvalue of maximal modulus (a simple spectral-radius eigenvalue); in particular the AKLT tensor is normal. Concretely, the length-\(2\) blocked tensor is injective.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 20.7AKLT tensor is not injective

The AKLT tensor is not injective at a single site: \(\operatorname{span}_{\mathbb {C}}\{ A^0, A^1, A^2\} \) is the three-dimensional space of traceless matrices \(\mathfrak {sl}(2,\mathbb {C})\), which is a proper subspace of \(M_{2}(\mathbb {C})\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 20.9\(\mathrm{SO}(3)\) on-site symmetry of the AKLT tensor

The AKLT tensor is on-site symmetric under the spin-\(1\) representation of \(\mathrm{SO}(3)\) (the spin-\(1\) representation of \(\mathrm{SU}(2)\) factors through \(\mathrm{SO}(3)\)), with the virtual representation being the projective spin-\(\tfrac 12\) representation. The projective phase is the non-trivial element of \(H^2(\mathrm{SO}(3),\mathrm{U}(1)) \cong \mathbb {Z}_2\), witnessing a non-trivial SPT phase. The \(\mathbb {Z}_2\) subgroup symmetry is proved below; the full \(\mathrm{SO}(3)\) projective representation is deferred.

LaTeX
Theorem 10.59Algebra span plus aperiodicity implies normality

If \(\operatorname{alg}(A) = M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) and \(\mathbb {1}\in S_1(A)\), then \(A\) is normal.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 11.84All-zero tensors are irreducible only in dimension at most one

If every Kraus operator vanishes and the tensor is irreducible, then the bond dimension is at most one.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 16.46Gauge invariance of PEPS state

Applying a gauge to a PEPS tensor preserves the state coefficients. Along each internal edge, the two endpoint insertions cancel:

\[ \begin{array}{c} \sum _j X_e(j,i)\, ((X_e^{-1})^\top )(j,k) = \delta (i,k) \\ \text{so the two edge gauges cancel on the shared edge index} \end{array} \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 12.16Block separation from canonical form

Let \(((\mu _k), (A_k))\) satisfy the canonical form conditions, and let \((B_k)\) be injective tensors satisfying the same TP normalization \(\sum _i (B_k^i)^\dagger B_k^i = \mathbb {1}\). If the block-diagonal tensors \(\bigoplus _k \mu _k A_k\) and \(\bigoplus _k \mu _k B_k\) generate the same MPV family, then each pair \((A_k, B_k)\) generates the same MPV family.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 12.17Block separation from normal canonical form

Let \(((\mu _k), (A_k))\) satisfy the normal canonical form conditions, and let \((B_k)\) be irreducible tensors of the same bond dimensions, satisfying the same TP normalization \(\sum _i (B_k^i)^\dagger B_k^i = \mathbb {1}\). If the block-diagonal tensors \(\bigoplus _k \mu _k A_k\) and \(\bigoplus _k \mu _k B_k\) generate the same MPV family, then each pair \((A_k, B_k)\) generates the same MPV family.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 11.3Per-block same MPV implies global same MPV

If \(A_k\) and \(B_k\) generate the same MPV family for each block \(k\), then the block-diagonal tensors \(\bigoplus _k \mu _k A_k\) and \(\bigoplus _k \mu _k B_k\) also generate the same MPV family.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 11.50Block decomposition from adjoint-fixed projections

Let \(A\) be a left-canonical MPS tensor, and let \((P_k)_{k=1}^m\) be orthogonal projections summing to \(\mathbb {1}\) that are fixed by the adjoint transfer map: \(\mathcal{E}_A^\dagger (P_k) = P_k\) for every \(k\). Then the same block decomposition conclusion holds as in Theorem 11.49, including the per-sector trace relation.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 11.49Block decomposition from commuting projections

Let \(A\) be a left-canonical MPS tensor, and let \((P_k)_{k=1}^m\) be orthogonal projections summing to \(\mathbb {1}\) that commute with every Kraus operator: \(P_k A^i = A^i P_k\) for all \(i, k\). Then there exist left-canonical blocks \((C_k)_{k=1}^m\) with unit weights such that \(A\) generates the same MPV family as \(\bigoplus _{k=1}^m C_k\). Moreover, each sector satisfies the per-sector trace relation \(V^{(N)}(C_k)(\sigma ) = \operatorname {tr}(P_k \cdot A^{i_1} \cdots A^{i_N})\) for every word \(\sigma = (i_1, \dots , i_N)\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 11.29Blocking yields a peripheral-spectrum primitive transfer map

Let \(A\) be an irreducible MPS tensor with \(D {\gt} 0\). Assume that \(A\) satisfies the TP normalization \(\sum _i (A^i)^\dagger A^i = \mathbb {1}\). Then there exists a blocking length \(p {\gt} 0\) such that the transfer map \(\mathcal{E}_{A^{[p]}}\) of the blocked tensor \(A^{[p]}\) is primitive, i.e. its peripheral spectrum is \(\{ 1\} \).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 2.32Blocking preserves same MPV

If \(A\) and \(B\) generate the same MPV family, then for any blocking length \(L\), the blocked tensors \(A^{[L]}\) and \(B^{[L]}\) also generate the same MPV family.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 10.38Word eigenvector gives blocked eigenvector

If \(A^{w_0} \varphi = \mu \varphi \) for a word \(w_0\) of length \(L\), then \((A^{[L]})^{j_0} \varphi = \mu \varphi \), where \(j_0\) is the encoded blocked index corresponding to \(w_0\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.80Prop. 7.6, (4)\(\to \)(2)

If the Lindblad data of a GKSL generator are block-upper-triangular with respect to some nontrivial projector \(P\), then there exists a density matrix \(\rho _0\) with nontrivial kernel satisfying \(L(\rho _0) = 0\). This is ( .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 11.69BNT grouping theorem

If equal-norm blocks have equal bond dimensions and represent the same MPS family, then one obtains a sector decomposition whose sector norms are strictly decreasing.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 11.72BNT partition from gauge equivalence

If equal-norm blocks are related by gauge equivalence with unit-modulus phase, the phases can be absorbed into sector weights to obtain a BNT grouping with strictly decreasing sector norms.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 13.7Permutation rigidity for proportional MPV decompositions

Let \((A_j)_{j=1}^{g_A}\) and \((B_k)_{k=1}^{g_B}\) be injective families satisfying the TP normalization \(\sum _i (A_j^i)^\dagger A_j^i = \mathbb {1}\) and \(\sum _i (B_k^i)^\dagger B_k^i = \mathbb {1}\), whose self-overlaps converge to \(1\) and whose cross-overlaps within each family converge to \(0\). Assume moreover that there exist MPS tensors \(A_{\mathrm{tot}}\) and \(B_{\mathrm{tot}}\), coefficient arrays \(a_{N,j}\) and \(b_{N,k}\), and a sequence \(c_N\) with nonzero limits \(a_j^\infty \), \(b_k^\infty \), \(c^\infty \) such that for every \(N\),

\[ |V^{(N)}(A_{\mathrm{tot}})\rangle = \sum _{j=1}^{g_A} a_{N,j} |V^{(N)}(A_j)\rangle , \qquad |V^{(N)}(B_{\mathrm{tot}})\rangle = \sum _{k=1}^{g_B} b_{N,k} |V^{(N)}(B_k)\rangle , \]

with \(a_{N,j} \to a_j^\infty \neq 0\) and \(b_{N,k} \to b_k^\infty \neq 0\), and also

\[ |V^{(N)}(A_{\mathrm{tot}})\rangle = c_N |V^{(N)}(B_{\mathrm{tot}})\rangle \]

with \(c_N \to c^\infty \neq 0\). Then \(g_A = g_B\), and there is a permutation \(\pi \) of the common block index set such that \(A_{\pi (k)}\) and \(B_k\) have the same bond dimension and are gauge-phase equivalent for each \(k\). The canonical-form application of these hypotheses is described in Remark 13.8.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 13.9Permutation rigidity with asymptotically orthonormal overlaps

Let \((A_j)_{j=1}^{g_A}\) and \((B_k)_{k=1}^{g_B}\) be two families of injective tensors satisfying the TP normalization \(\sum _i (A_j^i)^\dagger A_j^i = \mathbb {1}\) and \(\sum _i (B_k^i)^\dagger B_k^i = \mathbb {1}\), whose self-overlaps converge to \(1\) and whose cross-overlaps within each family converge to \(0\). If for every system size \(N\),

\[ \operatorname{span}_{\mathbb {C}}\{ |V^{(N)}(A_j)\rangle : 1 \le j \le g_A\} = \operatorname{span}_{\mathbb {C}}\{ |V^{(N)}(B_k)\rangle : 1 \le k \le g_B\} , \]

then \(g_A = g_B\), and there is a permutation \(\pi \) of the common block index set such that \(A_j\) and \(B_{\pi (j)}\) have the same bond dimension and are gauge-phase equivalent for each \(j\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 11.64Sorted decomposition preserves the represented MPS family

Under the same distinct-norm hypothesis, the sorted block family is gauge equivalent to the original decomposition and has strictly decreasing norms.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 11.65Normal canonical form after sorting

If each block is irreducible, trace-preserving, primitive, and has positive dimension, then sorting by strictly decreasing weight norms produces a normal canonical form.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 11.63Sorted reindexing by strictly decreasing weight norms

If nonzero weights \((\mu _k)_{k=1}^r\) have pairwise distinct norms, then there is a permutation of \(\{ 1,\dots ,r\} \) under which \(\| \mu _k\| \) is strictly decreasing.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 11.66Trivial sector decomposition for the sorted distinct-norm case

If weights are already strictly decreasing in norm, one obtains a sector decomposition with one copy per sector that preserves the represented MPS family.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 11.22Burnside’s theorem for Kraus algebras

Assume \(D {\gt} 0\). If \(A\) acts irreducibly on \(\mathbb {C}^D\), then the generated unital subalgebra is the full matrix algebra:

\[ \operatorname{alg}(A) = M_{D}(\mathbb {C}). \]

This is the complex finite-dimensional case of Burnside’s theorem (equivalently, Jacobson’s density theorem); see  .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 11.27Canonical form from peripheral primitivity

Let \((\mu _k, A_k)_{k=1}^r\) be a collection of block tensors and scaling factors satisfying:

  1. each \(A_k\) is injective,

  2. each \(A_k\) satisfies the TP normalization \(\sum _i (A_k^i)^\dagger A_k^i = \mathbb {1}\),

  3. \(|\mu _1| \ge \cdots \ge |\mu _r|\) (non-increasing),

  4. all \(\mu _k \neq 0\),

  5. each transfer map \(\mathcal{E}_{A_k}\) is primitive, i.e. has peripheral spectrum \(\{ 1\} \).

Then \((\mu _k, A_k)\) satisfies the canonical form conditions (Definition 12.9).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.29Prop. 7.2, direction 1\(\to \)2

If \(L\) is CCP and \(P = \mathbb {1}- |\Omega \rangle \! \langle \Omega |\), then

\[ P((L \otimes \operatorname{id})(|\Omega \rangle \! \langle \Omega |))P \ge 0. \]

This is ( .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.32Prop. 7.3: CCP generator implies CP semigroup

If \(L\) is CCP, then \(e^{tL}\) is CP for all \(t \ge 0\). This is ( .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 5.19Cesàro fixed-point theorem

Every quantum channel \(E : M_{D}(\mathbb {C}) \to M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) (with \(D \ge 1\)) has a nonzero positive semidefinite fixed point: there exists \(\rho \ge 0\), \(\rho \neq 0\), with \(E(\rho ) = \rho \).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 5.17Cesàro telescope

\(E(\sigma _N) - \sigma _N = \frac{1}{N}(E^N(\rho _0) - \rho _0)\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 13.5Canonical form with BNT separation yields a basis of normal tensors

If \(((\mu _k), (A_k))\) is in canonical form with BNT separation, then the block tensors form a basis of normal tensors for the block-diagonal tensor \(\bigoplus _k \mu _k A_k\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 11.35Left-canonical normalization for irreducible TP blocks

Let \(A\) be an irreducible MPS tensor already in TP gauge (\(\sum _i (A^i)^\dagger A^i = \mathbb {1}\)) with \(D {\gt} 0\). Then there exist a unitary \(U\) and a diagonal positive definite matrix \(\Lambda \) such that:

  1. \(B^i := U^\dagger A^i U\) is TP,

  2. \(\mathcal{E}_B(\Lambda ) = \Lambda \),

  3. \(\Lambda \) is diagonal and positive definite,

  4. \(A\) and \(B\) generate the same MPV family.

This is the left-canonical normalization step (Canonical Form II) of ( . The unitary conjugation occurs only after TP normalization has already been achieved; it is a second step inside TP gauge, not a unitary normalization of an arbitrary irreducible block.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 18.32Chain ground space for block-injective tensors

If \(A\) is \(L_0\)-block-injective, \(D \ge 1\), and the window size satisfies \(L \ge 2L_0\), then on the periodic chain with \(N \ge 2\) sites, the chain ground space equals the MPV submodule: \(\mathcal{G}_{N,L}(A) = \operatorname{span}\bigl\{ V^{(N)}(A)\bigr\} \).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 18.33Chain ground space for normal tensors

If \(A\) is normal, \(L_0\)-block-injective, and \(D \ge 1\), the window requirement is reduced from \(2L_0\) to \(L_0 {\lt} L\). The normality hypothesis enables this reduction via the structure theory of normal MPS (peripheral spectrum, canonical form); see Ref.  ( . Concretely, the window range can be reduced from \(2L_0\) to \(L_0 + 1\) via the peripheral-spectrum structure of the transfer map (Theorem 11.32) and the block-separation argument (Theorem 12.16).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 8.22Cesàro convergence for irreducible channels

Let \(E\) be an irreducible quantum channel on \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) with \(D {\gt} 0\), and let \(\rho \) be any density matrix. Then the Cesàro means

\[ \frac{1}{N} \sum _{n=0}^{N-1} E^n(\rho ) \]

converge to the unique positive definite density-matrix fixed point of \(E\). This is ( .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 8.21Unique density-matrix fixed point for an irreducible channel

Let \(E\) be an irreducible quantum channel on \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) with \(D {\gt} 0\). Then there exists a unique density matrix \(\sigma \) such that \(E(\sigma )=\sigma \). Moreover \(\sigma \) is positive definite. This is the qualitative form of ( .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.65Period divides bond dimension

Let \(E\) be as in Theorem 9.64, and let \(\gamma \) be a primitive \(m\)-th root of unity generating the peripheral eigenvalues. Then \(m \mid D\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 5.11Channels preserve density matrices

If \(E\) is a quantum channel, then \(E(\mathcal{D}_D) \subseteq \mathcal{D}_D\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 5.68Thm. 6.1(2): Determinant one iff unitary channel

For a CPTP map \(T : M_{D}(\mathbb {C}) \to M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\),

\[ |\det T| = 1 \; \iff \; \exists \, U \in \mathcal{U}(D),\quad T(\rho ) = U\rho U^\dagger . \]

This is ( .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 5.67Thm. 6.1(1): Determinant bound for positive TP maps

For any positive trace-preserving map \(T : M_{D}(\mathbb {C}) \to M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\), \(|\det T| \le 1\). This is ( .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.30Prop. 7.2, direction 2\(\to \)1

If \(L\) is Hermiticity-preserving and \(P((L \otimes \operatorname{id})(|\Omega \rangle \! \langle \Omega |))P \ge 0\) where \(P = \mathbb {1}- |\Omega \rangle \! \langle \Omega |\), then \(L\) is CCP. This is ( .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 5.46Prop. 2.1: Hermiticity correspondence

The Choi matrix \(\tau \) is Hermitian if and only if \(T(B^\dagger ) = T(B)^\dagger \) for all \(B \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 20.11Cluster-state tensor is not injective

The cluster-state tensor is not injective: \(\operatorname{span}_{\mathbb {C}}\{ A^0, A^1\} = \operatorname{span}_{\mathbb {C}}\{ |+\rangle \! \langle 0|, |-\rangle \! \langle 1|\} \) has dimension \(2\), not \(\dim M_{2}(\mathbb {C}) = 4\).

LaTeX
Theorem 20.13\(\mathbb {Z}_2 \times \mathbb {Z}_2\) on-site symmetry of the blocked cluster-state tensor

The length-\(2\) blocked cluster-state tensor is on-site symmetric under \(\mathbb {Z}_2 \times \mathbb {Z}_2\). The two generators act on the \(4\)-dimensional blocked physical space \((\mathbb {C}^2)^{\otimes 2}\) by \(\sigma _x \otimes I\) and \(I \otimes \sigma _x\), which commute and each square to the identity, defining a linear representation of \(\mathbb {Z}_2 \times \mathbb {Z}_2\). The corresponding virtual matrices are \(V_1 = \sigma _z\) and \(V_2 = \sigma _x\), which anticommute (\(V_1 V_2 = -V_2 V_1\)). This projective phase is the non-trivial element of \(H^2(\mathbb {Z}_2 \times \mathbb {Z}_2, \mathrm{U}(1)) \cong \mathbb {Z}_2\), witnessing a non-trivial SPT phase.

LaTeX
Theorem 17.27Gauge independence of the cocycle class

If the bond dimension satisfies \(D \ge 1\) and two projective representations \(\rho _1,\rho _2\) (with cocycles \(\omega _1,\omega _2\)) both arise as virtual representations of the same injective MPS tensor \(A\) under an on-site symmetry \(U\), then \(\omega _1 \sim \omega _2\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 14.11Eventual coefficient comparison from BNT linear independence

If two sector data \(S, T\) over the same basis, with coefficient arrays \(c_{S,j}^{(N)}\) and \(c_{T,j}^{(N)}\), produce equal total MPVs and the basis is eventually linearly independent (the BNT property), then \(c_{S,j}^{(N)} = c_{T,j}^{(N)}\) for all sufficiently large \(N\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 13.11Coefficient ratio decay

Under the canonical form with BNT separation of Definition 13.2, indexed so that \(\mu _0\) is the (strictly) dominant weight, one has \((\mu _k / \mu _0)^N \to 0\) for every \(k \ge 1\) as \(N \to \infty \).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 17.25Cohomologous-to is an equivalence relation

The relation “cohomologous to” is reflexive, symmetric, and transitive on the set of scalar 2-cocycles.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 11.46Common blocking period for a block family

Let \((B_k)_{k=1}^r\) be a finite family of MPS tensors with positive bond dimensions, each admitting a blocking period \(p_k\) making its transfer map primitive. Then \(p = \operatorname {lcm}(p_1, \ldots , p_r)\) is a common period: \(\mathcal{E}_{B_k^{[p]}}\) is primitive for every \(k\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 14.20Common-period blocking preserves transfer-map primitivity

If each tensor \(B_i\) has a primitive transfer map after blocking by its own positive period \(p_i\), then blocking the entire family to the common LCM period preserves primitivity of each member’s transfer map.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.28Commutator form equals standard Lindblad form

The commutator form (Definition 15.27) defines the same linear map as the standard Lindblad form (Definition 15.23).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 18.51Commuting parent Hamiltonian implies decorrelation

If a subspace \(K\) has a commuting parent Hamiltonian decomposition \(P_K = P_{AX} \circ P_{XB}\) with \([P_{AX}, P_{XB}] = 0\), and \(A\)-observables commute with \(P_{XB}\) while \(B\)-observables commute with \(P_{AX}\), then regions \(A\) and \(B\) are decorrelated w.r.t. \(K\). This is the backward direction of Proposition D.3 from  , Appendix D, §D.2.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 18.50Commuting complementary projectors

If \(P_K = P_{AX} \circ P_{XB}\) is a commuting parent Hamiltonian decomposition and

\[ Q_{AX} = 1 - P_{AX}, \qquad Q_{XB} = 1 - P_{XB}, \]

then

\[ Q_{AX} \circ Q_{XB} = Q_{XB} \circ Q_{AX}. \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 10.48Complement powers tend to zero

If \(A\) is a primitive MPS tensor with PSD fixed point \(\rho \), then \((\mathcal{E}_A - P_\rho )^n \to 0\) in operator norm.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 11.47Compression to a supported projection sector

Let \(A\) be an MPS tensor satisfying \(\sum _i (A^i)^\dagger A^i = P\) for an orthogonal projection \(P\), and suppose \(P A^i P = A^i\) for every \(i\). Then there exists a left-canonical tensor \(C\) of bond dimension \(n = \operatorname{tr}(P)\) with

\[ V^{(N)}(C)_\sigma = \operatorname{tr}\! \bigl(P \cdot A^\sigma \bigr) \]

for every \(\sigma \).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 17.45Local physical intertwining implies transfer-map covariance

If \(V\) and \(u\) are unitary and the local physical intertwining relation holds, then the transfer map is covariant.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 17.43Transfer-map covariance and doubled commutation are equivalent

Transfer-map covariance and commutation in the doubled transfer picture are equivalent for any matrix \(V\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 17.46Transfer-map covariance implies local physical intertwining

For an injective MPS, if \(V\) is unitary and the transfer map is covariant, then there exists a unitary \(u\) satisfying the local physical intertwining relation.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 11.51Conjugate-transposed Kraus family for irreducible TP tensors

Let \(A\) be a left-canonical MPS tensor with irreducible transfer map. Then the conjugate-transposed family \(K_i := (A^i)^\dagger \) is unital and irreducible, and there exists a positive-definite matrix \(\rho \) fixed by the adjoint map associated to \(K\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.8Prop. 7.1: Every continuous semigroup is exponential

Every norm-continuous dynamical semigroup \(T\) on \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) is of the form

\[ T_t = e^{tL} \]

for some generator \(L \in \mathrm{End}_{\mathbb {C}}(M_{D}(\mathbb {C}))\) and every \(t \ge 0\). This is ( .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 19.10Correlation length bound

For an injective trace-preserving MPS tensor, there exist \(C {\gt} 0\) and \(\xi {\gt} 0\) such that for all \(n \ge 0\) and all traceless \(X \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) (i.e. \(\operatorname{tr}(X) = 0\)),

\[ \| \mathcal{E}_A^n(X)\| \; \le \; C\, e^{-n/\xi }\, \| X\| . \]

Traceless matrices lie in \(\ker P\), where \(P\) is the fixed-point projection. Since \(\mathcal{E}_A - P\) has spectral radius strictly less than \(1\), the Gelfand formula gives exponential decay at rate \(\xi = -1/\log \rho (\mathcal{E}_A - P)\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 19.5Exponential decay bound

If \(|\lambda _j| \le |\lambda _2| {\lt} 1\) for all subleading eigenvalues, then there exists \(C_{XY}\ge 0\) such that

\[ |C(X,Y;n)| \le C_{XY}\, |\lambda _2|^n. \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 19.4Sum of exponentials

For a normal MPS, the connected correlator admits a spectral expansion of the form

\[ C(X,Y;n) = \sum _{j=1}^{D^2-1} c_j\, \lambda _j^n, \]

where \(\lambda _j\) are subleading transfer-matrix eigenvalues.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 5.44Prop. 2.1: CP iff Choi matrix is PSD

A linear map \(T : M_{D}(\mathbb {C}) \to M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) is completely positive (in the Kraus sense) if and only if its Choi matrix \(\tau \ge 0\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 5.5CP maps are positive

Every completely positive map is positive.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.33Prop. 7.3: CP semigroup iff CCP generator

\(e^{tL}\) is CP for all \(t \ge 0\) if and only if \(L\) is CCP.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.31Prop. 7.3: CP semigroup implies CCP generator

If \(e^{tL}\) is CP for all \(t \ge 0\), then \(L\) is CCP. This is ( .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 13.4Cross-overlap decay for distinct blocks in canonical form with BNT separation

If \(((\mu _k),(A_k))\) is in canonical form with BNT separation and \(j \neq k\), then \(O_{A_j A_k}(N) \to 0\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 10.57Cumulative span equals word span under aperiodicity

If \(\mathbb {1}\in S_1(A)\), then \(T_n(A) = S_n(A)\) for all \(n\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 10.58Cumulative spanning plus aperiodicity implies normality

If \(T_N(A) = M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) and \(\mathbb {1}\in S_1(A)\), then \(A\) is normal.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 10.12Cumulative span reaches \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\)

If \(A\) is normal, then \(T_{D^2}(A) = M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 10.11Cumulative span reaches \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) — explicit bound

If \(A\) is normal, then there exists \(n \le D^2\) with \(T_n(A) = M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 10.14Cumulative span reaches \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) — sharp bound

If \(A\) is normal, then \(T_{D^2 - d' + 1}(A) = M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\), where \(d' = \dim S_1(A) = \operatorname{kr}(A)\). This is the sharp form of ( : the cumulative span already reaches \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) by index \(D^2 - d' + 1 \le D^2\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 10.5Cumulative span stabilisation

If \(T_n(A) = T_{n+1}(A)\), then \(T_m(A) = T_n(A)\) for all \(m \ge n\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 10.6Dimension growth

If \(T_n(A) \neq T_{n+1}(A)\), then \(\dim T_{n+1}(A) {\gt} \dim T_n(A)\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 11.52Cyclic sector decomposition for irreducible TP tensors

Let \(A\) be a left-canonical MPS tensor with irreducible transfer map. Then there exist \(m \ge 1\) and left-canonical blocks \((C_k)_{k=1}^m\) such that the \(m\)-blocked tensor \(A^{[m]}\) decomposes as \(\bigoplus _{k=1}^m C_k\) (with unit weights).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 5.8Density matrices are compact

\(\mathcal{D}_D\) is compact (in the entry-wise topology on \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\)).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 5.9Density matrices are convex

\(\mathcal{D}_D\) is convex.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 6.4Eigenvalue bound

Each eigenvalue of a density matrix lies in \([0, 1]\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 5.10Density matrices are nonempty

For \(D \ge 1\), \(\mathcal{D}_D \neq \varnothing \).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 12.3Dimension preservation

Assume \(D_k \ge 1\) for all \(k\). If \(T\) is an algebra automorphism of \(\prod _{k=1}^r M_{D_k}(\mathbb {C})\), and \(\pi \) is the induced permutation, then \(D_{\pi (k)} = D_k\) for all \(k\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.9Lem. 7.1: Duhamel formula

Let \(\Delta = L' - L\). For \(t \ge 0\),

\[ e^{tL'} - e^{tL} = \int _0^t e^{(t-s)L} \Delta \, e^{sL'}\, ds. \]

This is ( .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.16Dyson series identity (

For \(t \ge 0\), the Dyson–Phillips series equals the perturbed semigroup:

\[ \sum _{n=0}^{\infty } \widetilde{T}^{(n)}_t = e^{tL'}. \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.14Continuity of Dyson iterates

Each Dyson iterate \(t \mapsto \widetilde{T}^{(n)}_t\) is continuous in the time parameter.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.14Eigenvalue bound

Let \(A, B\) be normalized MPS tensors. Every eigenvalue \(\mu \) of \(F_{AB}\) satisfies \(|\mu | \le 1\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 10.18Eigenvalue extraction

If \(A\) is normal, then there exist a word \(w_0\) with \(|w_0| \le D^2\), a nonzero scalar \(\mu \neq 0\), and a nonzero vector \(\varphi \neq 0\) such that \(A^{w_0} \varphi = \mu \varphi \).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 8.7Uniqueness of positive eigenvalue for irreducible CP maps

Let \(D \ge 1\) and let \(E\) be an irreducible completely positive map on \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\). Suppose \(\rho , \sigma \ge 0\) are nonzero positive semidefinite matrices satisfying \(E(\rho ) = r_1 \rho \) and \(E(\sigma ) = r_2 \sigma \) for real scalars \(r_1, r_2 {\gt} 0\). Then \(r_1 = r_2\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 10.17Nonzero trace implies eigenvector

If \(M \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) has \(\operatorname{tr}(M) \neq 0\), then \(M\) has a nonzero eigenvalue \(\mu \neq 0\) and a corresponding eigenvector \(\varphi \neq 0\) with \(M \varphi = \mu \varphi \).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 10.22Eigenvector spreading

Let \(A\) be a normal MPS tensor and let \(A^{i_0} \varphi = \mu \varphi \) with \(\mu \neq 0\) and \(\varphi \neq 0\). Then \(K_{D-1}(A, \varphi ) = \mathbb {C}^D\). This is ( .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 6.5Entropy upper bound

For a density matrix \(\rho \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) with \(D \ge 1\),

\[ S(\rho ) \le \log D. \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 6.2Entropy is non-negative

For any density matrix \(\rho \), \(S(\rho ) \ge 0\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 11.81Zero-word evaluation for the zero tensor

Every nonempty finite word evaluates to zero when all Kraus operators are zero.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 11.37Normal canonical form from a primitive block decomposition

Suppose \(A\) generates the same MPV family as a weighted block-diagonal tensor \(\bigoplus _k \mu _k A_k\), and assume that:

  1. each \(A_k\) is irreducible,

  2. each \(A_k\) satisfies the TP normalization \(\sum _i (A_k^i)^\dagger A_k^i = \mathbb {1}\),

  3. each transfer map \(\mathcal{E}_{A_k}\) is primitive (equivalently, has peripheral spectrum \(\{ 1\} \)),

  4. the moduli \(|\mu _k|\) are pairwise distinct,

  5. all \(\mu _k \neq 0\),

  6. all bond dimensions are positive.

Then there exist a blocking length \(p {\gt} 0\), weights \((\nu _k)\), and blocked tensors \((B_k)\) such that \(A^{[p]}\) generates the same MPV family as \(\bigoplus _k \nu _k B_k\), and \((\nu _k, B_k)\) satisfies the normal canonical form conditions.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.34Traceless Kraus operators exist

Assume \(d {\gt} 0\). Given any Kraus operators \(\{ K_j\} \), there exist \(c_j \in \mathbb {C}\) such that \(K_j' = K_j + c_j \mathbb {1}\) is traceless. This is part of ( .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 8.20Exponential positivity for irreducible CP maps

Let \(E\) be an irreducible completely positive map on \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\), let \(A \ge 0\) be nonzero, and let \(t {\gt} 0\). Then

\[ \exp (tE)(A) = \sum _{k=0}^{\infty } \frac{t^k}{k!} E^k(A) \]

is positive definite. This is ( .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 8.19Exponential truncation is positive definite

Let \(E\) be an irreducible completely positive map on \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\), let \(A \ge 0\) be nonzero, and let \(t {\gt} 0\). Then the finite exponential truncation

\[ \sum _{k=0}^{D-1} \frac{t^k}{k!} E^k(A) \]

is positive definite. This is the finite-sum core of ( .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 19.9Exponential convergence of injective primitive channels

Fix an injective, trace-preserving MPS tensor \(A\) with positive definite fixed point \(\rho \). Let

\[ P(X) = \frac{\operatorname{tr}(X)}{\operatorname{tr}(\rho )}\rho \]

be the corresponding fixed-point projection. Then there exist \(C {\gt} 0\) and \(0 {\lt} \delta \le 1\) such that for all \(n \ge 0\) and \(X \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\),

\[ \| \mathcal{E}_A^n(X) - P(X)\| \; \le \; C\, (1-\delta )^n\, \| X\| . \]

The spectral gap \(\delta \) exists by primitivity (Theorem 5.39, the complementary spectral gap).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.4Semigroup law for the exponential

For all \(t,s \in \mathbb {R}\), \(e^{(t+s)L} = e^{tL} \cdot e^{sL}\).

LaTeX
Lean
  • expSemigroupCLM_add
  • expSemigroup_comp
Theorem 15.5Continuity of the exponential semigroup

The map \(t \mapsto e^{tL}\) is continuous in the operator norm topology.

LaTeX
Lean
  • expSemigroupCLM_continuous
  • expSemigroup_isContinuousDynSemigroup
Theorem 10.9Fitting decomposition exists

Every linear endomorphism on a finite-dimensional vector space over an algebraically closed field admits a Fitting decomposition.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 10.47Fixed-point uniqueness from spectral gap

If \(A\) is a primitive MPS tensor with PSD fixed point \(\rho \), then every fixed point \(\sigma \) of \(\mathcal{E}_A\) is proportional to \(\rho \): \(\sigma = \frac{\operatorname{tr}(\sigma )}{\operatorname{tr}(\rho )} \rho \).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.53Fixed-point algebra is semisimple

The adjoint-fixed-point \(*\)-subalgebra of a trace-preserving Kraus map on \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) is a semisimple ring.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.54Abstract Wedderburn–Artin decomposition (Wolf Thm. 6.14)

There exist \(n\in \mathbb {N}\) and dimensions \(d_1,\dots ,d_n\ge 1\) such that the adjoint-fixed-point \(*\)-subalgebra is \(\mathbb {C}\)-algebra isomorphic to \(\prod _{k=1}^{n} M_{d_k}(\mathbb {C})\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 5.70Thm. 6.12: Fixed points form a \(*\)-subalgebra

Let \(E\) be a unital Kraus map whose adjoint map \(E^*\) has a positive definite fixed point \(\rho {\gt} 0\). Then \(\mathrm{Fix}(E)\) is a \(*\)-subalgebra of \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\). This is ( .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 11.17Unitary diagonal positive fixed point in TP gauge

Let \(A\) be an irreducible MPS tensor satisfying \(\sum _i (A^i)^\dagger A^i = \mathbb {1}\), and assume \(D {\gt} 0\). Then there exist a unitary \(U\) and a diagonal positive definite matrix \(\Lambda \) such that, for \(B^i := U^\dagger A^i U\),

\[ \sum _i (B^i)^\dagger B^i = \mathbb {1}\qquad \text{and}\qquad \mathcal{E}_B(\Lambda ) = \Lambda . \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 11.13PSD fixed point gives invariant projection

Let \(\rho \ge 0\) be a fixed point of \(\mathcal{E}_A\), and let \(P\) be the support projection of \(\rho \). Then for all \(i\), \((\mathbb {1}- P) A^i P = 0\): the range of \(P\) is invariant under all \(A^i\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 12.18Canonical form fundamental theorem, same-structure version

Under the hypotheses of Theorem 12.16, each pair \((A_k, B_k)\) is gauge equivalent. Hence the block-diagonal tensors \(\bigoplus _k \mu _k A_k\) and \(\bigoplus _k \mu _k B_k\) are gauge equivalent.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 14.5Fundamental Theorem — equal MPV case

Let \(A_{\mathrm{tot}}\) and \(B_{\mathrm{tot}}\) be MPS tensors in canonical form with BNT separation, with associated families \((\mu _j^A, A_j)_{j=1}^{g_A}\) and \((\mu _k^B, B_k)_{k=1}^{g_B}\). If

\[ V^{(N)}(A_{\mathrm{tot}})_\sigma = V^{(N)}(B_{\mathrm{tot}})_\sigma \]

for every \(N\) and \(\sigma \), then the block-diagonal tensors \(\bigoplus _{j=1}^{g_A} \mu _j^A A_j\) and \(\bigoplus _{k=1}^{g_B} \mu _k^B B_k\) are gauge equivalent.

LaTeX
Theorem 14.6Fundamental Theorem — equal MPV case with explicit coefficients

Let \(A_{\mathrm{tot}}\) and \(B_{\mathrm{tot}}\) be MPS tensors in canonical form with BNT separation, with associated families \((\mu _j^A, A_j)_{j=1}^{g_A}\) and \((\mu _k^B, B_k)_{k=1}^{g_B}\). Suppose there exist coefficient arrays \(a_{N,j}\), \(b_{N,k}\) and nonzero limits \(a_j^\infty \), \(b_k^\infty \) such that

\[ V^{(N)}(A_{\mathrm{tot}})_\sigma = \sum _{j=1}^{g_A} a_{N,j} V^{(N)}(A_j)_\sigma , \qquad V^{(N)}(B_{\mathrm{tot}})_\sigma = \sum _{k=1}^{g_B} b_{N,k} V^{(N)}(B_k)_\sigma , \]

with \(a_{N,j} \to a_j^\infty \neq 0\) and \(b_{N,k} \to b_k^\infty \neq 0\). If

\[ V^{(N)}(A_{\mathrm{tot}})_\sigma = V^{(N)}(B_{\mathrm{tot}})_\sigma \]

for every \(N\) and \(\sigma \), then \(g_A = g_B\), and there is a permutation \(\pi \) such that \(D_j^A = D_{\pi (j)}^B\) and, after reindexing the \(B\)-blocks by \(\pi \), the weighted block-diagonal tensors \(\bigoplus _{j=1}^{g_A} \mu _j^A A_j\) and \(\bigoplus _{j=1}^{g_A} \mu _{\pi (j)}^B B_{\pi (j)}\) are gauge equivalent.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 14.8Fundamental Theorem — equal MPV case, common block structure

Fix a common block structure: the same number of blocks \(g\), the same bond dimensions \((D_k)_{k=1}^g\), and the same weights \((\mu _k)_{k=1}^g\). Let \((\mu _k, A_k)_{k=1}^g\) and \((\mu _k, B_k)_{k=1}^g\) be two families in canonical form with BNT separation on this common data. If the block-diagonal tensors \(\bigoplus _k \mu _k A_k\) and \(\bigoplus _k \mu _k B_k\) generate the same MPV family, then each pair \((A_k, B_k)\) is gauge equivalent, and hence the corresponding block-diagonal tensors are gauge equivalent.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 14.2Fundamental Theorem — proportional MPV case

Let \(A_{\mathrm{tot}}\) and \(B_{\mathrm{tot}}\) be MPS tensors in canonical form with BNT separation, with associated families \((\mu _j^A, A_j)_{j=1}^{g_A}\) and \((\mu _k^B, B_k)_{k=1}^{g_B}\). Suppose there exist coefficient arrays \(a_{N,j}\), \(b_{N,k}\) and nonzero limits \(a_j^\infty \), \(b_k^\infty \), \(c^\infty \) such that

\[ V^{(N)}(A_{\mathrm{tot}})_\sigma = \sum _{j=1}^{g_A} a_{N,j} V^{(N)}(A_j)_\sigma , \qquad V^{(N)}(B_{\mathrm{tot}})_\sigma = \sum _{k=1}^{g_B} b_{N,k} V^{(N)}(B_k)_\sigma , \]

with \(a_{N,j} \to a_j^\infty \neq 0\) and \(b_{N,k} \to b_k^\infty \neq 0\), and suppose also that

\[ V^{(N)}(A_{\mathrm{tot}})_\sigma = c_N V^{(N)}(B_{\mathrm{tot}})_\sigma \]

for a sequence \(c_N \to c^\infty \neq 0\). Then \(g_A = g_B\), and there is a permutation \(\pi \) such that \(D_j^A = D_{\pi (j)}^B\) and \(A_j\) is gauge-phase equivalent to \(B_{\pi (j)}\) for each \(j\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 14.4Fundamental Theorem for normal canonical form in the proportional case

Let \(A_{\mathrm{tot}}\) and \(B_{\mathrm{tot}}\) be MPS tensors in normal canonical form with BNT separation, with associated families \((\mu _j^A, A_j)_{j=1}^{g_A}\) and \((\mu _k^B, B_k)_{k=1}^{g_B}\). Suppose there exist coefficient arrays \(a_{N,j}\), \(b_{N,k}\) and nonzero limits \(a_j^\infty \), \(b_k^\infty \), \(c^\infty \) such that

\[ V^{(N)}(A_{\mathrm{tot}})_\sigma = \sum _{j=1}^{g_A} a_{N,j} V^{(N)}(A_j)_\sigma , \qquad V^{(N)}(B_{\mathrm{tot}})_\sigma = \sum _{k=1}^{g_B} b_{N,k} V^{(N)}(B_k)_\sigma , \]

with \(a_{N,j} \to a_j^\infty \neq 0\) and \(b_{N,k} \to b_k^\infty \neq 0\), and suppose also that

\[ V^{(N)}(A_{\mathrm{tot}})_\sigma = c_N V^{(N)}(B_{\mathrm{tot}})_\sigma \]

for a sequence \(c_N \to c^\infty \neq 0\). Then \(g_A = g_B\), and there is a permutation \(\pi \) such that \(D_j^A = D_{\pi (j)}^B\) and \(A_j\) is gauge-phase equivalent to \(B_{\pi (j)}\) for each \(j\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 4.11Single-block Fundamental Theorem

Let \(A\) be an injective MPV tensor and let \(B\) be an MPV tensor of the same bond dimension. If \(A\) and \(B\) generate the same MPV family, then they are gauge equivalent: there exists \(X \in \mathrm{GL}_D(\mathbb {C})\) such that \(B^i = X A^i X^{-1}\) for all \(i\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 16.18Strengthened single-block FT (finite-length)

Let \(A\) be an injective tensor of bond dimension \(D \ge 1\), and let \(B\) be a tensor of the same bond dimension. If \(A\) and \(B\) agree on all MPV coefficients for system sizes \(N \ge N_0\) (for any threshold \(N_0\)), then \(A\) and \(B\) are gauge equivalent.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.87Full algebra generation forbids block-upper-triangular form

Let \((H, \{ L_j\} )\) be a Lindblad form. If the algebra generated by \(\{ L_j\} \) and \(\kappa \) is the entire matrix algebra \(M_d(\mathbb {C})\), then the Lindblad data do not admit a block-upper-triangular decomposition.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 16.35Fundamental Theorem from canonical form

Under the hypotheses of Theorem 16.34, every block pair \(A_k, B_k\) is gauge equivalent, and the block-diagonal tensors are globally gauge equivalent.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 16.36Explicit gauge from canonical form

Under the same hypotheses, there exist invertible matrices \(X_k \in \mathrm{GL}_{D_k}(\mathbb {C})\) such that \(B_k^i = X_k\, A_k^i\, X_k^{-1}\) for every block \(k\) and physical index \(i\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 16.13Fundamental Theorem for injective chains – Theorem 1 of

Let \(\mathbf{A} = (A_0, \ldots , A_{n-1})\) and \(\mathbf{B} = (B_0, \ldots , B_{n-1})\) be two injective non-TI periodic chains of length \(n \ge 3\) that generate the same chain state. Then \(\mathbf{A}\) and \(\mathbf{B}\) are cyclically gauge equivalent: there exist invertible matrices \(Z_0, \ldots , Z_{n-1} \in \mathrm{GL}_D(\mathbb {C})\), unique up to a common nonzero scalar factor, such that

\[ B_k^i = Z_k^{-1}\, A_k^i\, Z_{k+1} \]

for every site \(k \in \{ 0, \ldots , n-1\} \) and every physical index \(i\), with \(Z_n = Z_0\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 16.30Multi-block Fundamental Theorem

Let \(\{ A_k\} _{k=0}^{r-1}\) and \(\{ B_k\} _{k=0}^{r-1}\) be two families of MPS tensors with block dimensions \(D_k\), each \(A_k\) injective, and \(\mathcal{V}(A_k) = \mathcal{V}(B_k)\) for all \(k\). Then:

  1. Per-block gauge equivalence: \(B_k^i = X_k\, A_k^i\, X_k^{-1}\) for some \(X_k \in \mathrm{GL}_{D_k}(\mathbb {C})\), for every \(k\) and \(i\).

  2. Global gauge equivalence: the block-diagonal tensors \(\bigoplus _k \mu _k A_k\) and \(\bigoplus _k \mu _k B_k\) are gauge equivalent.

The second clause is obtained from the first by assembling the per-block gauges into one block-diagonal gauge.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 16.51Fundamental Theorem for injective PEPS

If two PEPS tensors on a simple graph are both vertex-injective and generate the same state, then they are gauge-equivalent (Theorem 2 of  ).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 11.70Gauge equivalence from non-decaying cross-overlap

For trace-preserving irreducible blocks, a non-decaying cross-overlap forces equal bond dimensions and gauge equivalence up to a unit-modulus phase.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 2.19Gauge invariance of MPVs

If \(A\) and \(B\) are gauge equivalent, then they generate the same MPV family.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 2.20Gauge-phase scaling of MPVs

If

\[ B^i = \zeta \, X \, A^i \, X^{-1} \qquad \text{for all } i, \]

then for every system size \(N\) and configuration \(\sigma \) one has

\[ V^{(N)}(B)_\sigma = \zeta ^N V^{(N)}(A)_\sigma . \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 16.52Gauge uniqueness up to scalar

On a connected graph, the gauge family in the Fundamental Theorem is unique up to a global multiplicative scalar.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 16.50Gauge consistency

Local gauges extracted at adjacent vertices are consistent across shared edges, yielding a global gauge family. On a shared edge, the two neighbouring local extractions match as one edge gauge:

\[ \begin{array}{c} \text{local gauges extracted at } u \text{ and } w \text{ agree on the shared edge } e \\ u \; \text{---} \; X_e \; \text{---} \; (X_e^{-1})^\top \; \text{---} \; w \\ \text{and define one global edge gauge family} \end{array} \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.17Gauged peripheral eigenvector yields Kraus intertwining

Let \(A\) and \(B\) be normalized tensors, and let \(X \neq 0\) satisfy \(F_{AB}(X) = \mu X\) with \(|\mu | = 1\). Choose positive-definite fixed points \(\rho _A,\rho _B\) of the transfer maps and gauge to unital families \(A'^i = S_A^{-1} A^i S_A\) and \(B'^i = S_B^{-1} B^i S_B\) with \(S_A S_A^\dagger = \rho _A\) and \(S_B S_B^\dagger = \rho _B\). Then the transported matrix \(X' = S_A^{-1} X (S_B^\dagger )^{-1}\) is nonzero, the families \(A'\) and \(B'\) are unital, and for every \(i\) one has

\[ X' (B'^i)^\dagger = \mu \, (A'^i)^\dagger X', \qquad A'^i X' = \mu \, X' B'^i. \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.18Intertwining yields gauge-phase equivalence

Let \(A\) and \(B\) be tensors of the same bond dimension. Suppose there are invertible gauges taking them to families \(A'\) and \(B'\), an invertible matrix \(X'\), and a scalar \(\mu \) with \(|\mu | = 1\) such that

\[ A'^i X' = \mu \, X' B'^i \qquad \text{for all } i. \]

Then \(A\) and \(B\) are gauge-phase equivalent.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 16.47Gauge equivalence implies same state

Gauge equivalence implies that the two PEPS tensors represent the same state.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 16.41Projective multiplication law for gauge matrices

Let \(A\) be an injective MPS tensor with on-site symmetry under a group representation \(U : G \to \mathrm{GL}_d(\mathbb {C})\). For each \(g \in G\), let \(X(g) \in \mathrm{GL}_D(\mathbb {C})\) satisfy \(\widetilde{A}_g^i = X(g)\, A^i\, X(g)^{-1}\) for all \(i\). Then for all \(g, h \in G\), there exists \(c(g,h) \in \mathbb {C}\) such that

\[ X(h)\, X(g) = c(g,h)\, X(g \cdot h). \]

In fact \(c(g,h) \ne 0\) since both sides are invertible, so \(c(g,h) \in \mathbb {C}^{\times }\). Diagrammatically, the two candidate gauges are competing local replacements for the same twisted tensor:

\[ \begin{array}{ccccccccccc}& & i & & & & & & i & & \\ & & \vert & & & & & & \vert & & \\ \bullet & \text{---} & \textcolor{red}{\bullet } & \text{---} & \bullet & = & \bullet & \text{---} & \textcolor{red}{\bullet } & \text{---} & \bullet \\ & & X(h)\, X(g) & & & & & & X(gh) & & \end{array} \]

and both sides conjugate \(A^i\) to the same twisted tensor \(\widetilde{A}_{gh}^i\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.35Shift invariance of generators

If \(K_j' = K_j + c_j \mathbb {1}\) and \(\kappa '\) is adjusted per ( , then \((\phi ', \kappa ')\) and \((\phi , \kappa )\) define the same generator.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.7Generator uniqueness

If \(e^{tL} = e^{tL'}\) for all \(t \ge 0\), then \(L = L'\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.44Thm. 7.1, Form (i) conversely

If \(L\) is a GKSL generator, then there exist a CP map \(\phi \) and a matrix \(\kappa \) such that

\[ L(\rho ) = \phi (\rho ) - \kappa \rho - \rho \kappa ^\dagger \]

and \(\phi ^*(\mathbb {1}) = \kappa + \kappa ^\dagger \).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.39Prop. 7.4, item 2: combined uniqueness

If two Lindblad forms \(F, F'\) induce the same generator and both have traceless Kraus operators, then \(\phi = \phi '\) and \(\kappa ' = \kappa + i\lambda \, \mathbb {1}\) for some \(\lambda \in \mathbb {R}\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.38Prop. 7.4, item 2: drift uniqueness modulo phase

If two Lindblad forms \(F, F'\) induce the same generator and both have traceless Kraus operators, then their drift matrices differ by an imaginary scalar: \(\kappa ' = \kappa + i\lambda \, \mathbb {1}\) for some \(\lambda \in \mathbb {R}\). This is part of ( .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.37Prop. 7.4, item 2: CP-part uniqueness

If two Lindblad forms \(F, F'\) induce the same generator and both have traceless Kraus operators, then their CP parts agree: \(\phi = \phi '\). This is part of ( .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 14.12Geometric sequence extrapolation

If two finite families of nonzero complex numbers have equal power sums \(\sum _i \alpha _i^k = \sum _j \beta _j^k\) for all \(k {\gt} N_0\), then equality holds for all \(k \geq 0\). The proof uses induction on the number of terms with a telescoping argument: subtract a geometric factor to reduce the number of terms, apply the induction hypothesis, and conclude via a geometric recurrence.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 20.3GHZ tensor is an RFP

The GHZ tensor is a renormalization fixed point.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 20.4GHZ tensor is not injective

The GHZ tensor is not injective: \(\operatorname{span}_{\mathbb {C}}\{ A^0, A^1\} \) is the two-dimensional space of diagonal matrices in \(M_{2}(\mathbb {C})\), which does not equal \(M_{2}(\mathbb {C})\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 20.2GHZ transfer map is idempotent

The transfer map of the GHZ tensor satisfies \(\mathcal{E}_A^2 = \mathcal{E}_A\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 20.5\(\mathbb {Z}_2\) on-site symmetry of the GHZ tensor

Let \(\mathbb {Z}_2 = \{ 1, \sigma _x\} \) act on \(\mathbb {C}^2\) by the Pauli-\(X\) matrix \(\sigma _x\). The GHZ tensor is on-site symmetric under this action: for each \(g \in \mathbb {Z}_2\), the twisted tensor \(\widetilde A_g\) defines the same MPV family as \(A\), i.e. \(\mathcal{V}(A)=\mathcal{V}(\widetilde A_g)\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.45Thm. 7.1: GKSL iff CCP + trace-annihilating

\(L\) is a GKSL generator if and only if \(L\) is CCP and trace-annihilating.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.46Thm. 7.1: GKSL iff Lindblad form

\(L\) is a GKSL generator if and only if

\[ L(\rho ) = i[\rho , H] + \sum _j \Bigl( L_j \rho L_j^\dagger - \tfrac {1}{2}\{ L_j^\dagger L_j, \rho \} _+ \Bigr) \]

with \(H = H^\dagger \). This is ( .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.43Thm. 7.1, Form (i)

If \(L(\rho ) = \phi (\rho ) - \kappa \rho - \rho \kappa ^\dagger \) with \(\phi \) CP and \(\phi ^*(\mathbb {1}) = \kappa + \kappa ^\dagger \), then \(L\) is a GKSL generator. This is ( .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 18.22Ground-space dimension

If \(A\) is injective and \(L \ge 1\), then \(\dim G_L(A) = D^2\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 18.24Intersection characterization

For injective \(A\) and \(L \ge 2\): \(\psi \in G_{L+1}(A) \iff \psi \in G_L^{\mathrm{left}} \cap G_L^{\mathrm{right}}\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 18.23Intersection property

If \(A\) is injective and \(L \ge 2\), then a state \(\psi \) on \(L+1\) sites satisfying both the left and right ground conditions lies in \(G_{L+1}(A)\):

\[ \psi \in G_L^{\mathrm{left}} \cap G_L^{\mathrm{right}} \Longrightarrow \psi \in G_{L+1}(A). \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 18.21Ground-space map injectivity

If \(A\) is injective and \(L \ge 1\), then \(\Gamma _L\) is injective.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 18.34Unique ground state on the periodic chain

For an injective tensor \(A\) with \(D \ge 1\) on a periodic chain of \(N \ge L\) sites with interaction range \(L \ge 2\), the chain ground space is one-dimensional, spanned by the MPV.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 18.61Ground space equals span of BNT

Let \(A = \bigoplus _{k=1}^g \mu _k A_k\) be in canonical form with BNT separation (Definition 13.3), where \(\{ A_1,\ldots ,A_g\} \) is a basis of normal tensors (Definition 13.1). For \(N \ge 2(L_0 + 1)\) (where \(L_0\) is the maximum injectivity length of the blocks), the ground space of the parent Hamiltonian satisfies

\[ \ker (H_N(A,L_0{+}1)) = \operatorname{span}\bigl\{ V^{(N)}(A_k) : k = 1,\ldots ,g\bigr\} . \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 18.54Commuting parent Hamiltonian identity

If \(P_K = P_{AX} \circ P_{XB}\) is a commuting parent Hamiltonian decomposition, then

\[ Q_{AX} + Q_{XB} - Q_{AX} \circ Q_{XB} = 1 - P_K, \]

where \(Q_{AX} = 1 - P_{AX}\) and \(Q_{XB} = 1 - P_{XB}\) are the complementary projectors. This is the frustration-free form of the parent Hamiltonian from , Appendix D, §D.2.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 18.52Commuting parent Hamiltonian implies decorrelation

Corollary of Theorem 18.51: if \(P_K\) has a commuting parent Hamiltonian decomposition and observables respect locality, then regions \(A\) and \(B\) are decorrelated w.r.t. \(K\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 18.43Idempotence gives a trivial commuting parent Hamiltonian

If

\[ P_K \circ P_K = P_K, \]

then \(P_K\) has a commuting parent Hamiltonian structure.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 8.26Irreducibility gives the spectral properties

Every nonzero irreducible completely positive map on \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) has the spectral properties of Definition 8.25.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 17.52String order holds for injective symmetric MPS

Let \(A\) be an injective tensor, symmetric under a unitary representation \(U\). Assume \(\mathcal{E}_A(\mathbb {1})=\mathbb {1}\), \(\Lambda \) is positive definite, \(\operatorname{tr}(\Lambda )=1\), and \(\mathcal{E}_A^\dagger (\Lambda )=\Lambda \). Then string order exists for every group element \(g\): the twisted transfer map \(\mathcal{E}_{U(g)}\) has the virtual gauge matrix as a fixed point with eigenvalue \(1\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 18.29One-dimensionality criterion

For finite-dimensional \(S\), \(\dim S = 1\) iff there exists a nonzero \(\psi _0 \in S\) such that every \(\psi \in S\) is of the form \(c\, \psi _0\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.6Derivative of the exponential semigroup

For all \(t \in \mathbb {R}\),

\[ \frac{d}{dt} e^{tL} = e^{tL} \cdot L. \]

This is ( .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.88Hermitian span + trivial commutant forbids block-upper-triangular form

If the Lindblad span is closed under Hermitian conjugation and its commutant is \(\mathbb {C}\cdot \mathbb {1}\), then the Lindblad data do not admit a block-upper-triangular decomposition.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 5.18Hermitian fixed-point decomposition

Let \(E\) be a quantum channel and \(X = E(X)\) a Hermitian fixed point with decomposition \(X = P_+ - P_-\) into orthogonal positive and negative parts (\(P_\pm \ge 0\), \(\operatorname{tr}(P_+ P_-) = 0\)). Then \(E(P_+) = P_+\) and \(E(P_-) = P_-\). This is ( .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 7.18Hilbert–Schmidt contraction

If a Kraus map is both unital and trace-preserving, then \(\operatorname{tr}(E(X)^\dagger E(X)) \le \operatorname{tr}(X^\dagger X)\) for all \(X \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 5.13Injectivity implies irreducibility

If \(A\) is an injective MPS tensor, then its transfer map \(\mathcal{E}_A\) is irreducible.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 11.5Scaling preserves injectivity

If \(A\) is injective and \(\zeta \neq 0\), then \(\zeta A\) is injective.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 11.85Irreducible block decomposition with trace-preserving gauge

After removing a trivial block, one obtains a block decomposition into irreducible blocks with a trace-preserving gauge.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 11.16Irreducible block decomposition

Every MPS tensor \(A\) admits a block-diagonal tensor \(\bigoplus _{k=1}^r A_k\) where each \(A_k\) is irreducible and \(\mathcal{V}(A) = \mathcal{V}\! \bigl(\bigoplus _k A_k\bigr)\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 11.34Irreducible block decomposition

Every MPS tensor \(A\) generates the same MPV family as a block-diagonal tensor \(\bigoplus _{k=1}^r A_k\) with each block irreducible.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 8.27Spectral properties imply irreducibility

If a completely positive map on \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) has the spectral properties of Definition 8.25, then it is irreducible.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 8.28Irreducibility iff Wolf’s spectral properties

Let \(E\) be a nonzero completely positive map on \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\). Then \(E\) is irreducible if and only if it has the spectral properties of Definition 8.25. This is ( .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 8.14Scaling preserves irreducibility

If \(E\) is an irreducible map and \(c \neq 0\), then \(c E\) is also irreducible. This is the scalar special case (\(C = \mathbb {1}\)) of ( .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 10.60Irreducible tensor plus aperiodicity implies normality

If \(A\) is an irreducible tensor, \(D {\gt} 0\), and \(\mathbb {1}\in S_1(A)\), then \(A\) is normal. This assembles the full chain:

\[ \text{irreducible tensor} \; \xrightarrow {\ref{thm:irreducible_tensor_to_action}}\; \text{irreducible action} \; \xrightarrow {\text{Burnside}}\; \operatorname{alg}(A) = M_{D}(\mathbb {C}) \; \xrightarrow {\ref{thm:algspan_to_normal}}\; \text{normal}. \]

This corresponds to the irreducibility-to-normality direction of ( . For the Burnside step, we appeal forward to Theorems 11.21 and 11.22 in Chapter 11; this is a presentation-level forward dependency, but the mathematical dependency is acyclic.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 11.24Irreducible action implies eventual cumulative spanning

Assume \(D {\gt} 0\). If \(A\) acts irreducibly on \(\mathbb {C}^D\), then there exists \(N\) such that \(T_N(A) = M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 17.9Irreducible form preserved by physical rotation

If \(u u^\dagger = I\) and \(A\) is in irreducible form II, then \(A_u\) is in irreducible form II with the same block structure and rotated blocks.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 10.51Primitive MPS with positive-definite fixed point has irreducible transfer map

If \(A\) is a primitive MPS tensor with positive-definite fixed point \(\rho \), then the transfer map \(\mathcal{E}_A\) is irreducible.

The proof uses fixed-point uniqueness (Theorem 10.47): every PSD fixed point is proportional to \(\rho \), so Wolf’s criterion for irreducibility (positive-definite fixed point implies irreducibility) applies directly.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.69Prop. 7.5: Irreducible semigroup implies primitive

Let \(T_t = e^{tL}\) be a quantum dynamical semigroup. If \(T_{t_0}\) is irreducible for some \(t_0 {\gt} 0\), then \(T_t\) is primitive (and irreducible) for every \(t {\gt} 0\). This is ( .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 17.5Irreducibility preserved by physical rotation

If \(u u^\dagger = I\) and \(A\) is irreducible, then \(A_u\) is irreducible.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 11.21Irreducible tensor implies irreducible action

If \(A\) is irreducible as a tensor (Definition 11.15), then the Kraus operators act irreducibly on \(\mathbb {C}^D\) (Definition 11.20).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 3.18MPDO RFP iff MPO ZCL

The provisional MPDO renormalization fixed-point condition is equivalent to the zero-correlation-length condition for MPO tensors.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 3.19MPDO RFP iff doubled-index MPS RFP

The provisional MPDO RFP condition is equivalent to the pure-state RFP condition for the doubled-index MPS tensor.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 19.25CID implies RFP

If an MPS tensor admits a positive definite right fixed point of its transfer map and has correlations independent of distance, then its transfer map is idempotent. This is the reverse direction of ( .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 10.36Blocking preserves normality

If \(A\) is normal and \(L {\gt} 0\), then the blocked tensor \(A^{[L]}\) is also normal.

LaTeX Lean
