Theorem 11.45Iterated blocking multiplies periods at the transfer-map level

For any MPS tensor \(A\) and integers \(m,n \ge 0\),

\[ \mathcal{E}_{(A^{[m]})^{[n]}} = \mathcal{E}_{A^{[mn]}}. \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 7.5Kadison–Schwarz inequality

Let \(E(X) = \sum _i K_i X K_i^\dagger \) be a unital Kraus map (i.e. \(\sum _i K_i K_i^\dagger = \mathbb {1}\)). Then for all \(X \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\),

\[ E(X^\dagger X) \ge E(X)^\dagger E(X). \]

This is ( .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 7.6Kadison–Schwarz for the adjoint channel

If \(\sum _i K_i^\dagger K_i = \mathbb {1}\) (trace-preserving), then the adjoint Kraus map satisfies \(E^*(X^\dagger X) \ge E^*(X)^\dagger E^*(X)\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.86\(\kappa \) block vanishing from generator compression

Let \((H, \{ L_j\} )\) be a Lindblad form with \(\kappa = iH + \tfrac {1}{2}\sum _j L_j^\dagger L_j\), and let \(P\) be an orthogonal projection such that the generator preserves the compression \(P M_d(\mathbb {C}) P\). If \((1-P) L_j P = 0\) for every \(j\), then \((1-P) \kappa P = 0\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.48Kossakowski form iff Lindblad form

A linear map admits a Kossakowski form iff it admits a Lindblad form.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 7.23KS equality implies Kraus commutation

Let \(E\) be a unital Kraus map. If \(E(X^\dagger X) = E(X)^\dagger E(X)\) for some \(X\), then \(X K_i^\dagger = K_i^\dagger E(X)\) for all \(i\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 5.53Thm. 2.1: Equal Stinespring Gramians

If two Kraus families define the same CPM, then \(\sum _\alpha B_\alpha ^\dagger B_\alpha = \sum _j A_j^\dagger A_j\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 5.52Thm. 2.1: Dual map equality from primal map equality

If two Kraus families satisfy \(\sum _\alpha B_\alpha X B_\alpha ^\dagger = \sum _j A_j X A_j^\dagger \) for all \(X\), then \(\sum _\alpha B_\alpha ^\dagger Y B_\alpha = \sum _j A_j^\dagger Y A_j\) for all \(Y\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 19.14Kraus-isometry criterion for RFP

Suppose there exists an isometry \(V : \mathbb {C}^d \to \mathbb {C}^{d^2}\), written as coefficients \(V_{(i_1,i_2),j}\), such that

\[ A^{i_1} A^{i_2} = \sum _{j=0}^{d-1} V_{(i_1,i_2),j} A^j \]

for all \(i_1,i_2 \in \{ 0,\ldots ,d-1\} \). Then \(A\) is a renormalization fixed point. This is the backward direction of ( .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 5.54Thm. 2.1 item 4: Rectangular Kraus freedom (necessary direction)

If \(\sum _\alpha B_\alpha X B_\alpha ^\dagger = \sum _j A_j X A_j^\dagger \) for all \(X\) and \(r_2 \le r_1\) (where \(\{ B_\alpha \} _{\alpha =0}^{r_1-1}\) is the larger family and \(\{ A_j\} _{j=0}^{r_2-1}\) the smaller), then there exists a rectangular isometry \(V\) (\(r_1 \times r_2\), \(V^\dagger V = \mathbb {1}_{r_2}\)) such that \(B_\alpha = \sum _j V_{\alpha j}\, A_j\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 5.55Thm. 2.1 item 4: Rectangular Kraus freedom (general index variant)

Variant of Theorem 5.54 with general finite index types \(\iota _1, \iota _2\) in place of \(\mathrm{Fin}\, r_1, \mathrm{Fin}\, r_2\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 5.50Thm. 2.1: Unitary freedom in Kraus operators

Let \(U\) be a unitary \(r \times r\) matrix (\(U^\dagger U = \mathbb {1}\)) and suppose \(K_j = \sum _\ell U_{j\ell }\, \tilde{K}_\ell \). Then \(\{ K_j\} \) and \(\{ \tilde{K}_\ell \} \) define the same Kraus map:

\[ \sum _j K_j X K_j^\dagger = \sum _\ell \tilde{K}_\ell X \tilde{K}_\ell ^\dagger \quad \forall \, X \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C}). \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 5.48Thm. 2.1: TP implies Kraus normalization

If \(T(X) = \sum _i K_i X K_i^\dagger \) is trace-preserving, then \(\sum _i K_i^\dagger K_i = \mathbb {1}\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 5.49Thm. 2.1: Kraus normalization implies TP

If \(\sum _i K_i^\dagger K_i = \mathbb {1}\), then \(T(X) = \sum _i K_i X K_i^\dagger \) is trace-preserving.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 5.51Thm. 2.1: Orthonormal Kraus transition is unitary

Let \(\{ K_j\} _{j=0}^{r-1}\) and \(\{ K'_j\} _{j=0}^{r-1}\) be two Hilbert–Schmidt orthonormal Kraus families, and suppose

\[ K_j = \sum _{\ell =0}^{r-1} U_{j\ell }\, K'_\ell . \]

Then the transition matrix \(U\) is unitary: \(U^\dagger U = \mathbb {1}_r\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 7.37CP variant: Kadison–Schwarz for commuting-dominant inputs

Let \(E^*\) be the adjoint of a trace-preserving Kraus map. If \(A \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) admits a positive semidefinite dominant \(B\) commuting with \(A\), \(A^\dagger \), and satisfying \(A^\dagger A \le B\), then \(E^*(A^\dagger ) E^*(A) \le E^*(B)\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 7.21Fixed-point peripheral eigenvectors satisfy KS equality

Let \(E\) be unital and let \(E^*\) be trace-preserving with a positive definite fixed point \(\rho {\gt}0\). If \(E(X)=\mu X\) with \(|\mu |=1\), then

\[ E(X^\dagger X)=E(X)^\dagger E(X). \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 7.22KS gap decomposition

For a unital Kraus map \(E\), the Kadison–Schwarz gap decomposes as

\[ E(X^\dagger X) - E(X)^\dagger E(X) = \sum _{i=0}^{d-1} R_i^\dagger R_i, \]

where \(R_i := X K_i^\dagger - K_i^\dagger E(X)\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 7.32CP Schwarz inequality for normal operators

Let \(E^*(X) = \sum _i K_i^\dagger X K_i\) be the adjoint Kraus map of a trace-preserving Kraus family, and let \(A \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) be normal. Then the Schwarz gap

\[ E^*(A^\dagger A) - E^*(A^\dagger ) E^*(A) \]

is positive semidefinite. Equivalently,

\[ E^*(A^\dagger ) E^*(A) \le E^*(A^\dagger A). \]

This is the completely positive case of ( .

LaTeX
Lean
  • KadisonSchwarz.kadison_schwarz_normal_cp
  • KadisonSchwarz.kadison_schwarz_normal_cp_le
Theorem 7.24KS equality for peripheral eigenvectors

Let \(E\) be a Kraus map that is both unital and trace-preserving. If \(E(X) = \mu X\) with \(|\mu | = 1\), then the KS gap vanishes: \(E(X^\dagger X) = E(X)^\dagger E(X)\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 8.11Left-canonical (trace-preserving) gauge

Let \(\sigma \) be a fixed point of the adjoint transfer map \(\sum _i (A^i)^\dagger \sigma A^i = \sigma \), and let \(S\) be invertible with \(S^\dagger S = \sigma \). Define \(A'^i = S A^i S^{-1}\). Then \(\sum _i (A'^i)^\dagger A'^i = \mathbb {1}\), i.e. the gauged Kraus map is trace-preserving. This is the left-canonical normalization used in subsequent chapters.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 17.4Left-canonical preserved by physical rotation

If \(u u^\dagger = I\) and \(A\) is left-canonical, then \(A_u\) is left-canonical.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 7.25Left multiplicative identity from KS equality

Let \(E\) be a unital Kraus map. If \(E(X^\dagger X) = E(X)^\dagger E(X)\), then \(E(X^\dagger Y) = E(X)^\dagger E(Y)\) for all \(Y \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 11.7Phase-scaling preserves normalization

If \(\sum _i (A^i)^\dagger A^i = \mathbb {1}\) and \(|\zeta | = 1\), then \(\sum _i (\zeta A^i)^\dagger (\zeta A^i) = \mathbb {1}\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 11.79Left-canonical primitive blocking from irreducibility

For a trace-preserving irreducible tensor, a positive blocking period yields a blocked tensor that is left-canonical and primitive.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 10.33Fixed-length vector spanning implies matrix spanning

Assume:

  1. \(H_n(A, \varphi ) = \mathbb {C}^D\) (length-\(n\) word products applied to \(\varphi \) span all of \(\mathbb {C}^D\)), and

  2. for each standard basis vector \(e_j\), the rank-one operator \(|\varphi \rangle \! \langle e_j|\) lies in \(S_m(A)\).

Then \(S_{n+m}(A) = M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 7.57Lieb concavity — restatement

Restates Theorem 7.56 for downstream use. Axiom-dependent: not fully formalized until Theorem 7.56 is proved.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 7.56Lieb concavity

For \(s\in [0,1]\), any matrix \(K\), and positive-definite matrices \(A_1,A_2,B_1,B_2\), the map \((A,B)\mapsto \operatorname{tr}(K^\dagger A^s K B^{1-s})\) is jointly concave:

\[ t\, \Re \operatorname{tr}(K^\dagger A_1^s K B_1^{1-s}) +(1-t)\, \Re \operatorname{tr}(K^\dagger A_2^s K B_2^{1-s}) \; \le \; \Re \operatorname{tr}\! \bigl(K^\dagger (tA_1+(1-t)A_2)^s K (tB_1+(1-t)B_2)^{1-s}\bigr). \]

See Lieb (1973) and Ando (1979).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.25Lindblad form equals generator decomposition

A Lindblad form with Hamiltonian \(H\) and operators \(\{ L_j\} \) defines the same linear map as the generator decomposition \((\phi , \kappa )\) with \(\phi (\rho ) = \sum _j L_j \rho L_j^\dagger \) and \(\kappa = iH + \frac{1}{2}\sum _j L_j^\dagger L_j\). This is ( .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.26Lindblad form is CCP

Every Lindblad form defines a conditionally completely positive map.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.24Lindblad form is trace-annihilating

Every Lindblad form defines a trace-annihilating linear map.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 4.6Existence and uniqueness of the linear extension

If \(A\) is injective and \(A\), \(B\) generate the same MPV family, then there exists a unique linear map \(T : M_{D}(\mathbb {C}) \to M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) satisfying \(T(A^i) = B^i\) for all \(i\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 4.7Multiplicativity of the linear extension

If \(A\) is injective and \(A\), \(B\) generate the same MPV family, and \(T : M_{D}(\mathbb {C}) \to M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) is the unique linear map satisfying \(T(A^i) = B^i\) for all \(i\), then \(T(MN) = T(M) T(N)\) for all \(M, N \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 5.64Trace-pairing expansion of a linear map

If \(\{ \sigma _i\} \) is trace-self-dual, then every linear map \(T : M_{D}(\mathbb {C}) \to M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) admits the expansion

\[ T(\rho ) = \sum _i \sum _j t_{ij}\, \operatorname {tr}(\sigma _j \rho )\, \sigma _i, \qquad t_{ij} = \operatorname {tr}(\sigma _i T(\sigma _j)). \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 17.48Local symmetry \(\Leftrightarrow \) spectral radius one

For a pure FCS, assume \(u u^\dagger = \mathbb {1}\), \(\mathcal{E}_A(\mathbb {1})=\mathbb {1}\), \(\Lambda \) is positive definite, \(\operatorname{tr}(\Lambda )=1\), and \(\mathcal{E}_A^\dagger (\Lambda )=\Lambda \). Then \(u\) is a local symmetry iff \(\rho (\mathcal{E}_u) = 1\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 16.49Local gauge extraction

At a single vertex, vertex-injectivity of both tensors and the same-state condition force a local gauge relation. Schematically, injectivity on the star neighbourhood provides a left inverse that isolates the local edge gauges:

\[ \begin{array}{c} \text{injectivity on } \mathrm{star}(v) \text{ gives a left inverse} \\ \text{which isolates the local edge gauges around } v \end{array} \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 7.55Jensen for concave log — restatement

Restates Theorem 7.54 for downstream use as a property of a positive map. Axiom-dependent: not fully formalized until Theorem 7.54 is proved.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 3.13LPDOs are MPDOs

Every LPDO tensor is an MPDO.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 18.57Martingale criterion for spectral gap

Consider a frustration-free Hamiltonian

\[ H = \sum _{i=1}^n h_i \]

with projector interaction terms (\(h_i^2 = h_i\)). Suppose that for every pair of overlapping terms \(h_i, h_j\) there exist constants \(c_{ij} \ge 0\) and \(\gamma {\gt} 0\) satisfying

\[ h_i h_j + h_j h_i \ge -c_{ij}(1-\gamma )(h_i + h_j), \]

and suppose further that \(c_{ij} = c_{ji}\) for every overlapping pair \((i,j)\) and that \(\sum _{j:\, j \ne i} c_{ij} \le 1\) for every \(i\). Then the smallest positive eigenvalue of \(H\) satisfies \(\lambda _{\min }^+(H) \ge \gamma \).

LaTeX
Theorem 7.28Multiplicative-domain characterization

Let \(E\) be a unital Kraus map. Then

\[ X \in \mathcal{A}_R(E) \iff E(X X^\dagger ) = E(X) E(X)^\dagger \]

and

\[ X \in \mathcal{A}_L(E) \iff E(X^\dagger X) = E(X)^\dagger E(X). \]

Together these are the two one-sided characterizations from ( .

LaTeX
Lean
  • KadisonSchwarz.mem_rightMultiplicativeDomain_iff
  • KadisonSchwarz.mem_leftMultiplicativeDomain_iff
Theorem 7.31Restriction to multiplicative domain is a \(*\)-homomorphism

If \(E\) is unital, then the restricted map \(E|_{\mathcal{A}(E)} : \mathcal{A}(E) \to M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) is a \(*\)-algebra homomorphism. This is the homomorphism conclusion of ( .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 7.30Multiplicative domain is a \(*\)-subalgebra

For a unital Kraus map \(E\), the full multiplicative domain \(\mathcal{A}(E)\) is a \(*\)-subalgebra of \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\). This is the algebraic content of ( .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 18.55Ground-space membership

If \(P_K = P_{AX} \circ P_{XB}\) is a commuting parent Hamiltonian decomposition, then

\[ P_K v = v \quad \Longleftrightarrow \quad P_{AX} v = v \text{ and } P_{XB} v = v. \]

This is the algebraic form of equation (D.2) from : \(K_{AXB} = (K_{AX} \otimes H_B) \cap (H_A \otimes K_{XB})\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.4Iterated mixed transfer

For all \(X \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) and \(N \ge 0\),

\[ F_{AB}^N(X) = \sum _{\sigma \in \{ 0,\ldots ,d{-}1\} ^N} A^\sigma X (B^\sigma )^\dagger , \]

where \(A^\sigma = A^{\sigma _1} \cdots A^{\sigma _N}\) denotes the word evaluation (Definition 2.2).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.5Matrix trace of iterated mixed transfer at the identity

For \(N \ge 0\),

\[ \operatorname{tr}\bigl(F_{AB}^N(\mathbb {1})\bigr) = \sum _{\sigma } \operatorname{tr}\bigl(A^\sigma (B^\sigma )^\dagger \bigr). \]

This is the matrix trace of \(F_{AB}^N\) evaluated at the identity matrix. It should not be confused with the operator trace \(\operatorname{Tr}(F_{AB}^N)\), which equals the MPV overlap (Theorem 9.7).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.16Spectral radius \(\ge 1\) implies gauge-phase equivalence

Let \(A, B\) be injective normalized MPS tensors. If \(\rho (F_{AB}) \ge 1\), then \(A\) and \(B\) are gauge-phase equivalent.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.28Gauge-equivalence from irreducible-TP spectral radius

Let \(A, B\) be irreducible trace-preserving normalized MPS tensors of bond dimension \(D\). If \(\rho (F_{AB}) \ge 1\), then \(A\) and \(B\) are gauge-phase equivalent.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 3.10MPO ZCL iff doubled-index MPS RFP

An MPO tensor has zero correlation length if and only if its doubled-index MPS view is a renormalization fixed point.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 3.8Positivity of the MPO transfer map

If \(X \ge 0\), then \(\mathcal{E}_M(X) \ge 0\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 2.28MPV decomposition

The MPV of a block-diagonal tensor decomposes as

\[ |V^{(N)}(A)\rangle \; =\; \sum _{k=1}^{r} \mu _k^N \, |V^{(N)}(A_k)\rangle . \]

Equivalently, for each configuration \(\sigma \) one has

\[ V^{(N)}(A)_\sigma \; =\; \sum _{k=1}^{r} \mu _k^N \, V^{(N)}(A_k)_\sigma . \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 18.31MPV nonvanishing for block-injective tensors

If \(A\) is \(L_0\)-block-injective with \(L_0 {\gt} 0\), \(D \ge 1\), and \(N \ge L_0 + 1\), then \(V^{(N)}(A) \ne 0\) in the \(N\)-site space.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 11.8MPV under block normalization

For any nonzero scaling factors \(\mu _k\), set \(\eta _k := \mu _k / |\mu _k|\) and \(B_k := |\mu _k| A_k\). Then \(\bigoplus _k \mu _k A_k\) and \(\bigoplus _k \eta _k B_k\) generate the same MPV family. Equivalently, the \(k\)-th block contribution factors as

\[ \mu _k^N V^{(N)}(A_k)_\sigma = \eta _k^N |\mu _k|^N V^{(N)}(A_k)_\sigma , \]

so the modulus contributes only through \(|\mu _k|^N\), while the remaining factor \(\eta _k\) is a unit-modulus phase.

LaTeX
Theorem 11.82Zero MPV for the zero tensor

For a nonempty spin chain, the MPS value of every spin word is zero for the zero tensor.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 11.39MPV of zero tensor

For any spin configuration \(\sigma \) of length \(N\),

\[ V^{(N)}(\mathbf{0}_{d,D})_\sigma = \begin{cases} D & \text{if } N = 0, \\ 0 & \text{if } N \ge 1. \end{cases} \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 11.2Per-block single-block FT

If all block tensors \(A_k\) are injective and the per-block same-MPV condition holds (\(A_k\) and \(B_k\) generate the same MPV family for each \(k\)), then each pair \((A_k, B_k)\) is gauge equivalent.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 11.4Global multi-block fundamental theorem

If each block \(A_k\) is injective and each pair \((A_k, B_k)\) generates the same MPV family, then the block-diagonal tensors \(\bigoplus _k \mu _k A_k\) and \(\bigoplus _k \mu _k B_k\) are gauge equivalent.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 6.13Mutual information is non-negative

For any bipartite density matrix \(\rho _{AB}\), \(I(A{:}B) \ge 0\).

LaTeX
Theorem 11.32Self-overlap is derived in normal canonical form

If \((\mu _k, A_k)\) satisfies the normal canonical form conditions, then \(O_{A_k A_k}(N) \to 1\) for every block \(k\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 13.13Newton–Girard trace recursion

If \(A, B \in M_{n}(\mathbb {C})\) satisfy \(\operatorname{tr}(A^k) = \operatorname{tr}(B^k)\) for all \(k \ge 1\), then \(A\) and \(B\) have the same characteristic polynomial.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 10.10Nilpotent power bound

If \(f\) is nilpotent on a space of dimension \(n\), then \(f^n = 0\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 18.40NNCPH implies RFP

If \(A\) is normal and its parent Hamiltonian with \(L = 2\) has commuting local terms for every chain length \(N \ge 2\), then \(A\) is a renormalization fixed point (RFP). Status: Scaffold; requires the ground-space characterisation of Beigi (J. Phys. A 45, 025306, 2012) needed for ( .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 11.83Nonzero Kraus operator in irreducible dimension at least two

An irreducible tensor of bond dimension at least two has at least one nonzero Kraus operator.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 10.13Nonzero trace product

If \(A\) is normal, then there exists a word \(w\) of length \(\le D^2\) such that \(\operatorname{tr}(A^w) \neq 0\). This is ( . For the trace argument below, the nontrivial case is \(D \ge 1\); when \(D = 0\) the normality hypothesis is vacuous.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 10.15Sharp nonzero trace product

If \(A\) is normal, then there exists a word \(w\) of length \(\le D^2 - \operatorname{kr}(A) + 1\) such that \(\operatorname{tr}(A^w) \neq 0\). This is ( with the sharp length bound.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 10.16Sharp nonzero trace product — positive length

If \(A\) is normal and \(D \ge 2\), then there exists a word \(w\) of positive length \(1 \le |w| \le D^2 - \operatorname{kr}(A) + 1\) such that \(\operatorname{tr}(A^w) \neq 0\). This strengthens Theorem 10.15 by requiring \(|w| \ge 1\), which is needed for the blocking argument in Theorem 10.28.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.15Factorial bound on the Dyson remainder

For \(s \in [0,t]\), \(M = \sup _{u \in [0,t]}\| e^{uL}\| \), and \(M' = \sup _{u \in [0,t]}\| e^{uL'}\| \):

\[ \bigl\| T'_s - \textstyle \sum _{n {\lt} N}\widetilde{T}^{(n)}_s\bigr\| \le M'\, \frac{(s\, \| \Delta \| \, M)^N}{N!}. \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.12Factorial norm bound for Dyson iterates

For \(s \in [0,t]\) and \(M = \sup _{u \in [0,t]} \| e^{uL}\| \),

\[ \| \widetilde{T}^{(n)}_s\| \le M\, \frac{(s\, \| \Delta \| \, M)^n}{n!}. \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 11.62Blocking preserves normality

If \(A\) is normal and \(p \ge 1\), then \(A^{[p]}\) is normal.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 11.80Normality from primitivity and a positive-definite fixed point

A primitive tensor admitting a positive-definite fixed point of its transfer map is normal.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 10.53Primitive MPS with positive-definite fixed point is normal

If \(A\) is a primitive MPS tensor with positive-definite fixed point \(\rho \), then \(A\) is normal (has eventually full Kraus rank). No aperiodicity assumption is needed.

This is the key connection between the spectral-gap primitivity of Definition 10.43 and normality, filling the gap that the aperiodicity-based argument in §10.11 leaves open.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 11.61TP-primitive irreducible tensor is normal

Let \(A\) be a left-canonical MPS tensor with \(D {\gt} 0\), irreducible transfer map, and peripheral spectrum \(\{ 1\} \). Then \(A\) is normal.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.89Cor. 7.2(1): full algebra generation implies non-reducibility

If the algebra generated by \(\{ L_j\} \) and \(\kappa \) is the entire matrix algebra \(M_d(\mathbb {C})\), then the QDS is not reducible. This is ( .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.90Cor. 7.2(2): Hermitian span + trivial commutant implies non-reducibility

If the Lindblad span is Hermitian-closed and its commutant is trivial, then the QDS is not reducible. This is ( .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.91Cor. 7.2(3): large Kossakowski rank implies non-reducibility

If \(\operatorname{rank}(C) {\gt} d^2 - d\), then the QDS is not reducible. This is ( .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 11.33Modulus-one eigenvalue rigidity for irreducible TP blocks

Let \(A\) and \(B\) be irreducible MPS tensors satisfying \(\sum _i (A^i)^\dagger A^i = \mathbb {1}\) and \(\sum _i (B^i)^\dagger B^i = \mathbb {1}\). If the mixed transfer map has spectral radius at least \(1\) (equivalently, it has a peripheral eigenvalue of modulus \(1\)), then \(A\) and \(B\) are gauge-phase equivalent.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 5.24\(1\) is a peripheral eigenvalue

If \(E\) has a nonzero fixed point \(\rho \neq 0\) with \(E(\rho ) = \rho \), then \(1\) is a peripheral eigenvalue of \(E\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 7.29One-sided multiplicative domains are subalgebras

For a unital Kraus map \(E\), both \(\mathcal{A}_R(E)\) and \(\mathcal{A}_L(E)\) are unital subalgebras of \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\).

LaTeX
Lean
  • KadisonSchwarz.rightMultiplicativeDomainSubalgebra
  • KadisonSchwarz.leftMultiplicativeDomainSubalgebra
Theorem 8.4Orthogonal trace condition

Let \(E\) be an irreducible completely positive map on \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\), and let \(A, B \ge 0\) be nonzero positive semidefinite matrices with \(\operatorname{tr}(B A) = 0\). Then there exists \(t\) with \(1 \le t \le D - 1\) such that

\[ \operatorname{tr}\bigl(B \cdot E^t(A)\bigr) {\gt} 0. \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.25Overlap decay

Let \(A, B\) be injective normalized tensors of the same bond dimension that are not gauge-phase equivalent. Then \(O_{AB}(N) \to 0\) as \(N \to \infty \).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.31Overlap decay (irreducible-TP)

Let \(A, B\) be irreducible trace-preserving normalized tensors of the same bond dimension that are not gauge-phase equivalent. Then \(O_{AB}(N) \to 0\) as \(N \to \infty \).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.23Rectangular overlap decay

If \(D_1 \neq D_2\) and both tensors are injective and normalized, then \(O_{AB}(N) \to 0\) as \(N \to \infty \).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.33Rectangular overlap decay (irreducible-TP)

If \(D_1 \neq D_2\) and both tensors are irreducible and trace-preserving normalized, then \(O_{AB}(N) \to 0\) as \(N \to \infty \).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.7Overlap equals transfer trace

For tensors \(A, B\) of bond dimension \(D \ge 1\),

\[ O_{AB}(N) = \operatorname{Tr}(F_{AB}^N). \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.8Rectangular overlap as transfer trace

For tensors \(A\) of bond dimension \(D_1 \ge 1\) and \(B\) of bond dimension \(D_2 \ge 1\),

\[ O_{AB}(N) = \operatorname{Tr}((F_{AB}^{\mathrm{rect}})^N). \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 14.10Sector decomposition formula

For a sector decomposition \(S\) with associated tensor \(A_{\mathrm{tot}}\), the MPV satisfies

\[ V^{(N)}(A_{\mathrm{tot}})_\sigma = \sum _{j=1}^{g} c_{S,j}^{(N)} V^{(N)}(A_j)_\sigma . \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 18.59Spectral gap for MPS parent Hamiltonians

For an \(L_0\)-block injective MPS tensor \(A\), let \(L := 2L_0\) be the interaction range (two blocked sites in the \(L_0\)-blocked picture). There exists \(\gamma {\gt} 0\) such that for all \(N \ge 2L\) with \(L_0 \mid N\),

\[ \lambda _{\min }^+(H_N(A,L)) \ge \gamma , \]

i.e. the parent Hamiltonian is uniformly gapped.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 18.45Absorption by the left projector

If \(P_K = P_{AX} \circ P_{XB}\) is a commuting parent Hamiltonian decomposition, then

\[ P_{AX} \circ P_K = P_K. \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 12.8Per-block same MPV gives global gauge equivalence

If all block tensors \(A_k\) are injective, and \(B_k\) are block tensors of the same bond dimensions such that per-block same-MPV holds, then each pair \((A_k, B_k)\) is gauge equivalent, and the block-diagonal tensors \(\bigoplus _k \mu _k A_k\) and \(\bigoplus _k \mu _k B_k\) are globally gauge equivalent.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 16.34Block separation from canonical form

Let \(\{ A_k\} \) be in canonical form with weights \(\{ \mu _k\} \), and let \(\{ B_k\} \) be another family with each \(B_k\) injective and left-canonical. If \(\mathcal{V}\! (\bigoplus _k \mu _k A_k) = \mathcal{V}\! (\bigoplus _k \mu _k B_k)\), then \(\mathcal{V}(A_k) = \mathcal{V}(B_k)\) for each block \(k\) individually. The proof isolates the leading block and treats the remaining blocks as an exponentially decaying tail with \(\rho = |\mu _1|/|\mu _0| {\lt} 1\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 16.31Per-block equality of MPV families and per-block gauge equivalence

Let \(\{ A_k\} \) be a family of injective block tensors. Then \(\mathcal{V}(A_k) = \mathcal{V}(B_k)\) for all \(k\) if and only if \(A_k\) and \(B_k\) are gauge equivalent for all \(k\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 14.40Eventual linear independence of a periodic basis

For a finite family of pairwise non-equivalent periodic tensors whose periods divide a common integer \(p\), there is \(N_0\) such that for every \(N \ge N_0\) the vectors \(\{ |V^{(pN)}(A_j)\rangle \} _j\) are linearly independent.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 14.29Periodic Fundamental Theorem

Let \(A\) and \(B\) be MPS tensors in irreducible form, with bases of periodic blocks \(\{ A_j\} _{j \in J}\) and \(\{ B_k\} _{k \in K}\) respectively. Suppose that \(\mathcal{V}(A) = \mathcal{V}(B)\) (equal MPV families for all lengths \(N\)).

Then \(|J| = |K|\) and there is a bijection \(\pi : J \to K\) such that \(A_j\) and \(B_{\pi (j)}\) are \(m_j\)-periodic for the same period \(m_j\). Moreover, for each \(j \in J\) there exists:

  1. an invertible matrix \(Y_j\) of size \(D_j \times D_{\pi (j)}\), and

  2. a diagonal unitary matrix \(Z_j\) of size \(D_j \times D_j\) satisfying \(Z_j^{m_j} = \mathbb {1}\) and \([A_j^i, Z_j] = 0\) for all \(i\),

such that

\[ Z_j\, A_j^i = Y_j\, B_{\pi (j)}^i\, Y_j^{-1} \quad \text{for all physical indices } i. \]

For each periodic block, the extra freedom is encoded by

\[ \begin{array}{ccc} \bullet & \text{---} & \bullet \\ \vert & & \vert \\ \multicolumn{3}{c}{\textcolor{red}{\bullet }} \\ \multicolumn{3}{c}{Z_j} \end{array} \]

rather than by an ordinary aperiodic gauge alone: the operator \(Z_j\) sits on the closing virtual bond and satisfies \(Z_j^{m_j}=\mathbb {1}\). Equivalently, setting \(Y = \bigoplus _j Y_j \otimes \mathbb {1}_{r_j}\) and \(Z = \bigoplus _j Z_j \otimes \mathbb {1}_{r_j}\), one has \(Z A^i = Y B^i Y^{-1}\) for all \(i\), with \([A^i, Z] = 0\) and \(\mathcal{V}(A) = \mathcal{V}(ZA)\).

Remark. The proof is conditional on the periodic-overlap dichotomy (Proposition 3.3 of  ), which remains to be proved.

LaTeX
Lean
  • MPSTensor.zgauge_construction
  • MPSTensor.equalCase_zgauge_pipeline
  • MPSTensor.fundamentalTheorem_periodic_equalCase_matching
  • MPSTensor.fundamentalTheorem_periodic_equalCase
Theorem 14.39Periodic overlap dichotomy

Let \(A\) and \(B\) be periodic tensors. Then at least one of the following alternatives holds:

  1. \(\langle V^{(N)}(A) | V^{(N)}(B) \rangle \to 0\) as \(N\to \infty \);

  2. \(A\) and \(B\) are equivalent up to repeated blocks.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 14.37Sector match yields repeated-block equivalence

For periodic tensors of the same period, existence of one matching sector pair implies repeated-block equivalence.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 14.38Different bond dimensions imply asymptotic orthogonality

If two periodic tensors have different bond dimensions, then \(\langle V^{(N)}(A) | V^{(N)}(B) \rangle \to 0\) as \(N\to \infty \).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 14.32Different periods imply asymptotic orthogonality

If \(A\) and \(B\) are periodic with different periods, then \(\langle V^{(N)}(A) | V^{(N)}(B) \rangle \to 0\) as \(N\to \infty \).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 14.34No sector match implies asymptotic orthogonality

If two periodic tensors with the same period admit no gauge-phase matching sector pair after blocking, then their overlap tends to zero.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 17.6Periodicity preserved by physical rotation

If \(u u^\dagger = I\) and \(A\) is periodic with period \(m\), then \(A_u\) is periodic with the same period.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 14.31Self-overlap along period multiples

If \(A\) is \(m\)-periodic, then the self-overlap along multiples of \(m\) converges to \(m\) (the period, viewed as a scalar).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.64Peripheral eigenvalues form a cyclic group

Let \(E\) be an irreducible unital trace-preserving Schwarz map on \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) with a positive-definite adjoint fixed point. Then there exist \(m \ge 1\) and a primitive \(m\)-th root of unity \(\gamma \) such that \(m \mid D\) and the peripheral eigenvalues of \(E\) are exactly \(\{ 1, \gamma , \gamma ^2, \ldots , \gamma ^{m-1}\} \).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.63Peripheral eigenvalues: cyclic structure

Let \(E\) be an irreducible unital Schwarz map on \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) with a positive-definite adjoint fixed point (trace-preservation is not required). Then there exist \(m \ge 1\) and a primitive \(m\)-th root of unity \(\gamma \) such that the peripheral eigenvalues of \(E\) are exactly \(\{ 1, \gamma , \gamma ^2, \ldots , \gamma ^{m-1}\} \).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 5.26Finiteness of peripheral eigenvalues

On a finite-dimensional space, the set of peripheral eigenvalues is finite.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.82Peripheral eigenvalue multiplicity one

Each peripheral eigenvalue has algebraic multiplicity one.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 5.28Peripheral powers imply root of unity

If the set of peripheral eigenvalues of a linear endomorphism \(E\) on a finite-dimensional space is closed under powers (\(\mu \in \mathrm{peripheral}(E)\) and \(n \ge 1\) imply \(\mu ^n \in \mathrm{peripheral}(E)\)), then every peripheral eigenvalue is a root of unity.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 5.34Roots of unity via adjoint fixed point

Let \(E(X) = \sum _i K_i X K_i^\dagger \) be a Kraus map that is unital, and assume that the adjoint Kraus map \(E^*(X) = \sum _i K_i^\dagger X K_i\) has a positive definite fixed point and that \(E\) is irreducible. Then every peripheral eigenvalue of \(E\) is a root of unity.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 5.27Power-stable peripheral eigenvalues are roots of unity

Let \(E\) be a linear endomorphism on a finite-dimensional space. If \(\mu \) is a peripheral eigenvalue of \(E\) and \(\mu ^n\) is an eigenvalue of \(E\) for every \(n \ge 1\), then \(\mu \) is a root of unity.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.10Cor. 7.1: Perturbation bound

For \(t \ge 0\),

\[ \bigl\| e^{tL'} - e^{tL}\bigr\| \le t\, \| \Delta \| \cdot \sup _{s \in [0,t]}\| e^{sL}\| \cdot \sup _{s \in [0,t]}\| e^{sL'}\| , \]

where \(\Delta = L' - L\). This is ( .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 8.15PSD eigenvector for positive maps

Let \(E : M_{D}(\mathbb {C}) \to M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) be a positive linear map with \(D {\gt} 0\), and assume that \(E(\sigma ) \neq 0\) for every nonzero PSD matrix \(\sigma \). Then there exist a nonzero PSD matrix \(\rho \) and a positive real \(r {\gt} 0\) such that \(E(\rho ) = r \rho \).

This is the finite-dimensional Perron–Frobenius theorem for positive maps on matrix algebras ( ( ; see also  ). The proof applies Brouwer’s fixed-point theorem to the normalization map \(\rho \mapsto E(\rho ) / \operatorname{tr}(E(\rho ))\) on the compact convex set of density matrices.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 16.7Physical realisation is multiplicative

Let \(A\) be an injective MPS tensor. For all \(X, Y \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\),

\[ O_A(XY) = O_A(X)\, O_A(Y). \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 12.4Decomposition of automorphisms of \(\prod M_{D_k}(\mathbb {C})\)

Assume \(D_k \ge 1\) for all \(k\). Every \(\mathbb {C}\)-algebra automorphism \(T\) of \(\prod _{k=1}^r M_{D_k}(\mathbb {C})\) decomposes as a block permutation \(\pi \) followed by per-block inner automorphisms: there exist \(\pi \in S_r\) with \(D_{\pi (k)} = D_k\) and invertible matrices \(X_k \in \mathrm{GL}_{D_k}(\mathbb {C})\) such that, for every tuple \(M = (M_1,\ldots ,M_r)\), \(T(M)_k = X_k M_{\pi (k)} X_k^{-1}\), where \(T(M)_k\) denotes the \(k\)-th block component of \(T(M)\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 12.7Per-block linear extensions define a product algebra automorphism

Let \(A_k\) and \(B_k\) be injective tensors of bond dimension \(D_k\) for \(k = 1, \ldots , r\), and suppose the per-block same-MPV condition holds. Then the per-block linear extensions \(T_k : A_k^i \mapsto B_k^i\) define an algebra automorphism of \(\prod _k M_{D_k}(\mathbb {C})\), which decomposes as a permutation \(\pi \) with \(D_{\pi (k)} = D_k\) and per-block conjugation matrices \(X_k\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 16.27Block-permutation decomposition

The product algebra automorphism \(T\) decomposes as a block permutation composed with per-block inner automorphisms: there exist a permutation \(\sigma \in S_r\) (with \(D_{\sigma (k)} = D_k\) for all \(k\)) and invertible matrices \(X_k \in \mathrm{GL}_{D_k}(\mathbb {C})\) such that

\[ (T(M))_{\sigma (k)} = X_k\, M\, X_k^{-1} \]

for all \(M \in M_{D_k}(\mathbb {C})\) (viewed as supported in block \(k\)) and all \(k\). Equivalently, the product-algebra automorphism first permutes the simple matrix blocks and then acts inside each block by an inner conjugation.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 11.36Irreducible block decomposition with left-canonical normalization

For any MPS tensor \(A\), there exist irreducible blocks \((A_k)_{k=1}^r\) that generate the same MPV family as \(A\). Moreover, for each block \(k\), if that block has already been placed in TP gauge and \(D_k {\gt} 0\), one obtains a left-canonical normalization (unitary, diagonal PD fixed point, MPV preservation). This yields the left-canonical normalization; further reduction to normal canonical form is carried out in Chapter 9.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 18.48Cross-absorption by the left projector

If \(P_K = P_{AX} \circ P_{XB}\) is a commuting parent Hamiltonian decomposition, then

\[ P_K \circ P_{AX} = P_K. \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 18.46Absorption by the right projector

If \(P_K = P_{AX} \circ P_{XB}\) is a commuting parent Hamiltonian decomposition, then

\[ P_K \circ P_{XB} = P_K. \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 18.44Idempotence of the product projector

If \(P_K = P_{AX} \circ P_{XB}\) is a commuting parent Hamiltonian decomposition, then

\[ P_K \circ P_K = P_K. \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 8.17Positive-definite adjoint eigenvector for irreducible tensors

Let \(A\) be an irreducible MPS tensor with \(D {\gt} 0\) and some \(A^i \neq 0\). Then there exist a positive definite matrix \(\sigma \) and a positive real \(r {\gt} 0\) such that \(\mathcal{E}_A^\dagger (\sigma ) = r \sigma \).

This combines the general PF existence ( ( ) with the irreducible upgrade to positive definiteness ( ( ); the application to the adjoint transfer map follows ( .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 7.39Positive maps preserve adjoints

If \(T : M_{D}(\mathbb {C}) \to M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) is positive, then \(T(A^\dagger ) = T(A)^\dagger \) for all \(A \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 7.40Order intervals are preserved

Let \(T : M_{D}(\mathbb {C}) \to M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) be positive with \(T(\mathbb {1}) \le \mathbb {1}\). If \(a \le 0 \le b\) and \(a \mathbb {1}\le A \le b \mathbb {1}\), then

\[ a \mathbb {1}\le T(A) \le b \mathbb {1}. \]

This is the matrix form of ( .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 7.38Positive maps are monotone

If \(T : M_{D}(\mathbb {C}) \to M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) is positive and \(A \le B\), then \(T(A) \le T(B)\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 7.41Spectral interval contractivity

Let \(T : M_{D}(\mathbb {C}) \to M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) be positive with \(T(\mathbb {1}) \le \mathbb {1}\), and let \(A \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) be Hermitian. If \(\operatorname{spec}(A) \subseteq [a,b]\) with \(a \le 0 \le b\), then

\[ \operatorname{spec}(T(A)) \subseteq [a,b]. \]

This is ( .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 7.54Jensen for concave log

For a positive unital map \(T\) and positive-definite \(A\):

\[ T(\log A) \le \log (TA). \]

This is ( for \(f=\log \). Requires unitality (\(T(\mathbb {1})=\mathbb {1}\)), not merely subunitality.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 7.50Jensen for concave real powers

For a positive subunital map \(T\) and \(p\in [0,1]\):

\[ T(A^p) \le (TA)^p. \]

This is ( for concave \(f(x)=x^p\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 7.52Jensen for convex real powers

For a positive subunital map \(T\) and \(p\in [1,2]\):

\[ (TA)^p \le T(A^p). \]

This is ( for convex \(f(x)=x^p\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 5.21Power decomposition

If \(E\) is trace-preserving and \(E(\rho ) = \rho \), then for \(n \ge 1\),

\[ E^n = P + (E - P)^n, \]

where \(P\) is the fixed-point projection.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 13.14Equal power sums imply equal multisets

Let \(\alpha , \beta : \{ 1, \ldots , n\} \to \mathbb {C}\). If \(\sum _i \alpha _i^k = \sum _i \beta _i^k\) for all \(k \ge 1\), then the multisets \(\{ \alpha _i\} \) and \(\{ \beta _i\} \) are equal.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 3.16Purification RFP implies LPDO

A purification-RFP tensor is, in particular, an LPDO.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 10.45Primitive MPS tensors have nonzero word products

If \(A\) is a primitive MPS tensor, then for every \(n \ge 0\) there exists a word \(w\) of length exactly \(n\) with \(A^w \neq 0\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 10.44Primitive overlap convergence

If \(A\) is a primitive MPS tensor, then \(O_{AA}(N) \to 1\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 11.44Blocking-period divisibility preserves primitivity

If the transfer map of \(A^{[p]}\) is primitive, \(p \mid q\), and \(q \ge 1\), then the transfer map of \(A^{[q]}\) is also primitive.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 5.39Primitive maps have a spectral gap on the complement

Let \(E : M_{D}(\mathbb {C}) \to M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) be trace-preserving, and let \(\rho \neq 0\) satisfy \(E(\rho ) = \rho \) and \(\operatorname{tr}(\rho ) \neq 0\). Let

\[ P(X) = \frac{\operatorname{tr}(X)}{\operatorname{tr}(\rho )} \, \rho \]

be the fixed-point projection associated to \(\rho \). Assume that:

  1. \(E\) is primitive;

  2. every eigenvalue \(\mu \) of \(E\) satisfies \(|\mu | \le 1\);

  3. whenever \(E(X) = X\) and \(\operatorname{tr}(X) = 0\), one has \(X = 0\).

Then every eigenvalue \(\nu \) of \(E - P\) satisfies \(|\nu | {\lt} 1\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 5.38Primitivity equals period one

A linear map with a nonzero fixed point is primitive if and only if its period is \(1\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 10.50Primitive with positive-definite fixed point implies irreducible tensor

If \(A\) is a primitive MPS tensor with PSD fixed point \(\rho \) and \(\rho \) is positive definite, then \(A\) is an irreducible tensor. This is part of ( (see also ( ).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.84Self-overlap convergence from a complementary spectral gap

Let \(A\) be a normalized MPS tensor with a nonzero PSD fixed point \(\rho \) of the transfer map. If \(\rho _{\mathrm{spec}}(\mathcal{E}_A - P) {\lt} 1\) (where \(P\) is the fixed-point projection), then \(O_{AA}(N) \to 1\) as \(N \to \infty \).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 10.61Primitive + positive definite fixed point imply normality

If \(A\) is a primitive MPS tensor with fixed point \(\rho \) and \(\rho \) is positive definite, then \(A\) is normal. No aperiodicity assumption is needed.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 11.43Primitive channels remain primitive under powers

If \(\mathcal{E}\) has peripheral spectrum \(\{ 1\} \) (is primitive) and \(m \ge 1\), then \(\mathcal{E}^m\) is also primitive.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 17.31Projective equivalence and cohomologous cocycles

Let \(\rho _1,\rho _2\) be projective representations with factor systems \(\omega _1,\omega _2\). Then

\[ \rho _1 \sim _{\mathrm{proj}} \rho _2 \iff \omega _1 \sim \omega _2. \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 11.25Proportional MPV implies gauge-phase equivalence

Let \(A\) and \(B\) be injective MPS tensors satisfying \(\sum _i (A^i)^\dagger A^i = \mathbb {1}\) and \(\sum _i (B^i)^\dagger B^i = \mathbb {1}\). If \(A\) and \(B\) generate proportional MPV families (\(V^{(N)}(A)_\sigma = c_N V^{(N)}(B)_\sigma \) for some \(c_N \in \mathbb {C}\)) and \(O_{AA}(N) \to 1\), \(O_{BB}(N) \to 1\), then \(A\) and \(B\) are gauge-phase equivalent.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 11.26Proportional MPV + primitive implies gauge-phase equivalence

Let \(A\) and \(B\) be MPS tensors with primitive transfer maps \(E_A, E_B\) and positive definite fixed points \(\rho _A, \rho _B\). If \(A\) and \(B\) generate proportional MPV families, then \(A\) and \(B\) are gauge-phase equivalent. This strengthens Theorem 11.25: primitivity supplies left-canonicality, irreducibility, and overlap convergence, reducing the hypothesis count from seven to four.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 8.8Existence of PSD fixed point

Assume \(D \ge 1\). Let \(A\) be an MPS tensor with \(\sum _i (A^i)^\dagger A^i = \mathbb {1}\) (so that \(\mathcal{E}_A\) is trace-preserving). Then there exists \(\rho \ge 0\), \(\rho \neq 0\), with \(\mathcal{E}_A(\rho ) = \rho \).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 8.2PSD fixed point is positive definite

Let \(A\) be an injective MPS tensor and let \(\rho \ge 0\), \(\rho \neq 0\), be a fixed point of the transfer map \(\mathcal{E}_A(\rho ) = \rho \). Then \(\rho \) is positive definite.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 8.3PSD fixed point is positive definite — irreducible version

If \(\mathcal{E}_A\) is irreducible and \(\rho \ge 0\), \(\rho \neq 0\) is a fixed point, then \(\rho \) is positive definite.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 8.5Uniqueness of PSD fixed point

Let \(A\) be an injective MPS tensor. If \(\rho , \sigma \ge 0\) are both nonzero fixed points of \(\mathcal{E}_A\), then \(\sigma = c \rho \) for some \(c \in \mathbb {C}\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 18.47Cross-absorption by the right projector

If \(P_K = P_{AX} \circ P_{XB}\) is a commuting parent Hamiltonian decomposition, then

\[ P_{XB} \circ P_K = P_K. \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.70Prop. 7.5: Irreducibility iff primitivity for QDS

For a quantum dynamical semigroup \(T_t = e^{tL}\):

\[ (\exists \, t_0 {\gt} 0,\ T_{t_0}\ \text{irreducible}) \; \iff \; (\forall \, t {\gt} 0,\ T_t\ \text{primitive and irreducible}). \]

This is ( .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 8.9Quantum Perron–Frobenius

Assume \(D \ge 1\). Let \(A\) be an injective MPS tensor with \(\sum _i (A^i)^\dagger A^i = \mathbb {1}\). Then the transfer map \(\mathcal{E}_A\) has a unique PSD fixed point \(\rho \) (up to scaling), and \(\rho \) is positive definite.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 10.40Rank-one extraction for blocked tensor

Suppose \(A\) is normal and \(N_0 \ge 1\) with \(S_{N_0}(A) = M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\). Then there exist words \(\sigma _0, \tau _0\) of length \(N_0\), nonzero vectors \(\varphi , \psi \), nonzero scalars \(\mu , \nu \), and \(m \in \mathbb {N}\) such that:

  1. \(A^{\sigma _0} \varphi = \mu \varphi \),

  2. \((A^{\tau _0})^T \psi = \nu \psi \),

  3. \(\varphi \psi ^T \in S_m(A^{[N_0]})\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.21Rectangular intertwining forces equal bond dimension

Let \(A\) and \(B\) be injective tensors of bond dimensions \(D_1\) and \(D_2\). If there exist \(X \in M_{D_1 \times D_2}(\mathbb {C}) \setminus \{ 0\} \) and \(\mu \in \mathbb {C}\) such that

\[ X (B^i)^\dagger = \mu \, (A^i)^\dagger X, \qquad A^i X = \mu \, X B^i \qquad \text{for all } i, \]

then \(D_1 = D_2\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 18.49Reverse product identity

If \(P_K = P_{AX} \circ P_{XB}\) is a commuting parent Hamiltonian decomposition, then

\[ P_{XB} \circ P_{AX} = P_K. \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 19.27Canonical-form blocks satisfy RFP iff ZCL

For a tensor in canonical form, each block is a renormalization fixed point if and only if it has zero correlation length. This is the RFP–ZCL part of ( .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 18.39RFP implies NNCPH

If \(A\) is a normal renormalization fixed point in left-canonical form, then for every chain length \(N \ge 2\) the parent Hamiltonian with \(L = 2\) has commuting local terms. Status: Scaffold; requires the structural theory of renormalization fixed points (Appendix B) needed for ( .

LaTeX
Theorem 19.18Unitary diagonal fixed point for a left-canonical normal RFP

Assume \(D {\gt} 0\). Let \(A\) be a normal renormalization fixed point such that

\[ \sum _i (A^i)^\dagger A^i = \mathbb {1}. \]

Then there exist a unitary \(U\) and a diagonal positive definite matrix \(\Lambda \) such that, for \(B^i := U^\dagger A^i U\),

\[ \sum _i (B^i)^\dagger B^i = \mathbb {1}\qquad \text{and}\qquad \mathcal{E}_B(\Lambda ) = \Lambda . \]

This is the diagonal fixed-point reduction in ( .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 19.16RFP normal tensor is injective

If \(A\) is a nonzero normal RFP tensor, then \(A\) is injective.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 19.20Full structural form for RFP tensors

A normal tensor \(A\) in canonical form II that is a renormalization fixed point admits the decomposition \(A^i = X \Lambda U^i X^{-1}\), where \(\Lambda \) is diagonal positive with \(\operatorname{tr}(\Lambda ) = 1\) and \(U\) is an isometry in the physical indices. The Lean statement lives in Archive/RFPStructuralFull.lean pending the rank-one idempotent CP classification (tracked by issue #233).

LaTeX
Theorem 19.17Left-canonical normal RFP tensor is injective

Assume \(D {\gt} 0\). Let \(A\) be a normal renormalization fixed point such that

\[ \sum _i (A^i)^\dagger A^i = \mathbb {1}. \]

Then \(A\) is injective. This is the left-canonical injectivity step in ( .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 19.21RG flow convergence for canonical-form tensors

For a tensor in canonical form with blocks \((A_k)\) and weights \((\mu _k)\), the iterated blocking \(\mathcal{E}_{A_k}^{2^n}\) converges pointwise to an idempotent linear map \(\mathcal{E}_\infty \) as \(n\to \infty \). That is, there exists \(\mathcal{E}_\infty \) with \(\mathcal{E}_\infty ^2 = \mathcal{E}_\infty \) such that \(\mathcal{E}_{A_k}^{2^n}(\rho ) \to \mathcal{E}_\infty (\rho )\) for all density matrices \(\rho \). This is ( .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 8.10Right-canonical (unital) gauge

Let \(\mathcal{E}_A(\rho ) = \rho \) and let \(S\) be an invertible matrix with \(S S^\dagger = \rho \). Define the gauged operators \(A'^i = S^{-1} A^i S\). Then \(\sum _i A'^i (A'^i)^\dagger = \mathbb {1}\), i.e. the gauged Kraus map is unital. This is the right-canonical normalization used in subsequent chapters.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 12.1Ring isomorphisms permute block ideals

Let \(\{ R_k\} _{k=1}^r\) be simple rings. Every ring isomorphism \(T : \prod _k R_k \to \prod _k R_k\) permutes the block ideals: there exists a permutation \(\pi \) such that \(T\) maps the \(k\)-th block ideal to the \(\pi (k)\)-th block ideal.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 17.8Physical rotation distributes over block assembly

The rotated block-diagonal tensor satisfies \((\bigoplus _k \mu _k A_k)_u = \bigoplus _k \mu _k (A_k)_u\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 14.13Weight multiset recovery from multiplicity data

Let \(S\) and \(T\) be two sector data over the same basis with the same multiplicities (\(r_j^S = r_j^T\) for all \(j\)). If the basis is eventually linearly independent and the total MPVs of \(S\) and \(T\) agree, then for each basis block \(j\) the sector weight multisets \(\{ \mu _{j,q}^S\} \) and \(\{ \mu _{j,q}^T\} \) are equal.

The argument proceeds as follows:

  1. BNT LI \(+\) equal MPVs \(\Rightarrow \) eventual coefficient equality (Theorem 14.11);

  2. Geometric extrapolation \(\Rightarrow \) full coefficient equality for all positive \(k\) (Theorem 14.12);

  3. Newton–Girard \(\Rightarrow \) equal weight multisets (Theorem 13.14).

At the level of block contributions, one compares the weighted sector sums \(\sum _j c_{A,j}^{(N)} V^{(N)}(A_j)\) and \(\sum _j c_{B,j}^{(N)} V^{(N)}(B_j)\), with the coefficients now aggregated over the multiplicity layers.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 7.51Jensen for concave real powers — restatement

Restates Theorem 7.50 for downstream use as a property of a positive map. Axiom-dependent: not fully formalized until Theorem 7.50 is proved.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 7.53Jensen for convex real powers — restatement

Restates Theorem 7.52 for downstream use as a property of a positive map. Axiom-dependent: not fully formalized until Theorem 7.52 is proved.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 17.7SameMPV preserved by physical rotation

If \(\mathcal{V}(A) = \mathcal{V}(B)\), then \(\mathcal{V}(A_u) = \mathcal{V}(B_u)\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 11.42Blocking distributes over MPV equivalence

If \(A\) generates the same MPV family as \(\bigoplus _k \mu _k B_k\), then for any \(p \ge 0\),

\[ A^{[p]} \sim \bigoplus _k \mu _k^p\, B_k^{[p]}. \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 16.17Finite-length agreement implies full agreement

Let \(A\) be an injective tensor of bond dimension \(D \ge 1\), and let \(B\) be a tensor of the same bond dimension. If \(A\) and \(B\) generate the same MPV family for every system size \(N \ge N_0\), then they generate the same MPV family for every system size.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 11.10Unitary conjugation preserves the MPV family

If \(U\) is a unitary matrix, then \(A\) and \(U^\dagger A U\) generate the same MPV family.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.41Absorbs the adjoint map

If \(T(\sigma )=\sigma \), then \(E_\sigma \circ T^* = E_\sigma \).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.38Idempotence

\(E_\sigma (E_\sigma (X)) = E_\sigma (X)\) for all \(X\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.44Scalar map is a conditional expectation

Let \(K\) be trace-preserving, let \(\rho {\gt}0\) be a positive-definite fixed point of the associated channel \(T(\rho )=\sum _i K_i\rho K_i^\dagger \) (so \(T(\rho )=\rho \)), and assume every adjoint fixed point of \(T^*\) is a scalar multiple of \(\mathbb {1}\). Then \(E_\rho \) is a conditional expectation onto the adjoint fixed-point \(*\)-subalgebra.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.45Wolf Cor. 6.6 — complete positivity of \(E_\sigma \)

For any positive semidefinite \(\sigma \), the scalar conditional expectation \(E_\sigma \) is a completely positive map.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.46Wolf Cor. 6.6 — \(\sigma \)-trace preservation of \(E_\sigma \)

If \(\operatorname {tr}\sigma \neq 0\), then for every \(X\)

\[ \operatorname {tr}\bigl(\sigma \, E_\sigma (X)\bigr) = \operatorname {tr}(\sigma X). \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.37Unitality

\(E_\sigma (\mathbb {1})=\mathbb {1}\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 7.36Thm. 5.6: Schwarz inequality for commuting-dominant operators

Let \(T : M_{D}(\mathbb {C}) \to M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) be a positive linear map with \(T(\mathbb {1}) \le \mathbb {1}\). If \(A \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) admits a positive semidefinite dominant \(B \ge 0\) with \(A^\dagger A \le B\) and \(B\) commuting with \(A\) (in the sense \(BA = AB\), \(BA^\dagger = A^\dagger B\)), then

\[ T(A^\dagger ) T(A) \le T(B). \]

This is ( .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 7.33Prop. 5.1: Schwarz inequality for normal operators

Let \(T : M_{D}(\mathbb {C}) \to M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) be a positive linear map with \(T(\mathbb {1}) \le \mathbb {1}\). If \(A \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) is normal, then

\[ T(A^\dagger A) - T(A^\dagger ) T(A) \ge 0. \]

This is ( .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 7.35Thm. 5.5: Schwarz inequality for subnormal operators

Let \(T : M_{D}(\mathbb {C}) \to M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) be a positive linear map with \(T(\mathbb {1}) \le \mathbb {1}\). If \(A \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) is subnormal (i.e. there exists a normal operator on a larger space whose compression to \(\mathbb {C}^D\) is \(A\)), then

\[ T(A^\dagger A) - T(A^\dagger ) T(A) \ge 0. \]

This is ( .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 11.73Sector decomposition from TP primitive irreducible blocks

From a trace-preserving primitive irreducible block decomposition with nonzero weights and non-decaying cross-overlaps for equal-norm blocks, one obtains a sector decomposition with strict norm ordering.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 11.59Sector irreducibility from orbit-sum lift

Let \(A\) be a left-canonical MPS tensor with irreducible adjoint transfer map, and let \((P_k)_{k=1}^m\) be the cyclic sector projections. Suppose the orbit-sum lift hypothesis holds: for every sector \(k\) and every subprojection \(Q \le P_k\) fixed by \((\mathcal{E}_A^\dagger )^m\), there exists a global projection \(R\) that is invariant under \(\mathcal{E}_A^\dagger \) and faithfully reflects triviality (\(Q = 0 \Leftrightarrow R = 0\), \(Q = P_k \Leftrightarrow R = \mathbb {1}\)). Then \((\mathcal{E}_A^\dagger )^m\) is irreducible on each corner \(P_k\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 4.8Simplicity of \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\)

A nonzero multiplicative \(\mathbb {C}\)-linear endomorphism of \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) is bijective.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 4.9Skolem–Noether

Every \(\mathbb {C}\)-algebra automorphism of \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) is inner: for any \(f \in \mathrm{Aut}_{\mathbb {C}\text{-alg}}(M_{D}(\mathbb {C}))\), there exists \(X \in \mathrm{GL}_D(\mathbb {C})\) such that \(f(M) = X M X^{-1}\) for all \(M\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.20Strict spectral gap

Let \(A, B\) be injective normalized MPS tensors that are not gauge-phase equivalent. Then \(\rho (F_{AB}) {\lt} 1\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.29Strict spectral gap (irreducible-TP)

Let \(A, B\) be irreducible trace-preserving normalized MPS tensors of the same bond dimension that are not gauge-phase equivalent. Then \(\rho (F_{AB}) {\lt} 1\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.22Rectangular spectral gap

Let \(A\) be an injective normalized tensor of bond dimension \(D_1\) and \(B\) an injective normalized tensor of bond dimension \(D_2\), with \(D_1 \neq D_2\). Then \(\rho (F_{AB}^{\mathrm{rect}}) {\lt} 1\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.32Rectangular spectral gap (irreducible-TP)

Let \(A\) (bond dimension \(D_1\)) and \(B\) (bond dimension \(D_2\)) be irreducible trace-preserving normalized tensors with \(D_1 \neq D_2\). Then \(\rho (F_{AB}^{\mathrm{rect}}) {\lt} 1\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 19.11Spectral gap from injectivity

For an injective trace-preserving MPS tensor, there exists \(\delta {\gt} 0\) such that every eigenvalue \(\mu \neq 1\) of the transfer map satisfies \(|\mu | \le 1 - \delta \).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.15Spectral radius bound

\(\rho (F_{AB}) \le 1\) for normalized tensors.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 8.23Spectral radius is the Perron eigenvalue

Let \(E\) be an irreducible completely positive map on \(M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\). Suppose \(\rho {\gt} 0\) and \(E(\rho ) = r\rho \) with \(r {\gt} 0\). Then the spectral radius of \(E\) is \(r\). This is ( .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 8.24Real spectral-radius identity

Under the hypotheses of Theorem 8.23, the real-valued spectral radius of \(E\) is also equal to \(r\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.51Prop. 6.9: stationary support is full

For an irreducible channel \(E\), the stationary support equals the identity: \(\mathrm{supp}(\rho _\infty ) = \mathbb {1}\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 5.59Thm. 2.2: Stinespring dual representation

For Kraus operators \(\{ K_j\} \) and \(A \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\),

\[ V^\dagger \, (A \otimes \mathbb {1}_r)\, V = \sum _j K_j^\dagger A\, K_j = T^*(A). \]

This is the Stinespring representation of the dual (Heisenberg picture) map \(T^*\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 5.61Thm. 2.2: Stinespring Schrödinger representation

The Kraus map \(T(\rho ) = \sum _j K_j \rho K_j^\dagger \) equals the partial trace over the dilation space:

\[ T(\rho )_{ij} = \sum _{k=0}^{r-1} (V \rho V^\dagger )_{(i,k),(j,k)} = \operatorname {tr}_r(V\rho V^\dagger ). \]

This is the Schrödinger-picture Stinespring representation.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 5.58Stinespring Gram identity

The Stinespring isometry satisfies

\[ V^\dagger V = \sum _{j=0}^{r-1} K_j^\dagger K_j. \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 5.60Thm. 2.2: Isometry condition iff TP

\(V^\dagger V = \mathbb {1}_D\) if and only if \(\sum _j K_j^\dagger K_j = \mathbb {1}_D\). In particular, \(V\) is an isometry precisely when the Kraus map is trace-preserving.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 17.49String order \(\Leftrightarrow \) local symmetry

For an injective MPS, assume \(u u^\dagger = \mathbb {1}\), \(\mathcal{E}_A(\mathbb {1})=\mathbb {1}\), \(\Lambda \) is positive definite, \(\operatorname{tr}(\Lambda )=1\), and \(\mathcal{E}_A^\dagger (\Lambda )=\Lambda \). Then string order for \(u\) exists iff \(u\) is a local symmetry.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 17.53String order is an SPT invariant

If \(A\) and \(B\) are injective, both on-site symmetric under a unitary representation \(U\), and if \(\mathcal{E}_A(\mathbb {1})=\mathbb {1}\), \(\mathcal{E}_B(\mathbb {1})=\mathbb {1}\), \(\Lambda _A\) and \(\Lambda _B\) are positive definite, \(\operatorname{tr}(\Lambda _A)=1\), \(\operatorname{tr}(\Lambda _B)=1\), \(\mathcal{E}_A^\dagger (\Lambda _A)=\Lambda _A\), and \(\mathcal{E}_B^\dagger (\Lambda _B)=\Lambda _B\), then for every \(g \in G\), string order exists for \(A\) with twist \(U(g)\) if and only if it exists for \(B\). In particular, since Definition 17.51 implies on-site symmetry for both tensors, this theorem applies to tensors in the same SPT phase.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 6.10Strong subadditivity

For a tripartite density matrix \(\rho _{ABC}\),

\[ S(\rho _{ABC}) + S(\rho _B) \le S(\rho _{AB}) + S(\rho _{BC}). \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 10.52Primitive MPS with positive-definite fixed point is strongly irreducible

If \(A\) is a primitive MPS tensor with positive-definite fixed point \(\rho \), then \(A\) is strongly irreducible in the sense of ( : \(\mathcal{E}_A\) is irreducible, the fixed point \(\rho \) is positive definite, and the peripheral spectrum of \(\mathcal{E}_A\) is \(\{ 1\} \).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.48Support projection invariance (Wolf Lemma 6.4)

Let \(E\) be a quantum channel and \(\rho \ge 0\) a positive semidefinite fixed point (\(E(\rho ) = \rho \)). Let \(P\) denote the support projection of \(\rho \). Then

\[ P \, E(P X P) \, P = E(P X P) \]

for all \(X \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.40Trace-annihilating generators yield trace-preserving semigroups

If \(L\) is trace-annihilating, then \(e^{tL}\) is trace-preserving for every \(t \in \mathbb {R}\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 16.37TI reduction corollary

Let \(A\) and \(B\) be MPS tensors of bond dimension \(D\) with \(A\) injective. Consider the two constant (translation-invariant) chains \((A, \ldots , A)\) and \((B, \ldots , B)\) of length \(n \ge 1\). If the combined tensors of the two chains generate the same MPV family, then:

  1. \(B\) is gauge equivalent to \(A\): there exists \(X \in \mathrm{GL}_D(\mathbb {C})\) with \(B^i = X\, A^i\, X^{-1}\) for all \(i\).

  2. \(B\) is injective.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 16.38TI reduction from same state

Under the same setup, if \(n \ge 3\) and the two constant chains generate the same chain state (rather than the same MPV family on the combined tensor), the same conclusion holds, provided one can pass from equality of chain states to equality of MPV families on the combined tensor.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 3.22Pure-state recovery of the RFP condition

For an MPS tensor \(A\), its diagonal MPO embedding is MPDO-RFP if and only if \(A\) is an RFP:

\[ A^{\mathrm{MPO}} \text{ is MPDO-RFP} \iff A \text{ is RFP}. \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 3.23Pure-state recovery of zero correlation length

For an MPS tensor \(A\), the diagonal MPO embedding is MPDO-RFP if and only if \(A\) has zero correlation length.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 3.21Transfer map of the diagonal pure-state embedding

The transfer map of the diagonal MPO associated to \(A\) agrees with the original MPS transfer map:

\[ \mathcal{E}_{A^{\mathrm{MPO}}} = \mathcal{E}_A. \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 8.18TP-gauge existence for irreducible tensors

Let \(A\) be an irreducible MPS tensor with \(D {\gt} 0\) and some \(A^i \neq 0\). Then there exist a positive real \(r\), a positive definite matrix \(\sigma \), and a gauge-equivalent tensor \(B\) such that

\[ B^i = \sigma ^{1/2} \cdot (r^{-1/2} A^i) \cdot \sigma ^{-1/2} \]

is trace-preserving: \(\sum _i (B^i)^\dagger B^i = \mathbb {1}\).

This is the “spectral rescaling \(+\) TP gauge” step of ( . The similarity is generally non-unitary.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 11.41Arbitrary-input TP-gauge reduction with zero-block separation

For any MPS tensor \(A\), there exist:

  1. a zero-tail bond dimension \(D_0\),

  2. nontrivial blocks \((B_k)_{k=1}^r\) with nonzero weights \((\mu _k)_{k=1}^r\), each block irreducible and left-canonical (\(\sum _i (B_k^i)^\dagger B_k^i = \mathbb {1}\)), with positive bond dimension,

such that

\[ V^{(N)}(A)_\sigma = V^{(N)}(\mathbf{0}_{d,D_0})_\sigma + V^{(N)}\! \bigl(\textstyle \bigoplus _{k=1}^r \mu _k B_k\bigr)_\sigma . \]

This is the furthest unconditional arbitrary-input reduction step available before periodicity removal.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 11.60Arbitrary tensor to primitive block decomposition

For any MPS tensor \(A\), there exist a period \(p \ge 1\), a zero-tail bond dimension \(D_0\), nonzero weights \((\mu _k)_{k=1}^r\), and left-canonical blocks \((B_k)_{k=1}^r\) with primitive transfer maps and positive bond dimensions, such that

\[ V^{(N)}\! \bigl(A^{[p]}\bigr)_\sigma = V^{(N)}\! \bigl(\mathbf{0}_{d^{[p]},D_0}\bigr)_\sigma + V^{(N)}\! \bigl(\textstyle \bigoplus _k \mu _k B_k\bigr)_\sigma . \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.41Trace-preserving semigroups have trace-annihilating generators

If \(e^{tL}\) is trace-preserving for every \(t \ge 0\), then \(L\) is trace-annihilating.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 5.47Prop. 2.1: Trace of Choi matrix for TP map

If \(T\) is trace-preserving, then \(\operatorname {tr}(\tau ) = 1\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 4.5Nonvanishing trace on injective tensors

If \(D \ge 1\), \(A\) is injective, \(A\) and \(B\) generate the same MPV family, and \(B^i = 0\) for all \(i\), then we have a contradiction.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 4.1Nondegeneracy of the trace pairing

For \(M \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\), \(\operatorname{tr}(M N) = 0\) for all \(N \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) if and only if \(M = 0\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 4.4Injectivity of the trace pairing map

If \(A\) is injective, then \(\ker \Phi _A = \{ 0\} \).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.83Complementary spectral gap implies trace convergence to \(1\)

Let \(E\) be trace-preserving with fixed point \(\rho \) (\(\operatorname{tr}(\rho ) \neq 0\)) and let \(P\) be the fixed-point projection (Definition 5.20). If \(\rho _{\mathrm{spec}}(E - P) {\lt} 1\), then \(\operatorname{Tr}(E^n) \to 1\) as \(n \to \infty \).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 7.47Trace concavity of real powers — restatement

Restates Theorem 7.46 for downstream use. Axiom-dependent: not fully formalized until Theorem 7.46 is proved.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 7.46Trace concavity of real powers

For \(p\in [0,1]\), PSD matrices \(A_1,A_2\), and \(t\in [0,1]\):

\[ t\, \Re \operatorname{tr}(A_1^p)+(1-t)\, \Re \operatorname{tr}(A_2^p) \; \le \; \Re \operatorname{tr}\! \bigl((tA_1+(1-t)A_2)^p\bigr). \]

Follows from operator concavity of \(x\mapsto x^p\) composed with trace monotonicity on the Loewner order.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 7.49Trace convexity of real powers — restatement

Restates Theorem 7.48 for downstream use. Axiom-dependent: not fully formalized until Theorem 7.48 is proved.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 7.48Trace convexity of real powers

For \(p\in [1,2]\), PSD matrices \(A_1,A_2\), and \(t\in [0,1]\):

\[ \Re \operatorname{tr}\! \bigl((tA_1+(1-t)A_2)^p\bigr) \; \le \; t\, \Re \operatorname{tr}(A_1^p)+(1-t)\, \Re \operatorname{tr}(A_2^p). \]

Follows from operator convexity of \(x\mapsto x^p\) composed with trace monotonicity on the Loewner order.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.22Trace constraint implies trace-annihilating

If \((\phi , \kappa )\) satisfies \(\phi ^*(\mathbb {1}) = \kappa + \kappa ^\dagger \), then \(L(\rho ) = \phi (\rho ) - \kappa \rho - \rho \kappa ^\dagger \) is trace-annihilating.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 5.45Prop. 2.1: TP implies partial trace condition

If \(T\) is trace-preserving, then \(\operatorname {tr}_A(\tau ) = \frac{1}{D} \mathbb {1}_D\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 5.15Transfer map is a channel

If \(\sum _i (A^i)^\dagger A^i = \mathbb {1}\), then the transfer map \(\mathcal{E}_A(X) = \sum _i A^i X (A^i)^\dagger \) is a quantum channel (CPTP).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 17.3Transfer map preserved by physical rotation

If \(u u^\dagger = I\), then the transfer map is invariant under physical-index rotation: \(\mathcal{E}_{A_u} = \mathcal{E}_A\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 10.46Iterated transfer does not vanish

If \(A\) is a primitive MPS tensor, then \(\mathcal{E}_A^n \neq 0\) for all \(n \ge 0\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.24Transfer powers converge to zero

Let \(A, B\) be injective normalized tensors that are not gauge-phase equivalent. Then \(F_{AB}^n(X) \to 0\) for every \(X \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.30Transfer power decay (irreducible-TP)

Under the hypotheses of Theorem 9.29, \(F_{AB}^n(X) \to 0\) for every \(X \in M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 11.6Transfer map under scaling

\(\mathcal{E}_{\zeta A}(X) = |\zeta |^2 \mathcal{E}_A(X)\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 19.19Rank-one classification for RFP transfer maps

Let \(A\) be an injective left-canonical RFP tensor with positive-definite fixed point \(\rho \) of the transfer map \(\mathcal{E}_A\). Then \(\mathcal{E}_A = P_\rho \), where \(P_\rho (X) = \frac{\operatorname{tr}(X)}{\operatorname{tr}(\rho )}\, \rho \) is the rank-one fixed-point projection.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 17.47Spectral radius bound

For an injective MPS, assume \(u u^\dagger = \mathbb {1}\), \(\mathcal{E}_A(\mathbb {1})=\mathbb {1}\), \(\Lambda \) is positive definite, \(\operatorname{tr}(\Lambda )=1\), and \(\mathcal{E}_A^\dagger (\Lambda )=\Lambda \). Then every eigenvalue \(\lambda \) of \(\mathcal{E}_u\) satisfies \(|\lambda | \le 1\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 11.14Two-block decomposition

If the transfer map has a PSD fixed point \(\rho \) that is not positive definite (i.e. \(\rho \) has a nontrivial kernel), then there exist MPS tensors \(A_1, A_2\) of smaller bond dimensions such that the two-block tensor \(A_1 \oplus A_2\) generates the same MPV family as \(A\): \(\mathcal{V}(A) = \mathcal{V}(A_1 \oplus A_2)\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 18.35Unique ground state for block-injective tensors

If \(A\) is \(L_0\)-block-injective with \(L_0 {\gt} 0\) and \(D \ge 1\), the parent Hamiltonian with interaction range \(2L_0\) has a unique ground state on the periodic chain.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 18.36Optimal unique ground state for normal tensors

If \(A\) is normal (hence \(L_0\)-block-injective for some \(L_0 {\gt} 0\)), \(D \ge 1\), and in normal form, the interaction range can be reduced to \(L_0 + 1\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 11.71Gauge phases have unit modulus for primitive irreducible blocks

For trace-preserving primitive irreducible blocks, every gauge-phase equivalence has a phase factor of modulus \(1\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 12.13Vandermonde separation

If \(\mu _1,\ldots ,\mu _r\) are pairwise distinct complex numbers and \(\sum _{k=1}^r c_k \mu _k^N = 0\) for \(N = 0,1,\ldots ,r-1\), then \(c_k = 0\) for all \(k\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 10.20Vector span stabilisation

If \(K_n(A, \varphi ) = K_{n+1}(A, \varphi )\), then \(K_m(A, \varphi ) = K_n(A, \varphi )\) for all \(m \ge n\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 3.27Horizontal canonical form implies vertical canonical form

Every MPDO in horizontal canonical form is also in vertical canonical form.

LaTeX
Theorem 17.21Virtual representation theorem for injective MPS

Let \(A\) be an injective MPS tensor that is on-site symmetric under a group representation \(U : G \to \mathrm{GL}_d(\mathbb {C})\). Then there exist:

  1. a scalar 2-cochain \(\omega : G \times G \to \mathbb {C}^{\times }\), and

  2. a map \(\rho : G \to \mathrm{GL}_D(\mathbb {C})\) satisfying

    \[ \rho (g)\, \rho (h) = \omega (g,h)\, \rho (gh) \qquad \text{for all } g, h \in G, \]

such that, defining the gauge matrices \(X(g) := \rho (g^{-1})\), one has

\[ \widetilde{A}_g^i = X(g)\, A^i\, X(g)^{-1} \qquad \text{for every } g \in G, i \in \{ 0,\ldots ,d{-}1\} . \]

In tensor-network notation, for each fixed \(g\) this is the local gauge relation

\[ \begin{array}{ccccc}& & i & & \\ & & \vert & & \\ \textcolor{red}{\bullet } & \text{---} & \bullet & \text{---} & \textcolor{red}{\bullet } \\ X(g) & & & & X(g)^{-1} \end{array} \]

with the black node denoting the tensor named in the surrounding formula and the two red side nodes acting on the virtual legs. In particular, \(\rho \) is a projective representation of \(G\) on the bond space \(\mathbb {C}^D\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 17.16Virtual symmetry equation

If \(A\) is injective and on-site symmetric under \(U\), then for every \(g \in G\) there exist an invertible matrix \(X(g) \in \mathrm{GL}_D(\mathbb {C})\) and a nonzero scalar \(\varphi (g) \in \mathbb {C}^{\times }\) (in fact \(\varphi = 1\) in the single-block case) such that \(\widetilde{A}_g^{\, i} = \varphi (g)\, X(g)\, A^i\, X(g)^{-1}\) for all \(i\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 17.50Virtual unitary from string order

For an injective MPS, assume \(u u^\dagger = \mathbb {1}\), \(\mathcal{E}_A(\mathbb {1})=\mathbb {1}\), \(\Lambda \) is positive definite, \(\operatorname{tr}(\Lambda )=1\), and \(\mathcal{E}_A^\dagger (\Lambda )=\Lambda \). If string order exists for \(u\), then there exists a virtual unitary \(V\) and a unit-modulus scalar \(\mu \) such that \(\sum _j u_{ij} A^j = \mu \, V A^i V^\dagger \) for all \(i\). Diagrammatically this is the same local intertwining relation, but only up to an overall unit scalar on the virtual side:

\[ \begin{array}{ccccccc}& & i & & & & i \\ & & \vert & & & & \vert \\ u & & \textcolor{red}{\bullet } & & & & \bullet \\ & & \vert & & = & & \\ \bullet & \text{---} & \bullet & & \textcolor{red}{\bullet } & \text{---} & \textcolor{red}{\bullet } \\ & & & & V & & V^\dagger \end{array} \]
LaTeX Lean
Theorem 14.1Weak Fundamental Theorem (conditional)

Let \((\mu _j^A, A_j)_{j=1}^{g_A}\) and \((\mu _k^B, B_k)_{k=1}^{g_B}\) be two families in normal canonical form, each satisfying the non-degeneracy condition that no two distinct blocks in the same family are gauge-phase equivalent. Let \(A_\mathrm {tot}\) and \(B_\mathrm {tot}\) be tensors with MPV decompositions

\[ V^{(N)}(A_\mathrm {tot})_\sigma = \sum _j a_{N,j}\, V^{(N)}(A_j)_\sigma , \quad V^{(N)}(B_\mathrm {tot})_\sigma = \sum _k b_{N,k}\, V^{(N)}(B_k)_\sigma , \]

with \(a_{N,j} \to a_j \neq 0\) and \(b_{N,k} \to b_k \neq 0\). Suppose moreover that \(V^{(N)}(A_\mathrm {tot})_\sigma = c_N\, V^{(N)}(B_\mathrm {tot})_\sigma \) with \(c_N \to c \neq 0\). Then \(g_A = g_B\) and there is a permutation \(\pi \) such that \(D_j^A = D_{\pi (j)}^B\) and \(A_j\) is gauge-phase equivalent to \(B_{\pi (j)}\) for every \(j\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 10.24Wielandt analysis exists

Every normal MPS tensor admits a Wielandt analysis.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 10.39Blocked fixed-length matrix spanning

Suppose \(A\) is normal and \(L {\gt} 0\), and we have:

  1. a word \(w_0\) of length \(L\) with eigenvector \(A^{w_0} \varphi = \mu \varphi \) (\(\mu \neq 0\), \(\varphi \neq 0\)),

  2. a word \(\tau _0\) of length \(L\) with transpose eigenvector \((A^{\tau _0})^T \psi = \nu \psi \) (\(\nu \neq 0\), \(\psi \neq 0\)),

  3. a rank-one element \(\varphi \psi ^T \in S_m(A^{[L]})\) for some \(m\).

Then there exists \(N\) such that \(S_N(A) = M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\).

Remark. The proof gives the explicit bound \(N = ((D{-}1) + m + (D{-}1)) \cdot L\); the statement above records only the existential conclusion.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 10.26Wielandt bound

If \(A\) is a normal MPS tensor with bond dimension \(D\), then \(T_{D^2}(A) = M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\). In particular, the cumulative span stabilises at index at most \(D^2\). This is the cumulative-span part of ( . The paper’s theorem is stronger: via Lemma 2(b) it upgrades this to a fixed-length conclusion \(S_N(A) = M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\), proved below in Theorem 10.41.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 10.25Wielandt chain

Let \(A\) be a normal MPS tensor with bond dimension \(D \ge 1\). Then the following hold simultaneously:

  1. \(T_{D^2}(A) = M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\) (cumulative span stabilises);

  2. there exists a word \(w_0\) with \(|w_0| \le D^2\) and \(\operatorname{tr}(A^{w_0}) \ne 0\);

  3. there exist \(w_0, \mu \ne 0, \varphi \ne 0\) with \(|w_0| \le D^2\) and \(A^{w_0} \varphi = \mu \, \varphi \);

  4. for every \(\varphi \ne 0\), \(K_{D^2}(A, \varphi ) = \mathbb {C}^D\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 10.28Wielandt’s inequality — general bound

Let \(A\) be a normalized MPS tensor (\(\sum _i (A^i)^\dagger A^i = \mathbb {1}\)) that is primitive in the paper sense. Then

\[ \iota (A) \; \le \; (D^2 - \operatorname{kr}(A) + 1) \cdot D^2, \]

where \(\iota (A) = \min \{ n : S_n(A) = M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\} \) is the full-Kraus-rank index. This is ( .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 10.41Fixed-length word-span saturation via blocking

If \(A\) is a normal MPS tensor with bond dimension \(D\), then there exists \(N\) such that \(S_N(A) = M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\). This gives the fixed-length spanning conclusion corresponding to ( .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.60Wolf Thm. 6.15 (scalar fixed-point algebra case)

In the scalar fixed-point algebra setting, the weighted map \(X \mapsto \frac{\operatorname{tr}(\rho X)}{\operatorname{tr}(\rho )}\mathbb {1}\) is a conditional expectation onto the adjoint fixed-point \(*\)-subalgebra.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.56Wolf Thm. 6.2(3): exponential positivity condition

Let \(E\) be an irreducible completely positive map, \(A \ge 0\) with \(A \neq 0\), and \(t {\gt} 0\). Then \(\exp (tE)(A)\) is positive definite. Conversely, if \(\exp (tE)(A)\) is positive definite for every such \(t\), \(A\), then \(E\) is irreducible (full equivalence ).

LaTeX
Lean
  • exp_posDef_of_irreducible_cp
  • irreducible_iff_exp_posDef_forall
Theorem 9.58Wolf Thm. 6.8: combined equivalence

Under normalization, primitivity in Wolf’s sense is equivalent to the conjunction of eventual full Kraus rank, normality, and strong irreducibility.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 9.57Wolf Thm. 6.8: Kraus-span equivalence

Under normalization, primitivity in Wolf’s sense is equivalent to eventual full Kraus rank.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 7.45Wolf Example 5.3 is not completely positive

The map from Example 7.42 is not completely positive.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 7.43Wolf Example 5.3 is positive

The map from Example 7.42 is positive.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 7.44Wolf Example 5.3 satisfies the Schwarz inequality

For every \(A \in M_2(\mathbb {C})\),

\[ T(A^\dagger A) - T(A^\dagger ) T(A) \ge 0, \]

where \(T\) is the map from Example 7.42.

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.81Prop. 7.6: Full equivalence

For a GKSL generator \(L : M_{D}(\mathbb {C}) \to M_{D}(\mathbb {C})\), the four conditions

  1. rank-deficient fixed density,

  2. rank-deficient kernel element,

  3. invariant compression, and

  4. block-upper-triangular Lindblad form

are equivalent. This is ( .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.77Prop. 7.6, (1)\(\leftrightarrow \)(2)

A rank-deficient density matrix is a fixed point of \(e^{tL}\) for all \(t \ge 0\) if and only if it lies in \(\ker L\). This is ( .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.78Prop. 7.6, (1)\(\to \)(3)

If \(L\) is a GKSL generator and there exists a rank-deficient fixed density matrix, then the semigroup preserves a nontrivial compression. This is ( .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 15.79Prop. 7.6, (3)\(\leftrightarrow \)(4)

For a GKSL generator \(L\), the semigroup preserves a nontrivial compression if and only if the Lindblad data can be chosen block-upper-triangular. This is ( .

LaTeX
Lean
  • wolf_prop_7_6_three_implies_four
  • wolf_prop_7_6_four_implies_three
Theorem 10.56Word span monotonicity from aperiodicity

If \(\mathbb {1}\in S_1(A)\), then \(S_n(A) \le S_{n+1}(A)\) for all \(n\).

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 19.26Zero correlation length iff idempotent transfer

An MPS tensor has zero correlation length (i.e. is both locally orthogonal and CID) if and only if its transfer map is idempotent. This is ( .

LaTeX Lean
Theorem 11.40Zero-block separation

Every MPS tensor \(A\) admits a block decomposition into:

  1. a trivial block of bond dimension \(D_0\) with all Kraus operators zero (accumulating all all-zero irreducible blocks), and

  2. a finite family of nontrivial blocks \((B_k)_{k=1}^r\), each irreducible, each with at least one nonzero Kraus operator, and each with positive bond dimension,

such that

\[ V^{(N)}(A)_\sigma = V^{(N)}(\mathbf{0}_{d,D_0})_\sigma + V^{(N)}\! \bigl(\textstyle \bigoplus _{k=1}^r B_k\bigr)_\sigma \]

for every spin configuration \(\sigma \) of length \(N\). At \(N = 0\) this reads \(D = D_0 + \sum _{k=1}^r D_k\). For \(N \ge 1\) the zero-tail term vanishes.

LaTeX Lean
